subject: Articulation Program Proposals - Teach-Out
description: We currently have two proposal types for articulations - new and modify. However, we occasionally need to have one of these programs in a teach-out status. We've been using the Active Status field on the Codes and Dates page, but this doesn't show in our reports or the search in Meta. Can we either add a new proposal type for articulation teach-outs or have that active status field show in both of the AR admin reports?

comments.body: We currently have two proposal types for articulations - new and modify. However, we occasionally need to have one of these programs in a teach-out status. We've been using the Active Status field on the Codes and Dates page, but this doesn't show in our reports or the search in Meta. Can we either add a new proposal type for articulation teach-outs or have that active status field show in both of the AR admin reports?

Ariel,

I'm not for sure what you mean by "**teach-out status**".

Not sure how creating a new program proposal type would help.

Looking at programs using the proposal types "**New Articulation Program**" and "**Articulation Modification**", I see on page "**Codes and Dates**", field "**Active Status**". Where this field is on the program form, you can pull it in your program search, by using the "**Output Fields**" option, under that option find the page the field exists on, then check it, click **Search**, and you will see that field on the program search page. See image examples below.

 ![](https://acadea.zendesk.com/attachments/token/Vee7kfisPfevYhIpFDW1JWqsc/?name=image.png)

 ![](https://acadea.zendesk.com/attachments/token/QhlyVcZsE4WhO4uJFvUTgf8BH/?name=image.png)

Where this field is on the program form, we can also add it to reports if needed. Just to confirm, you want this field "**Active Status**" from page "**Codes and Dates**" added to the admin reports "**Articulation AR articulated programs**" and "**Articulation AR courses**", correct? See image example below. Which column in both reports do you want this field to display in?

 ![](https://acadea.zendesk.com/attachments/token/KTWFHtUfPmi6Egs6kzQooURCo/?name=image.png)

Michael Gregston
Support Supervisor

O: 208-522-1225
E: mike.gregston@acadea.com | W: www.acadea.com
A: 1600 John Adams Pkwy, Suite 200, Idaho Falls, Idaho 83401

Was talking with **Shirley Boyette** on the phone about this ticket, and I went to the client production site and created the new proposal type "**Articulation Teach-Out**". Shirley, will check with the client to see if this completes this ticket or if something else was needed.

subject: Additional Admin Report - Articulation AR Programs
description:
In the Articulation AR Programs report, we would like the following changes:
1 - Change the title of Column D to Articulation Agreement Status (this is not urgent)
2 - In Column J, have the report pull only the numerical CIP Code value (it is currently populating the text part), and then add 1 column to the right of J that pulls the text value for the CIP field (i.e. state-assigned degree name)
3 - Add a column for Total Credits awarded. This would pull from the Course Blocks page. There are a few articulations that have a variable credit amount. If this can be displayed, that's ideal, but if not, we'd like the maximum amount to display.

comments.body:
In the Articulation AR Programs report, we would like the following changes:
1 - Change the title of Column D to Articulation Agreement Status (this is not urgent)
2 - In Column J, have the report pull only the numerical CIP Code value (it is currently populating the text part), and then add 1 column to the right of J that pulls the text value for the CIP field (i.e. state-assigned degree name)
3 - Add a column for Total Credits awarded. This would pull from the Course Blocks page. There are a few articulations that have a variable credit amount. If this can be displayed, that's ideal, but if not, we'd like the maximum amount to display.

Ariel,

In looking at the admin report "**Articulation AR articulate programs**", the first record program "**Microsoft Office Specialist – Microsoft Office Access (to CIT-AS)**" (940), says it is mapped to an articulation programs of "**Computer Information Technology (CIT-AS)**" (1498), and then in that program it has the "**Florida CIP Code**" of "**1511010307 - Computer Information Technology**", but in the report it says "**IT Security**" in column "**J**". That **Florida CIP Code** title of "**IT Security**" has the code "**1511100307**" which does not match what is in that other program ID (1498), nor does the title match, and I'm not seeing that field "**Florida CIP Code**" for program ID (940) in any of its pages. This seems incorrect to me, or am I misunderstanding how this column "**J**" works/displays? See image example below.

 ![](https://acadea.zendesk.com/attachments/token/1jVDhHzaMIeGyg9E2RWoIrItz/?name=image.png)

Michael Gregston
Support Supervisor

O: 208-522-1225
E: mike.gregston@acadea.com | W: www.acadea.com
A: 1600 John Adams Pkwy, Suite 200, Idaho Falls, Idaho 83401

Hi, 

No, you're definitely not totally off the mark. We have a handful of programs where the state program name/CIP code seem don't align with our program names in the way one might expect. I think some of them are a result of CIP Codes being added after we already developed a program with the same name or things like that. Cybersecurity/IT Security is probably the one that stands out the most, but you may come across others. The mismatch is actually 'correct' as far as this report's needs are concerned though. Thanks!

Hi Andrijana,

Thank you for the video, that helps me see exactly where you're looking at.

The "**Import From Block Library**" list in a program does not pull from the field "**Block Type**", page "**Main**", in a course block library proposal.

It pulls from the field "**Block Type**", page "**Course Blocks**", in a course block library proposal record.

See image example below.

 ![](https://acadea.zendesk.com/attachments/token/8YMiIPWjb1Z1C9r2IASOUKidE/?name=image.png)

The "**Import From Block Library**" list in a program was never designed to pull from that one field on the "**Main**" page. It was designed to pull from the one field in the "**Course Blocks**" page in that section ordered list, so that if you have many course block records in a one library proposal, they should all have a different block type selected per record, so that you can see the different course block library naming in that "**Import From Block Library**" list.

Where you're selecting the same "**Block Type**" name for all your different course block library records in the "**Course Blocks**" page, that is why you have the same naming in the program "**Import From Block Library**" list.

That "**Block Type**" field on page "**Main**" has nothing to do with the "**Import From Block Library**" list in a program form as far as I know of. Looking at the history of this field, looks like it was add for your course block library proposal entity title display only, so that when you make this selection, it auto updates the proposal entity title that you see at the top of the page when in the course block library proposal.

 ![](https://acadea.zendesk.com/attachments/token/2luedREqwInpNudet6wkSNg2R/?name=image.png)


Michael Gregston
Support Supervisor

- **Course Block Title**

Check where program "**Import Library Block**" options titles come from. See if we can update those titles to be more meaningful per option, so client isn't seeing duplicates, and so they can tell which option is which. If the import option titles come from the custom configuration title for the course block record in the course library proposal, can we add a custom query display to show the "**course block library title - block type**", examples:

- **AA General Education Requirement-Fa26 - Admission Requirements**
- **AA General Education Requirement-Fa26 - State Mandated Prerequisites**
- **AA General Education Requirement-Fa26 - Lower Division Support courses**
- **AA General Education Requirement-Fa26 - AA General Education Requirements**
just above the field "**Block Type**" at the block level, so it displays in the block record when expand/close expand of that record, if that title will then display in the program form for "**Import Library Block**" options titles as the option to make it more clear what each option is. Note, due to how long the title will be, may have to do new development to add a scroll bar from left to right, so the client can see the full titles.

 ![](https://acadea.zendesk.com/attachments/token/0Y2PJMdtXUhNA6MglDmhhOK6Z/?name=image.png)

Need to check with **Clinton Worle** on the above 2nd item of that import option title displays.

Once we have come up with possible solutions, need to discuss those possibilities with the client to see what would be best.
Talked with Clint, need to write up options and send to client.

Andrijana,

I confirmed with our support team that the program "**Import Library Block**" options titles come from, the custom title configuration display for the course block records as seen in my previous image.

We can do one of the two options:

1. Update the course block title to be what is in field "**Course Block Title**".
2. Or we can add a custom query display to include the proposal title and the selected option "**Block Type**" for that course block library records and just "**Block Type**" for program records.

Note, if you go with option 2:

- We will need to make the same configuration for both course block library forms and program forms where they both have to be set up the same way.
- Your titles could be very long, you won't be able to see the full differences if you have multiple blocks in the same course block library proposal and the titles are long. Would require new development to adjust the width of that display, which is release dependent. Statement of work may be required for this new development.
- A possible workaround instead of new development we might be able to override the display with "**Course Block Title**" field if data exists in that field per that field on each block record, so their would be the custom default display when that field is empty, but when you fill that field in, it would then override that title display.
If you go with option 1:

- We will need to make the same configuration for both course block library forms and program forms where they both have to be set up the same way.
- You control the length of the title at all times and can fix is as needed when needed.

Which way are you wanting to go?


Michael Gregston
Support Supervisor

Andrijana

A: 1600 John Adams Pkwy, Suite 200, Idaho Falls, Idaho 83401

No, I created the academic org on Monday myself. I've done it before with no issues but to be on the safe side, Shirley and I both checked behind to ensure it was set-up correctly and in our call with Natalie she had no explanation to what could be causing it, other than there have been some delays with items previously. On our end, I believe this is the longest delay we've seen for such a minor addition, especially since it was showing on the programs side for us both, but not in the courses side.
Sorry, misread. Yes, I opened the new course proposal.

Andrijana,

I did a little digging and found out that your course field "**Associated Academic Organization**" is configured to cache the lookup options for this field on our servers. When you added the new "**Academic Org**" option, it was not displaying in this course field due to the field was still using the cached lookup values that help your course proposal load faster.

The reason you didn't see the options for a few days is because the cached lookup hasn't refreshed for a few days. The server cache just happened to get refreshed either last night or today, and that is why the new option is displaying now.

We have created a development support ticket to look into this issue that all our clients can have with new or updated options in the organization tool and lookup pages.

There are a couple different workaround ways until a main fix is put into place.

1. When a new option is added to organization tool, create a support ticket for the specific course you want to pull the new option in, and I can manually go update that course form template version so that the option shows immediately. It will only show for this one template, all other templates will show once the cache is refreshed on them, which can happened randomly depending what all is being done during that time.
2. You can call our support line and I can go do this manually with you on the phone.
3. We could update this course field configuration to be like your program form, but doing this can increase course load time.

If you would like to discuss any of the above, call our support line and i can try to answer any question you might have.


Michael Gregston
Support Supervisor

O: 208-522-1225
E: mike.gregston@acadea.com | W: www.acadea.com
A: 1600 John Adams Pkwy, Suite 200, Idaho Falls, Idaho 83401

Hi Mike…



I am sure Andrijana will reply to the ticket, but I wanted to say THANK YOU and it isappreciated that you took the time and the inclination to “dig” around.  You never know sometimes whether it is client-specific or system-wide specific.  And including an explanation is very helpful too! Understanding the how’s and why’s a process is the way it is helps us to determine our internal process/procedures – and to realize something is not broken. Just because something isn’t quite right doesn’t necessarily mean that it is wrong.



You rock!



**_~S_**





**Shirley Boyette**

_Curriculum/Research Analyst_

Curriculum Services

subject: SPC - Programs - Move programs to current active form template
description:
This is a follow-up to your previous request #43581 "PROGRAMS –  add PLO date field accred body  CS277"

THIS IS NOT COMPLETE.  It was supposed to be applied to the ACTIVE form/proposals; however, it is only on the new/draft proposal.

Need this applied to the ACTIVE PROPOSALS AS SOON AS POSSIBLE.

Both my ticket description and my attachment ticket was clear that this was to the ACTIVE form.

comments.body:
This is a follow-up to your previous request #43581 "PROGRAMS –  add PLO date field accred body  CS277"

THIS IS NOT COMPLETE.  It was supposed to be applied to the ACTIVE form/proposals; however, it is only on the new/draft proposal.

Need this applied to the ACTIVE PROPOSALS AS SOON AS POSSIBLE.

Both my ticket description and my attachment ticket was clear that this was to the ACTIVE form.

Shirley,

The changes made in ticket #43581 were applied to the current active program form versions.

Note, the program "**Event Planning Management (EVENT-CT)**" (800), is not using the most current active program form version. It is using an older form version. If you were to modify this program, it would then use the current active form version.

Any program that is using the current active form version will have this update, it doesn't just apply to new proposals being made.

Yes, when you create a new draft program it will have the changes where they are using the current active form version.

If we are to move all existing programs that are not historical that are not on the current active form version to the current active form versions, there are pros and cons to this.

One of the cons of moving a proposal from an old form template to a new form template, is the changes between the form versions, it could remove fields, so you could lose data, or it could add fields that haven't been filled out for that proposal where the new fields didn't exist for that program when it was made.

This is why we can't just move active programs to the current active forms in the previous ticket, due to the data issues it could have. Also, we can't just move active programs, to the current active form templates, we would have to move all programs that are not historical to the current active form versions, or else you will have approved, in review, or draft programs that are on older form versions that would not have the updates.

Knowing the above, are you still wanting to use this new request to move all existing programs that are not historical that are on an older program form version to the current active form versions, even knowing the above consequences?


Michael Gregston
Support Supervisor

O: 208-522-1225
E: mike.gregston@acadea.com | W: www.acadea.com
A: 1600 John Adams Pkwy, Suite 200, Idaho Falls, Idaho 83401

Hi Mike...



Thank you for the explanation. However, 1) it would have been good to have received this information/clarification before the work was done, and 2) over the last 2 years we have submitted tickets to add fields in both programs and courses, and this is the first time hearing your response.  We haven't had any problems before.



Now maybe I didn't word the ticket correctly, so again, someone asking a clarification question would have been good.  And, let me know how I need to word tickets going forward.



If we were removing a field, then I would definitely have questioned it with Natalie or Support before submitting the ticket.  But adding a new field to all programs and tomove all existing programs that are not historical that are not on the current active form version to the current active form versions did not seem like an issue to me.



Please move forward to add these 2 new fields to the Program Outcomes tab as stated in the original ticket.  And I would also expect that if there are any other unforeseen issues that someone will respond back in the ticket (or call or email or let Natalie know) to let me know.



I would appreciate an ETA.



Thanks!



**_~S_**





**Shirley Boyette**

_Curriculum/Research Analyst_

Curriculum Services

Ticket moved to Code review

Here are the changes that have been made regarding this ticket.


- Updated the program client entity subtype "**Industry**" to now be "**Industry Certs**" and update the plural "**Industries**" to "**Industry Certs**" for this same client entity subtype.
- Updated the program proposal type title "**Industry Certificate**" to now be "**Industry Certification**".
- Created a new page called “**Related Programs**” between the two existing pages “**Co-Contributor(s)**” and “**Programs**”:
  - On the page “**Basic Program Information**”, moved the drop-down field “**Does this Certification have an Articulation Agreement(s)?**”, OL checklist “**Which program(s) does this program articulate to?**”, and static text “**If any documentation is associated with this Agreement, please upload on the Attachments page in the Checklist.**” to this new page. Keep the show/hide configuration between the two fields.
  - Updated the static text “**If any documentation is associated with this Agreement, please upload on the Attachments page in the Checklist.**” to bold/red font.
- Created a new page called “**Typical Process to get Certified**” between the two existing pages “**Eligibility**” and “**Expectations and Considerations**”:
  - Created a new RTE text-area field with the field display name “
    -

1. **Successfully complete the required course(s) and meet any eligibility requirements.**

      1. **Receive Opt-In form invite from your instructor.**
      2. **File the Opt-In form.**
      3. **Receive Test Prep Toolkit email or Next Step email from the Get Certified Team.**
      4. **Complete action items from email(s) received, including comprehensive exam prep.**
      5. **Aim to earn 90% or above on comprehensive practice exams and applicable labs.**
      6. **Now you are ready to file the Claim Your Voucher form.**
      7. **Receive Voucher Included email or Next Step email from the Get Certified Team.**
      8. **Schedule exam appointment to get certified within ~30-days of receiving voucher code.**

**10.  After the exam, send Score Report to ****GetCertified@spcollege.edu.**** (Required for grant reporting.)**“, this will allow the user to copy/paste the text into the RTE text-area if needed.

- Updated the page “**Programs**” to now be “**Courses**”.
- Updated the page “**Eligibility**” to now be “**Final Eligibility Course**”.
- Program page “**Basic Program Information**”:
  - Field “**Award Type**”:
    - Created the new award type option “**Third Party Industry Certification**”.
  - Field “**Effective Term**”, removed this field.
    - Also removed this same field but named “**Actual Program Effective Term**” from the “**Codes and Dates**” page.
  - Created a new field called “**Effective Date**”, date picker, and make it **required**. Also, add this field to the “**Codes and Dates**” page.
  - Field “**Next Program Review (Month/Year)**”, removed this field.
    - Also removed this same field but named “**Next Program Review**” from the “**Codes and Dates**” page.
  - Created a new field called “**Next Program Review**”, date picker, and make it **required**. Also, add this field to the “**Codes and Dates**” page.
- Program page “**Courses**” that used to be named “**Programs**”:
  - Drop-down field “**Should this course be indicated on degree Pathway (noted above)?**”, when “**Yes**”, display the static text “**(If yes, inform Curriculum Services with course prefix/# and relevant SPC Certificate or Degree.)**” in red font.
- Program page “**Overview**”:
  - Field “**Program Certification Summary**”, updated field display name to “**Industry Certification Overview**”.
  -

Field “**Learn more about this certification:**

**What is on the certification exam**“, updated this field display name to “**Certification Exam**”, then added the field help text “**Learn more about this certification:**
**What is on the certification exam:**“ that the user can copy/paste into the RTE field.

  - Field “**Cost of the certification exam voucher (free to eligible students**”, updated field display name to “**Cost of the certification exam voucher (free to eligible students)**”.
    - Also, updated this field backing store to allow for decimal input.
  - Field “**Accommodations during a Certification Exam**”:
    - Removed the text “**Learn more about apply for this certification exam accommodations**” below this RTE.
    - We can’t input text into the user input field, so instead updated the help text “**Each industry certification exam has specific processes and requirements for approved accommodations. (Existing accommodations approved through SPC are not automatically applied.)**“, to include a hard return and then display the text “**Learn more about how to apply for certification exam accommodations:**“, so that the user can copy/paste the text into the RTE.
- Program page “**Final Eligibility Course**” that used to be named “**Eligibility**”:
  - Created a new custom query text display field called “**Course Prefix/#; Course Name**” at the top of the page, should display the course information from the “**Course**” drop-down field on page “**Courses**”, used to be named “**Programs**”, of the course saved options with the highest “**Course Sequence #**”.
  - Removed the section “**Academic course progression to final eligibility course (if applicable)**”.
  - Removed the OL section “**Requirements**”.
  - Added a new RTE text-area field “**Requirements**”, just below the field “**Includes In-class eligibility requirements**”.
- Program page “**Expectations and Considerations**”:
  - Moved the following bulleted list up into the field “**Expectations and Considerations**” help text that would display just below the field display name:
    - **Students who become eligible to get certified and plan to accept the opportunity are expected to file their Opt-In form immediately, to receive exam prep access and support.**
    - **To ensure students finish what they start and maximize their free certification opportunities at SPC, they should aim to get certified within 6 months after they file their Opt-In form.**
    - **All eligible students must finish getting certified no later than 6 months after graduating from their College Credit Certificate or Associate in Science program or entering a Bachelor’s degree program.**
    - **It is not recommended that students wait until graduation to pursue their certification opportunities!**
    - **Most successfully certified students report spending 30+ hours out of class, per certification, preparing to get certified.**
    - **A clear understanding of the exam’s objective domains and competencies, knowledge of terminology, ability to complete hands-on exercises like labs, and earning high scores on comprehensive practice exams in training and testing modes, are necessary to ensure students are well prepared to achieve certification.**
    - **Scores on comprehensive practice exams of 90% (or 900) and above are good indicators that students are ready to claim their voucher, and to schedule their exam.**
    - **Test prep tools and vouchers are not covered by tuition or student fees. Primary funding is from the federal “Strengthening Career & Technical Education for the 21st Century Act (Perkins V)” for Career and Technical Education (CTE) students, an entitlement grant that St. Petersburg College administers annually.**
    - **Once a student has claimed and received their voucher code, they are expected to schedule an exam appointment within approximately 30 days.**
    - **The Get Certified team administers the distribution of vouchers to ensure all purchased vouchers are used before their expiration date. If a student neglects to register (apply) their voucher by scheduling an exam appointment within the 30-day window, that voucher may be reassigned to serve another student, and a new Claim Your Voucher form must be filed to receive a replacement voucher.**
    - **Retake vouchers are occasionally available to students who have been unsuccessful on their first attempt. To claim a retake voucher, students may be asked to submit proof of a recent, comprehensive practice exam with a score of 90% or above.**
- Noticed this program template doesn’t have any reports, mapped to “**All Fields**” report.

The work completed on this ticket will be deployed shortly to your production environment. Once the deployment is complete, the changes will take effect immediately.

Thank you,

Acadea Support

subject: SPC - REPORTS - Program of Study & Comparison - Program - Follow up - #43221
description:
I would like to request that all our tickets’ status be set to “Waiting on your reply” before deploying to production.  We should have a chance to test/review the work done before deployment.  Otherwise, we will have to ask to have everything posted to the sandbox first.

This ticket is (again) a perfect example.

The text added is correct and in the right place. However, and this piece is on us, where the effective term is noted, the text (Fall 2022 (610)) should be the same effective term of that specific program.  The verbiage is static; the effective term is dynamic (and is the same term as that specific program).
  ![](https://acadea.zendesk.com/attachments/token/JMiZ0w8WddPvpScfqX5LcF8SE/?name=inlineImage.jpeg) ![](https://acadea.zendesk.com/attachments/token/4zr53gMEWMzEQAlXMstqfEdyc/?name=inlineImage.jpeg)

We should have been able to test/review this ticket before put to productuon.  We would have caught this before deployment.  Now we need this fixed ASAP/URGENT as these reports  are student/all users-facing.  Students start classes on Monday so it is imperative it is fixed …like, yesterday.

I’m OK with it being fixed on production as long as there is someone (you or someone) on your end to double check (QA) this.  (If it can’t be fixed today, then at least put it back the way it was and then have someone fix it on Monday.)

Please let me know how soon this can be completed, and how this process will work going forward.

Also let me know that you have received this and if any questions.

description:

The "Import from Block Library" window is missing at least 2 important and relatively new blocks. (see image)
AA General Education Requirements-Fa25

There were created in the April time frame and were showing in the Import Block window.

The active transfer plan program type proposals (TRs) created using the AA block are still showing correctly. 

Am I missing something? Need this ASAP please.

~S

comments.body:

Shirley,

See image below.

 ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/L1FSEFiHPhrKwjfay98OuyT1W/?name=image.png)

The "**Import From Block Library**" options did originally come from the field "**Course Block Title**", but recently on 04/23/2025 your college had it changed to the display to the field "**Block Type**".

This is why it looks like there are so many duplicates, is because this field "**Block Type**" is a drop-down with the exact same options for all your active course block library proposals.

The field "**Course Block Title**" allows you to control the unique name, but the field "**Block Type**" does not allow you to change it.

We can change this back to the field "**Course Block Title**" which will then give you your unique names back. See image below.

 ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/BCTKgnlWFi2dCmMcIHRxMxoDy/?name=image.png)

Do you want this?

Note, if we change it back this does affect the display for the "**Import From Block Library**" options, but it also affects the title display in the program "**Course Blocks**" page, and it also affects the course blocks display anywhere it exists, like program reports, catalog.

 ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/53qhZNTKCEkztdAwaDjWKCVMd/?name=image.png)

Also, if we make this change, you may have to go update that title display in programs and/or course block libraries where exists if needed.

We would also probably have to look into updating the display anywhere the course blocks displays exists, due to some of your proposals you're using both the fields above and below with the exact same display, so that text will look like it is duplicating at times do you users inputting the exact same thing for the two different fields. See image below.

 ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/9OLUpp984cMDMcaSqkZf1yVfW/?name=image.png)

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225
Relates to #42254.

subject: SYSTEM – Program Approval Workflows
description:
Hi, I’m trying to update the Program New Workflow and created a draft version. I added a new level and I'm having issues with the target status saving. My goal is to have another level for our internal edits that does not get unlocked for originators and doesn't send system notifications. 

Please see attached ticket for details and screenshots. Could this be completed by 5/26/25? It would need to be activated before any program proposals are launched for our current cycle. 

Please let me know if you have any questions.
Kind Regards, 
Andrijana Ekberg

comments.body:
Hi, I’m trying to update the Program New Workflow and created a draft version. I added a new level and I'm having issues with the target status saving. My goal is to have another level for our internal edits that does not get unlocked for originators and doesn't send system notifications. 

Please see attached ticket for details and screenshots. Could this be completed by 5/26/25? It would need to be activated before any program proposals are launched for our current cycle. 

Please let me know if you have any questions.
Kind Regards, 
Andrijana Ekberg

Andrijana,

Looking at the workflow, "**Program New Workflow**", I see your draft version "**Draft 4.22.2025 for Spring 2026 cycle**" and looking at the workflow "**Program Modification Workflow**", I see your draft version "**Draft 5.5.2025 for Spring 2026 cycle**".

The workflow action “**Target Status**” drop-down is a known bug, and we have a DST ticket in to look into fixing that.

That is why when you change it and then leave and go back to it, it goes back to what it was before.

Workaround for the workflow action “**Target Status**” doesn’t save when an option is selected. It will only show the option selected if a currIQunet Support team member manually inserted it using back end configuration.

It sounds like you want the action "**Target Status**" to be "**CS Approved**" for the action "**Disapprove**" at the step/level "**Level 04**" when user/position "**Curriculum Coordinator**" takes this action. Is this correct?

We can look into configuring this for those two specific workflow versions above.

Should we be doing this configuration for any other workflow versions? If so, we would need to know the exact version, exact level, exact position, exact action taken, and exact status to change too when that specific action is taken.

As for the request for the custom "**Level Name**", we currently do not have that functionality to display it for the **View Proposal History** page. That field doesn't display anywhere except for here in the edit approval process workflow page.

As for being able to get this done by 05/26/2025, I can set this ticket priority to high, but I can't gurantee it will be done by then where we have a backlog of support tickets, but we will try to get to it as soon as possible.


Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Hi Mike, 

Thank you for checking in on this. Correct, if you can please update both on your end to the following, I would greatly appreciate that. 

After thinking more about it, could this be applied to the sandbox first so we can test before applying to production?

No other workflows will need this adjustment, but I did notice some of the other target levels are not saving in the drafts so I'll include all below just in case. These are the only two workflows that need this adjustment; although the new workflow does have an additional level compared to the modification.

Program New Workflow "Draft 4.22.2025 for Spring 2026 cycle":

Level 2 Approve = Target Level- 4, Target Status- "CS Approved"
Level 2 Request Changes stays at Level 2, Target Status- "CS Approved"

*Level 3 Approve = Target Level- 4, Target Status "CS Approved"
Level 3 Request Changes stays at Level 3, Target Status- "CS Approved"

*Level 4 Approve = Next Level- 4, Target Status "CC Approved"
*Level 4 Disapprove = Next Level- 3, Target Status "CS Approved"

Level 5 Approve = Target Level- Next Level, Target Status- "AVP Approved"
Level 5 Disapprove = Target Status "CC Approved", Restarts at Level 4

Level 6 Approve = Target Level- Next Level, Target Status- "BOT Approved"
Level 6 Disapprove = Target Status "AVP Approved", Restarts at Level 4

Level 7 Implement = Completes Proposal, Target Status- "Active"
Level 7 Disapprove = Target Status "BOT Approved", Restarts at Level 3


Program Modification Workflow "Draft 5.5.2025 for Spring 2026 cycle":

Level 2 Approve = Target Level- 4, Target Status- "CS Approved"
Level 2 Request Changes stays at Level 2, Target Status- "CS Approved"

Andrijana,

Yes, I have updated this support ticket to go to sandbox first for clients testing.

Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Hi Mike, 

That's perfect, thank you!

Kind Regards, 
Andrijana

I am the developer for this ticket.

I just wanted to double check that this is correct, because it appears that Approve will send it back to the workflow's same level.  If I set this up, when the Curriculum Coordinator does the Approve action on that level it will appear to do nothing because the proposal will just go right back to him.  This looks like a mistake to me, so I just wanted to double check before sending this to testing.  Did you mean for this action to go to level 5 as in the Next Level?

Thanks,
Trenton Jansen

Hi Trent,

Thank you for catching that, you are correct that was left in error. Approving should go to next level, 5. I appreciate you reaching out to confirm!

Kind Regards,
Andrijana

subject: SYSTEM - New user permissions issue
description:
New user approval positions won't save, please see attached ticket and video for details.

Please let me know if you need any further information.

Thank you, 
Andrijana Ekberg

comments.body:
New user approval positions won't save, please see attached ticket and video for details.

Please let me know if you need any further information.

Thank you, 
Andrijana Ekberg

Andrijana,

I just tried on our testing server and your production server and was able to add the position "**C&I Blue Group**" to the user "**Rebecca Overdorf**" with no issues. See image below. Note, I remove the position from your production site where I don't know if you wanted it left there or not.

 ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/ddckAQpYuWseghpeY1b65UO8T/?name=image.png)

The difference between my production screenshot and your video of your production site is, I'm using HTTPS in my URL for your site, and it looks like your video is not using HTTPS. We can tell by the "**Not secure**" in your URL video.

Normally when a client tries to reach our site if they are using HTTP, we auto direct them to HTTPS, but if your college is using some sort of proxy server where it is limiting their users to HTTP, then that could cause issues. I suggest you talk with your I.T. Department and see if they can to refresh the certificate for the metadata, so that you're able to connect to the currIQunet site using HTTPS. I believe that will fix the issue you're running into.

Where I'm not able to reproduce the issue, please give that a try and let us know if that helps with the issue you're running into and if this helped complete this support ticket or not.


Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Hi Mike, 

When I click on the URL in my address bar it expands to show https, I'm including what that looks like. Should this look different than what I have?

Kind Regards, 
Andrijana

Andrijana,

The HTTPS in your URL is good, but just before that it still says "**Not secure**". That should not be there. I believe that has something to do with your college proxy server that they are using between currIQunet and your college users. Most likely, they need to update a certificate on the proxy server that they are using.

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Thank you for the explanation! I have submitted a ticket to our IT department and will see what they can do. While I wait for a response, can we keep this ticket open until I can try it again?

Kind Regards,
Andrijana Ekberg

Andrijana,

Yes, I will set this ticket to pending status until we hear back from you.

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Good afternoon, 

I'm still working with our support team on my "Not secure" connection, no one else on my team receives this. With that said, I had another team member try adding the user permissions to Rebecca Overdorf's access and upon saving she had the same issue, no position saved for Rebecca. Is there something else in the background that could be preventing this?

I was able to add the approval for Rebecca. Several poeple on the team have tried this and it is not working for them at the school. They are getting some new faculty and need this working so they can add the permissions ASAP.

Andrijana,

I tried testing again, but this time as you, and was still able to add the position, and enter a start date as you. See image below.

 ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/413czmbvmvraJ4DEi1hUwfcCF/?name=image.png)

I'm not for sure why it would work for some and not others, where I haven't been able to reproduce the issue.

You could try adding position to user at home on a personal computer that is not VPN to your college to see if that works.

You could also try using a different browser and/or try incognito mode.


Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Hi Mike, 

I tried Google Chrome incognito mode as well as Firefox and still have the same issue. When I save it flashes quickly but still states "No matching records found." I've attached videos of this; the blue group shows in the first video from your addition. 

I also tried my personal cell phone via Safari and have the same issue. I'm the last one in the office today so I don't have anyone else to test it at this time but if occurring on my SPC computer and personal phone, it seems like the issue would still affect the others on our team as well.

Erica,

Thank you for the details. I see now why it worked for me and not for you. I was using today's date and you are entering a future date.

Looks like this is a bug. I will move this ticket forward as a DST to look into fixing the issue of when trying to use a future date, it inserts the record mapping incorrectly, and then doesn't display in UI do to the bad data mapping that was inserted incorrectly.

The current workaround is to use the current date that you're adding the position to the user.

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Hi Mike, 

Thanks for noticing that. I thought the first few times I tried it, it didn't save even without a date but I could be remembering wrong. I just tried it without a date and it seems to work on my end. I'll continue assigning access without dates in the meantime. 

Kind Regards,
Andrijana

Andrijana,

Correct, when you don't enter a date, it just uses the current date and works. It also works if you enter the current date.

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Good morning Mike, 

Our committee start reviews today, if you're able to look into this soon I promise not to open any new tickets today! :)

As mentioned previously, I added C&I group user permissions for three new members. However, today I received an email from Rebecca Overdorf; she had received an automated system email for the Articulations Coordinator role. I checked her access and for some unknown reason she had that role and C&I Blue Group listed twice. I was able to delete the Articulations Coordinator permission but cannot delete either duplicate of C&I Blue Group. Shirley also tried this and it won't work. I did not list any dates when adding her access last week. It does the same thing as before when I try to delete one- just flashes over the box but no update made. 

I'm not sure if this would be an issue for her once courses are assigned for review today. But she is new to all of this so wanted to avoid any confusion in case her approvals show duplicates or her automated system emails. Are you able to delete one of these duplicates on your end? 

I checked the others but she's the only new member this has happened with.
I sincerely appreciate your assistance!

Thank you, 
Andrijana

Andrijana,

I went to your production site and deleted the position "**C&I Blue Group**" from the user "**Rebecca Overdorf**" in their **Approval** permissions five or six times before that extra record removed. Somehow it was added that many times, and needed to be deleted that many times to get the data to fix itself for all those duplicate records.

Will continue to leave this ticket as block by DST until we hear movement for this bug.


Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Oh my! Thank you so so much, I truly appreciate it!! Only shows once on my end now. 

Sounds good on ticket status!

subject: SPC - Pathway Notes
description:
I ran into an issue today with pathway notes not displaying correctly on active program proposals. Yesterday, I updated DIG-AS (ID:1580) and left a pathway note, when I checked that today it was gone. Today, I updated the LEAN-CT (ID:1084) active pathway and although I added a note, it was not showing on the pathway report. I refreshed my page, the report, clicked another tab and back in, it took me three times adding it to finally stick. Not so sure if it will still be there by tomorrow. I can send this in as a ticket but wanted to check if there anything happening in the background that could be causing this? I’ve noticed the Approvals page in Sandbox earlier was also working wonky.

We will be correcting about 26 pathways shortly, all requiring pathway notes.

**Sorry, I didn’t realize this screenshot cut off, but the pathway notes were blank with text in the box within the proposal.**

 ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/ZdsYpdcgOQH9Qtx4JlrLeAgli/?name=inlineImage.png)

Andrijana,

I went into program "**Digital Media (DIG-AS)**" (1580), page "**Program Pathways**", field "**Pathway Notes**", inputted some text, and then went and look at the report "**Program Pathways**", and I see the updated text with no issues. See attached image below.

 ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/bGIoEoL70vOcLKimeu115fqQu/?name=image.png)

I went into program "**Lean Six-Sigma Green Belt (LEAN-CT)**" (1084), page "**Program Pathways**", field "**Pathway Notes**", inputted some text, and then went and look at the report "**Program Pathways**", and I see the updated text with no issues. See attached image below.

 ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/3MbkNOZkCU1MrNvNflH308t6r/?name=image.png)

I haven't been able to reproduce the issue.

What browser are you using?

Are you log in as you or a different user? If different user, which user?

Have you cleared your browser cache and cookies?

Have you tried other browsers to see if it works?

Are you VPN to your college with your personal computer or are you at your college use college computer on college network?

Are you using multiple tabs in the same browser, where each tab is using the Meta/Maverick site and making changes to multiple things around the same time in the different tabs?

Are other users having the same issue that you're having?

What text are you trying to input into the RTE text-area?

Are you inputting text or copy/paste text into the RTE text-area?

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Hi Mike, 

My apologies for the delayed response. Thanks for looking into this. Below are my answers. 

What browser are you using? Chrome
 
Are you log in as you or a different user? If different user, which user? Logged in as me only
 
Have you cleared your browser cache and cookies? Yes
 
Have you tried other browsers to see if it works? I just tried Firefox and for now at least, it seemed to take a direct type in note and pasted in note. However, sometimes I've noticed even Chrome may take it the first time and the following day the note may be gone. 

Not to get off topic, but I also had a change in a different program - program summary, not stick recently. Last week I updated a program summary, verified the change showed on the program of study, and left an admin note and this week although my admin note of the change was still in the proposal, the change wasn't there anymore nor the recent "page last saved by" so I'm not sure what happened with that either. 
 
Are you VPN to your college with your personal computer or are you at your college use college computer on college network? VPN w/college computer
 
Are you using multiple tabs in the same browser, where each tab is using the Meta/Maverick site and making changes to multiple things around the same time in the different tabs? No, I don't believe so. I may have some other Meta tabs open such as a report or search screen but only making changes to one proposal at a time. 
 
Are other users having the same issue that you're having? Shirley experienced it a couple times, but not recently. She uses Firefox and Chrome. 
 
What text are you trying to input into the RTE text-area? Lately the text has been "Pathway sequence was updated X/XX/2025 to reflect recommendations for Fall 2025 (0655)."

Are you inputting text or copy/paste text into the RTE text-area? I've tried both ways, typing directly into the text field and pasting from desktop sticky note.

Andrijana,

Where we're VPN into college/work, that itself normally slows down the system process of saving data.

Where I haven't been able to reproduce the issue, I can only assume that after a user inputs data, maybe it didn't save due to moving off the page too quickly.

Any time I enter data into a text-box or text-area, I always click just outside the text input area, to make sure it forces a save and I wait a few seconds to make sure it saves.

Sometimes when there is so much data on page/tab and auto-save triggers, it can take a few seconds longer then normal where it is trying to re-save every thing in that page/tab.

The page "**Program Pathways**" for program ID (1580) does have a lot of courses on it, so it will take longer to save where there is so much data to re-save on this page.

I tried testing again today and I still haven't been able to reproduce the issue. Have you or any other user been able to reproduce the issue today, and if so, what exact steps was taken, so I can follow along and replicate the issue?

Also, if you can reproduce the issue on a normal bases, we can hop on a Teams Meeting, so I can watch to see the process of getting that issue you're running into.


Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Hi Mike, 

That makes sense, thank you. I will be sure to always click out of the textbox after adding a note in the future. I wasn't able to replicate the issue today with directly typing a note in the field, but I was able to replicate it with pasting a note. I took a screen record of it just in case. Once I refreshed the page, it was appearing on the pathway report. Now, that's not always the case for me but I do feel better equipped in having a couple tricks up my sleeve when I may run into it again. It's okay to close this ticket for now, if it ends up being a bigger issue for me later and can't get it to stick, I'll be sure to reach back out. I just wanted to share what steps I take when I've had it happen. I've attached the screen recording for reference. 

Thanks for your help!

Andrijana,

Is your bookmark URL to currIQunet site still using http instead of https?

I noticed in your video, your browser says "Not secure", which normally means it is using http. That could be part of the issue.

 ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/Q0UWEmqaKlNFZbdAJK7gqMR4V/?name=image.png)


Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Andrijana,

Also, when you see that "Not secure" in the browser URL, it could also mean your college is using some sort of proxy server, and if their proxy server does not have their certificate updated, this could also cause issues with saving and slow time.

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Andrijana,

I will go ahead and close this ticket out as you requested, but if you run into it again, give us a call, and we can do a Teams call to share screens, and I can watch your process, to see if I see anything going wrong if you like.

Regards,

subject: SPC - Course Block Library Auto Update - Urgent
description: SPC updated a course block library on the active version and it did not auto update the blocks in all the programs that it is currently in. Please see if there is configuration or something we need to get this updated.

comments.body: SPC updated a course block library on the active version and it did not auto update the blocks in all the programs that it is currently in. Please see if there is configuration or something we need to get this updated.
I did not say anything because I am only like 60% sure that this not correct and blocks do not auto update so I wanted to verify first.
If I am right, can we run a script to update them? We need tro do something to fix this.

Shirley,

The Course Block Library does not auto update, it only copies the data to a program at the time of import.

This configuration does this so that the user can change the import of data as needed per each program.

If you update an Active Course Block Library, you would then have to re-import to each of the programs that you want to import to as needed.

If you're importing to a program you already imported to for the first imported block version, then you would want to delete the first imported block library from that program, and then import the new block version to that program if needed, or you can manually update the program as needed.

The system process for the Course Block Library is working as it was designed to.

Does this complete this support ticket?

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Hi Mike…



This does complete this ticket.



However, the side problem to this is that if there needs to be an edit made to a group block in the Library, we will have to update the block and then drop it back into the program.  For this project, there are 98 programs this has to be done.  So, for example, a word is misspelled, and we need to fix it, we would then have to go back and re-drop that group block again into 98 programs.  This is not efficient.



The next phase of this project I have about 50 – 60 more to review and update manually – after I get the relevant blocks in the library updated.

** **

**THANKS, MIKE…I appreciate all you do!!**

** **

**~S**

subject: Need info for new program type/form - Industy Certs
description:
Hi Mike...

I am going to create a new program type, forms, and reports for Industry Certifications (IC).  It will be set up very similar to our current Articulations program type (AR).

What/How is the best way for me to set this up for each form (new, modification, deactivation), pages for each form, fields on each page, and reports.

So you have a basic template or something I can use?  I want to make this as easy as possible for me to design but also easy for you all to interpret (aka read my mind LOL) what I want and need.

Any guidance will be great and appreciated!

We can also set up a call too with you and/or someone on your team, and/or Natalie. LMK

THANKS!
~S

Looking at your current program proposal types for **Articulation**, I see these three program proposal types:

If you're wanting three new program proposal types for **Industry Certifications**, we will need to create them for you, where I would assume they need new program forms specifically just for these three new program proposal types.

I would assume we would do the same for **Industry Certifications**.

If you want the forms to be somewhat the same as the forms for **Articulation**, you can use those as examples when giving us details of your requests, take screenshots of all the pages and fields, with edits of what you want to keep, what you want removed, and what you want new.

subject: SPC - Notes Tab
description:
know the Notes tab in the History page of the proposals is still a work-in-progress with an open ticket we had of the author field being required. But I wanted to bring this to your attention anyway because before the UI update, the Notes would automatically include the following information once added: Author, date, and time. However, upon adding a note to CHM 2045 none of this appears, only the textbox.

I just wanted to check if this was just how it is right now or if this is something being worked on. I’m not sure why it would ask for author, though, if it won’t be used in the display. Please advise, thank you!!

-- This notification was sent from JIRA MS-18503 to this ticket by Trenton Jansen. --

I am the developer for this ticket
Yes, this is how it currently works in Maverik. However, I believe in the old UI admins could also leave notes with an author other than themselves.  
In terms the author of the note not appearing on the note in Maverik, this is true.  However, If you want to know the author of the note you can still hit the edit button on the note to look at the author of the note.

Andrijana,

Regards,

(208) 522-1225

Hi there, 

Thank you for the reply! Yes, in the old UI you could leave notes as another author besides yourself but that is also true in the new UI. I just added a note as if I'm Angela on CHM 2045 (Minor Mod, ID: 10063) to test and it still posted, see attached. 

The old UI would automatically pull the selected author, date, and time, as shown in the previous image. Can this be added to the new UI? This should be information readily available when you click on that Notes tab in proposal history and not necessarily dig for author by clicking edit if no edits are needed. The history tab is able to pull the user and action date/time, could this be similarly applied to the notes somehow?

In addition, what are the rules behind this Notes tab? From my understanding, the old UI had this tab for every proposal in the workflow, and admins could leave notes at any level within the workflow. But course MUH 1110 (Minor Mod, ID: 10070) doesn't have a notes tab at all. Course CHM 2045 (Minor Mod, ID: 10063) has Admins Position Group with all us Admins as Authors in the drop down, but course EDF 4930 (Major Mod, ID: 10010) only has Position Group Admins and Curriculum Services as the only author. I've attached screenshots of these instances. Please advise. 

Thank you!

-- This notification was sent from JIRA MS-18503 to this ticket by Trenton Jansen. --

For your request about changes in how notes work in Maverik, the only thing I can do is to put in a request for development to the people above me.  If it is accepted, I do not know how long it would take for this change to occur, it is done based on priority and it would go out during a normal release.  If it is not accepted, I would reply back to you.  Would you like for me to do so?

For the rules involved in the notes tab, all admins can view and leave notes as any user in the current level of the workflow of any proposal regardless of whether the admin is in that workflow. The reason why the notes tab does not appear for MUH 1110 (Minor Mod, ID: 10070) is because for some reason this course has no users assigned in its current level of the workflow.  

This is something that I am not sure is correct behavior, if I put in a request for development at the same time I can also ask if this is correct behavior and if it is not change this so that Notes tab can be viewed while there are no users assigned in its current level of the workflow.

Andrijana,

The question/field for “If embedded SPC Cert…” is already answered as yes. But no Certificates are noted in the selection 0 of 11, even though they are showing in the table.

Is there anyway someone can look at everything that has to do with the Pathways?  Too many things are happening.

This ticket is a duplicate of #41954. Closing this ticket.

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Shirley,

The reason "**CAD-CT, RAPID-CT**" display for program "**Applied Engineering Technology (ENG-AS)**" (1752) in course "**ETD 2364C**" in the column "**SPC Certificates**", but does not display in the field "**Does this course count toward an embedded SPC Certificate?**" checklist section is because those two programs are "**Historical**", and that checklist has custom configuration to filter out the historical programs.

Do you want me to update the custom configuration to only display historical programs when saved to that course for the field "**Does this course count toward an embedded SPC Certificate?**" checklist section? That way you can uncheck them as needed.

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Shirley,

I believe you will say yes to my last question, so I will move forward with this ticket, so we can get this moving forward towards your live site.

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Shirley,

**Developer Comments:**

- Program page "**Program Pathways**":

  - Section "**Sequence**":

    - For the third section "**Please select the certificates**", that only displays when field "**Does this course count toward an embedded SPC Certificate?**" is "**Yes**", updated this checklist to continue to display "**Active**" programs, and to only display "**Historical**" programs if saved to the course in current program.
    - If a historical program is saved, then display that checked option with the added red text “**(Historical)**”, that way the user is able to uncheck them as needed.
    - If not needed, then just leave as is, so that the historical mappings stay displaying for historical purposes.
    - If the user unchecks the historical options, then those options will disappear once the page has been refreshed/or newly opened after they have been unchecked, as long as they are not checked in any of the other courses in the “**Program Pathways**”.
    - If the historical program still displays as an option, that means you have to go uncheck them in the other courses in that same program.
- Tested with the program "**Applied Engineering Technology (ENG-AS)**" (1752) in record course "**ETD 2364C**", confirmed I can now see this historical saved options, and I’m able to uncheck them, so that the program code no longer displays in the column “**SPC Certificates**” that displays at the bottom of the page in the custom table display.
- ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/SArMGEy83rjxf2QJQ9YFNFJUt/?name=image.png)
- ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/49tskUnkjtz1dsY5VprCEvrob/?name=image.png)
- ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/pPkOFkL8VjuxUCRxYgZdIkwS4/?name=image.png)

Now moving this support ticket forward. If no errors, changes will then be applied to the production site.

We will let you know once the changes have been applied to the production site.
Ticket moved to Code review

Mike…Well, it depends.  I thought that for these certificates, that as long as there was an active version then it should always pick up that active version.  We don’t need historical for this. And, when the current active ends up turning historical and a new active version is available, then it should auto-change to the new active version.  We are not going to go back in and re-mark the new active nor remove historical.



If the above can’t be done, then some discussions are needed.



Also, going forward, when a certificate turns historical and no longer an active version, that we will then need to do what to remove the certificate from the Pathway?  Will we have to go in and actually change the field question to NO and that will remove them from the table/report?



~S



** **

Shirley,

Clarification on Certificate Proposal aka Program Proposals:

Looking at the configuration for your custom program/certificate proposals, I found that auto-update is not enabled. It has never been available in this setup. The system has been designed to hide program/certificate proposals if they are updated to historical status.

For clarification, while the current program has auto-update enabled for courses mapped to it, this does not extend to program/certificate proposals. These proposals remain static once saved, even if they become historical.

Your existing configuration ensures that when a program is marked as historical, it continues to display the courses that were mapped to it. This prevents unwanted updates from occurring when program/certificate proposals are modified and reactivated, preserving historical data.

You previously mentioned that you expected active versions of certificates to be picked up automatically. However, program/certificate proposals do not currently function that way. This might be due to a distinction in how courses are displayed. The “Course” column operates differently, showing active versions within course families even when a program is historical. As a result, historical programs will always display active courses within those families rather than the exact versions that were originally mapped.

I can explore options for implementing an auto-update for program/certificate proposals inside courses that are inside programs, but I am unsure whether it will work as expected or if there will be any limitations. I will do some research and testing and update you once I have more information.

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Thanks for the information, Mike.  Let me take these rules to my team to review so that we completely understand Meta’s process.  Then decide if we need to ask for a change on your end or do we make a process change on my end.  If a change must be made on my end, it involves many others outside of my team.  Give a couple of days to get back to you.



~S

Shirley,

I have developed a new configuration for **program/certificate proposals** inside courses that are inside programs, to auto update to the newly **Active** version of those program/certificate proposals inside courses that are inside programs, when those program/certificate proposals are modified, and the modified version becomes **Active**, to then look for its parent, and to update the parent program/certificate proposals to the child where program/certificate proposals in a course that are in a program that has not gone **Historical**.

I believe this will now work the way you were expecting it to before.

I believe I can also update any existing proposals where exists to their active parent version, if they are not already set up that way.

Do you want me to move forward with this new development?

I will also leave in the previous development that I stated, so that you can still see historical mappings as needed for historical purposes. This new configuration also allows you to update any current programs if needed, this doesn't mean you have to, it just means it is there if needed.

Do you want me to move forward with what I have come up with?

Do you want my changes to go directly to live, or would you like to test them in sandbox first?

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Shirley,

Does the below sound and look good to you, and is this ticket ready to move forward towards live?

**Developer Comments:**

- Program page "**Program Pathways**":

  - Section "**Sequence**":

    - For the third section "**Please select the certificates**", that only displays when the field "**Does this course count toward an embedded SPC Certificate?**" is "**Yes**":

      - Updated this checklist to continue to display "**Active**" certificate/program, and to only display "**Historical**" certificate/program if saved to the course in current program, this is so the historically saved data displays as it should when exists.
      - If a historical program is saved, then display that checked option with the added red text “**(Historical)**”, that way the user is able to uncheck them if needed.
      - If not needed, then just leave as is, so that the historical mappings stay displaying for historical purposes.
      - If the user unchecks the historical options, then those options will disappear once the page has been refreshed/or newly opened after they have been unchecked, as long as they are not checked in any of the other courses in the “**Program Pathways**” for that current program.
      - If the historical program still displays as an option, that means you have to go uncheck them in the other courses in that same program if needed.
    - For the first section "**Please select the certificates**", that only displays when the field "**Does this course count toward an embedded Articulation Agreement?**" is "**Yes**":

      - Updated this checklist to continue to display "**Active**" articulation/certificate/program, and to only display "**Historical**" articulation/certificate/program if saved to the course in current program.
      - If historical, then display red text "**(Historical)**" at end of option for historical purposes.
  - Field "**Course**":

    - Updated the display of the subject code and course number to have a space in between them, so it is easier to read and search for.
- Created custom auto update configuration for **Program/Certificate** proposals that are saved in a course that is saved in a program when triggered by **Program/Certificate** status changes. Note, this also applies to **Articulation/Certificate/Program** section.
- Created a script to update all programs that are not “**Historical**”, where if in page “**Program Pathways**”, has a course mapped to the program, and that course is mapped to an articulation/certificate/program or a certificate/program, and if any of those records are “**Historical**”, then update it to the “**Active**” version if exist in that program family.
- Report “**Program Pathway**”:

  - Section “**Pathway Sequence**”:

    - Column "**SPC Certificates**", updated the column to display program code only when exists.
- Due to the custom configuration that is based off award type code, removing the "**Award Type**" aka "**Degree Types**" lookup in the **Lookup Manager**, so that the client doesn't accidentally affect the custom configuration on the program forms and reports.
- Tested with the following programs:

  - **Applied Engineering Technology (ENG-AS)** (1752), in record course "**ETD 2364C**", confirmed the above when I look in this course in page “**Program Pathways**”, for field “**Does this course count toward an embedded SPC Certificate?**” is checked “**Yes**”, and the two historical checked programs now displays the active programs as checked. Client can leave as it, or uncheck if needed now.
  - **Early Childhood Education (CHDEV-AS)** (1284), confirmed I can see the saved “**Historical**” options.

  - Confirmed all the above.


Regards,
Mike Gregston

subject: PROGRAMS – Pathway Report/Table duplicate SPC Certs   CS260
description:
PROGRAM Pathway table and Report (#239) showing in various rows, SPC Certs more than once even though set up to show only once in each respective  row/sequence #.
CLOUD-AS (#1484) and
EAM-AS  (#1608) EAM subplan


IS THERE A WAY TO CHECK OTHER ACTIVE PROGRAMS TO SEE IF SPC CERTIFICATES ARE DUPLICATED?

See attachment for details and screenshots.

~S

comments.body:
PROGRAM Pathway table and Report (#239) showing in various rows, SPC Certs more than once even though set up to show only once in each respective  row/sequence #.

If so, you can't, because the word "**Certificate**" would come first before the word "**Practitioner**" if you're wanting alpha order.

The alpha order starts from the first letter and then compares every letter after it for sorting.

Shirley,

I did some testing and I confirmed when the checkbox **Enable Auto Sort** is checked, it doesn't auto sort the **Link List** records. I also found to get it to work, after you check that checkbox, click inside one of your **Link List** options, then click out of it or click in another **Link List** item where you update the text for that item, and it will then trigger the auto sort. Shirley,

For some reason, those two **Artificial Intelligence Responsible...** records did not have a sort order created for each recorded.

On your live site, I disabled the "**Enable Auto Sort**", and then refreshed the page, then I drag and drop those two **Artificial Intelligence Responsible...** records to where they are supposed to be, then refreshed the page, then I enabled "**Enable Auto Sort**", and refreshed the page, and I believe now they are all in the correct order on menu page "**Programs by Degree Type**".

I then repeated this process for page "**Academic Programs of Study**", so it also should be in the correct order.

The above is not something you would normally have to do, but I did try a test that seems to work for future:

- Starting with a brand-new page, add a **Link List** section, then click on “**Enable Auto Sort**”, then refresh the page, then add three or more records that are not in order, then refresh the page, it then does auto sort the records.

The checkbox “**Enable Auto Sort**” seems a little finicky if turning off and on and not refreshing in between.

If you are modifying a catalog and the sort order in that catalog is correct, it should continue to work correctly.

If you are creating something brand-new, follow the bullet point above.

I have created a development support ticket for our development to look into, but I don't know when or if it will be added to are road map or not.

Please go check those two pages, and let me know if everything is looking good now, or let me know if more ordering needs updated.

Does this help complete this support ticket?

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

I took a quick look and seems ok.



As long as you open a dev ticket,  then we should be good for now.

subject: SPC - API - Documentation - URGENT
description:
I would be remiss if I didn’t express our frustration over this situation.  I believe that we have asked for documentation and updates with system changes such as this.  While I don’t know the exact protocol, I do know that this has happened several times and leads to a negative impact on us as well as our students.  The information that has been impacted with this most recent issue, is attached to a requirement of the state of Florida, putting us out of compliance.  I believe this can be avoided if we are given information on these type of changes prior to implementation and my understanding is that this is a standard process.

We need to create documentaion that shows the name of the fields and the name in the API and if we can store this in Meta and keep it updated when there is any changes, that would be the best. This has caused several issues at SPC and we need to get this resolved ASAP.

This is a new field that was recently (late summer-ish/early fall-ish) added to the Pathways.  This field is added as a new column to the table (at the bottom of the form page) and on the Pathway Report.  The Articulation Agreement, if yes, is added to that column and should display both the program title and the program code as a link.  The SPC Certificate column should display ONLY the program as a link, and the Ind Cert column is the full title (no program code or link – yet).

subject: SPC - Pages are slow to load
description: Please take a look at the configuration and see ifwe can increase the load time. It is taking longer than Meta 3.

comments.body: Please take a look at the configuration and see ifwe can increase the load time. It is taking longer than Meta 3.

Hello Shirley,

I was able to take a deep dive into all configuration and I could clean up a little but with how complicated your program forms are they will take a little bit to load.
Here are a list of things I looked at and changed:

- Fix Help text to ensure uniformity on form load
- Fix Required Read only (calculated fields)
- Remove INLINE HTML from System level fields (none present at time of fix but just in case)
- Removed MetaTemplateTypes that are not used
- Remove bad Section Settings that do nothing but take up bandwidth
- Fix Old Style Calculated fields (None present at time of fix but just in case)
- Delete Empty Help text that was Invisible on Maverick but took up bandwidth
- Delete Empty Sections that would be invisible but took up bandwidth
- Update Old Style Drop downs (Literals) to new style (None present at time of fix but just in case)
- Delete old show/hide rules when we use to compare to Negative One in the back end that hasn’t done anything for a while but takes up bandwidth
- Checked all Look ups that are filtered to ensure they use Context if possible to speed them up
- Updated all Templates
  - Note this means all Templates will need to be cached again so the very first load will be the longest.
Notes about forms:

- Course
  - Nothing seems to take that long to load on any course form and is consistent will other clients
- Program
  - They are very complex with loading with the following all creating a lot different loads
    - Subplan
    - Pathway Set Up
    - Program Pathways
    - Course Blocks
    - Articulation Programs
      - This is because it has to load (Render) and check list with 500+ items into it for a selection of Programs
On testing I looked at Program 485 as it seemed to have a good amount of Data to load in and I was able to take it down from 13 Seconds to 10 (This was after the Template was cached)

Thank you,
Nate W.
Ticket moved to Code review

subject: SPC - Florida CIP Table

PROGRAM         STATE#                 META#                  FED#

ELEDR-BS           1101312021     1013120201      13.1202

(id 1092)

Elementary Education



All the FL colleges are using/reporting the State #, however, we have a slightly different # in Meta.  Trying to determine how/why that is. Some of the FL CIP # have changed and not sure why.  Maybe the file that was used to update the CIP table back when we moved to Meta something happened.  This week while I was looking through old emails around the 2022/23 time frame, I remember seeing something from you all (maybe Clint???) that said that any old number that were already in a proposal will not go away so they stay in the proposal (even is historical), but the old number no longer available in the active table…only the new number would be available.



The State is saying that their number is correct however, the Meta number is or is not correct?



Help!



Can you send me a copy of the FL CIP table that you have?



I have some other program examples if needed.

subject: SPC - Maverick Items
description:
It looks like each time (again) there is a refresh or a release to Maverick, the other work that has been done disappears.  Just ran across today that the Courses > Course Owner page is showing back up AND the Approval button has disappeared.



Here is a screenshot I sent to Andrijana in a chat back on 7/29…as you can see, on that date anyway, the Course Owner had been removed and the Approvals button added.

Kind of like the same problem we kept having with the clone proposal and the work Mike G. did.  It would be there and then again gone.



I don’t know where we are with tickets these days.  Some things have been done, some done and disappeared, and some not done.  It is hard to do testing when things like the above happens.



Also, the Course Requisites in Maverick still not working correctly either.  Page doesn’t auto save, have to leave proposal or refresh the page for the changes to show up, remove a requisite, but it does not clear from the Catalog View, it’s adding “minimum grade of C” with no indication to do so. MAN 9999 (id# 9585) is my practice/test course.  There is a ticket #38766 I can’t test b/c nothing in the Requisites seems to function correctly.

subject: PROGRAMS - Pathway Embedded Certificates Duplicate/Red Font
description:

Can you please look into this issue? We are planning on making changes to the Pathways Tab and Reports when we move to the new UI and would like this to be figured out and cleaned up prior to. 

Please see attached ticket for more details. Let me know if you have any questions.
