Hello Andrijana,

It is showing this to keep historical data. The red ones that get pulled get pulled in because it was historically mapped to a older version and the black is the new version. If you deselect all the red ones selected and only the black ones they should all disappear and you are up to date. Please let me know how you would like to continue.

Thank you,
Nate W.

Hi Nate, 

Thanks for looking into this. We’ve never had historical versions pull into the pathways as selections, I’m not sure why this is happening now. Are you able to update this so it’s only pulling active versions in the list, as it was before? It should auto-update. 

For instance, if CJPSS-AS has an embedded certificate of CST-CT and the certificate goes through the workflow for an update, that CJPSS-AS program should reflect the new version of the certificate (once implemented) in the AS active proposal > pathway.

At this time, it doesn't do much version-wise on the pathway report but we're looking to add links to embedded certificates programs of study in Maverick so this will need to only pull in actives. However, in the pathway building, we'll need this cleaned up so it's ready for that Maverick update. 

Please let me know if you have any questions.
Thank you, 
Andrijana

description:

SEE ATTACHMENT FOR INFORMATION AND DETAILS

Thanks!
~S

comments.body:
Programs > COMPARISON – PROGRAM report (391) between two historical proposals is not displaying correctly and missing information. (Between active version and 1 historical is OK.)

Would like to know (ASAP) if it is because the COURSE BLOCKS were set up completely different between the 2 versions and it is difficult for the Comparison report to render correctly? 

Or is/are there errors in the report generation? 

SEE ATTACHMENT FOR INFORMATION AND DETAILS

Thanks!
~S

Hi Shirley,

I have assigned this ticket to a developer's queue. The developer will be in contact with you if they have any questions.

Hello Shirley,

You are correct on your assumption it gets very hard for our code to compare two different version of the form and if they are set up very different from each other. The comparison report is a built in item into Meta that we do not configure at all besides a couple little things (two scroll bars, only show differences), it always goes through the same thing on the back end. If the Comparison report would throw a single error, the whole report would not render and you would be met with a error page.  Sadly I can't do any simple or quick ticket to resolve this and possibly would require a whole new way to compare reports that we do not have. Please let me know how you would like to continue with this ticket.

Thank you,
Nate W.

Thank you, Nate, for getting back so quickly.  It is much appreciated!!



I will get back to you soon…no later than end of day.



~S





_ _

Hi Nate...

Thank you for the explanation and that my assumption was correct.  Since you answered my question and now there really is no fix needed...so no ticket is needed.
Marking as solved.

~S

subject: SPC - Meta Preferred Name
description:
Earlier this week when I logged into Meta, my name was updated to “Anj” in the system. I did not do this nor did anyone on my Team. I thought maybe it somehow suddenly fed in from V2, but I couldn't access the site to be sure. Then, I noticed more usernames were adjusted (below). I was finally able to access V2 today and it looks like it’s pulling in whatever the preferred name was there into Meta. I’m not sure why this is suddenly occurring but is there a way we can prevent that? The preferred name is what shows on the proposal approval history and “page last saved by” and I rather avoid having to research who Shaky Pudding is (lol) or seeing duplicates. (Ironically, with that C&I Red Team we also have two folks with the last name Thomas)



If we can either prevent the preferred name from V2 pulling in, or just have it so the actual first and last name is used for these purposes, that would prevent future confusion.

Hi Shirley,
My name is Heather and I'm the developer working on this ticket.

We can provide admin reports in XML format - this tends to work best with simple datasets but can work with more complex datasets as well.

With the way our system is currently set up, an admin report can only be set in our database to output as one format type at a time. Would you like this report to be available in both XML and Excel? If that's the case, I can create two report versions - one that outputs in Excel, and one that outputs in XML.

I've attached examples of what this particular admin report would look in both output formats below. Please look these over and let me know if everything looks correct, and which format(s) you would like to set as the output for the report.

**Note:** We could also set up XML reports as API endpoints so that they could all be grabbed directly by your IT department instead of being individually generated by an admin. That setup would take some additional configuration on our end, but if you are wanting XML data available for your admin reports, that may be a better long-term solution.

Thank you,
~Heather Ludwig

Hi Heather...

Thanks for the info.  It is good to know that you all can provide an XML file.  

I think for now, for the report above, an Excel file will work great.

~S

Shirley,
Sounds good! I am moving this forward to our testing. When it passes testing, the ticket will be applied to your live site and the admin report will be available as an Excel file.

~Heather Ludwig

subject: SPC - Cloning
description: Please add the cloning proposals to both course and programs.

comments.body: Please add the cloning proposals to both course and programs.

Hi Shirley,

I have assigned this ticket to a developer's queue. The developer will be in contact with you if they have any questions.

Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Hello,

My name is Mike G. I just wanted to let you know I am starting development on this request. If I have any questions, I will contact you.

Have a great day!

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Shirley,

To configure the cloning feature, it is best to create a new proposal type for this purpose for course and for program.

**Courses:**

- What do you want the new proposal type for course cloning to be named?
- Which workflow do you want this new proposal type to use?
- We would just use the **New Course** form for this new proposal type.
- What course statuses do you want this new course cloning proposal type to be able to clone from?
  - Example, do you want to only be able to clone from courses that have the status of Active, or maybe the status of Active and Approved.
- What course fields do you want to be blacklisted?
  - This means specific fields do not copy over when a course is cloned.
  - Example: subject code, course number, course title, first effective term, and originator.
**Programs:**

- What do you want the new proposal type for program cloning to be named?
- Which workflow do you want this new proposal type to use?
- We would just use the **New Program** form for this new proposal type.
- What program statuses do you want this new program cloning proposal type to be able to clone from?
  - Example, do you want to only be able to clone from programs that have the status of Active, or maybe the status of Active and Approved.
- What program fields do you want to be blacklisted?
  - This means specific fields do not copy over when a program is cloned.
  - Example: program title, award type title, what is the term the Program or associated changes will take effect?, and originator.

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Shirley,

Which of these existing course proposal types do you want to be able to clone from? Do you want to be able to clone from all of them?

- New Credit Course
- Course Major Modification
- Course Minor Modification
- Course Reactivation Proposal
- Course Deactivation 5 Year
- Course Deactivation
- Course Administrative Modification

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

All if possible.



~S

subject: COURSES Reports - Course Impact Programs fix (from CS188 / ZD 39053)
description:
This ticket relates to ticket # 39053. It was not resolved correctly.

The COURSE IMPACT report (#419), in the PROGRAMS section should be:

Program Title
(Program Code)
“Status”
Effective Term

And include ALL program statuses:
DRAFT
All APPROVAL workflow statuses
ACTIVE 
HISTORICAL

where Effective Term ≤ 5 yrs  
([term – 75 = past term --- rolling terms]– EX: 640 through present; 635 through 560, 630 through 555, 625 through 550, etc.) 

List of Programs should be listed in alphabetical order by PROGRAM CODE,  
then by Effective Term newest to oldest 
(will typically be draft/approval workflow status, then active, then historicals). 
 
PROGRAMS refers to all program types:  Programs, Transfer Plans, and Articulations. 

ACG 2071 (9738) Course Impact report (419) should look something like this:

Accounting Technology Operations (ACTAP-CT) Active (640)
Accounting Technology Operations (ACTAP-CT) Historical (625)
Business Administration (BUS-AS) Active (640)
Business Administration (BUS-AS) Historical (625)
Business Administration (BUS-AS) Historical (610)
Business Administration (BUS-AS) Historical (585)
Business Administration (BUS-AS) Historical (625)
Business Administration (BUS-BS) Active (610)
Business Administration (BUS-BS) Historical (625)
Business Administration (BUS-TR) Active (640)
Business Administration (BUS-TR) Historical (610)
Business Administration (BUS-TR) Historical (580)
FAMU Accounting (FMACC-TR) Active (640)
FAMU Accounting (FMACC-TR) Historical (610)
FAMU Accounting (FMACC-TR) Historical (580)
etc…

(see attachment to see the way the report looks today)

Let me know if any questions.
Shirley

subject: Course/Programs using historical in error    CS198
description:
SEE ATTACHMENT FOR DETAILS AND SCREENSHOTS

#1   Course DEH 4852 (id 8343)
Should this course automatically update the prerequisite(s) to the Active version when a new version is available?
It does not appear to be that way.  It is showing historical, which is a major error.  

DEH 3813 (active id 7625)    (historical id 1959)
DENH-BAS  (active id 1606)   (historical id 1023)
Could an idea be that some of our courses when brought over from V2 into Meta used the then Active version ID number instead of the actual program title so it didn’t automatically update to the new active version?
If you look at the active version of DEH 4852 and look at the Requisites page, this is what is in each of the fields.
This tells me that it will only look for the specific requisite course ID (1959) and program plan ID 1023 and not for an updated/new active course/program proposal.
 
#2   Also, not sure if related or not. I had a ticket 39565 for Articulation Programs that is resolved.  However, as part of the ticket, Daniel added a list of other programs that were showing historical articulated programs (file attached to ticket).  
I would think that when a modification was done to a program, that it would then pick up the new active version.  
Let me know your thoughts and if these 2 things are related or completely different and need 2 different tickets or something I need to do on my end.

   On DEH, it should automatically update to the active version. We need to put a ticket in for that one
We should be able to use the auto-update to update the Articulation programs. Let's add that to the same ticket and our guys should be able to get this working.

Shirley,

Yes, the course requisites auto update is turned on for Meta, so when a child course goes active, the parent course should be updated to the child course in the course requisites page.

When we imported your courses over from V2, we did not update course requisite mappings as far as I know of if the course was active and the course requisite was historical, they should still have the same mappings as they had in V2. So if you had a Historical course mapped to an Active course as a course requisite, then that mapping would still exist.

Looking at course **DEH 3813** (7625), it was implemented back on 03/21/2016, so I would assume this was already mapped this way before the import, meaning the historical course was mapped to the active course already before import.

To fix the course, **DEH 4852** (8343), you as admin user can go manually update the course requisite as needed. Then the next time course **DEH 3813** is modified, it should auto update the course requisites as needed.

For the Articulation Programs, no, it does not auto update if a check program is modified. This checklist is just a checklist, it doesn't currently have the functionality for auto update.

Does this help complete this support ticket?

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Shirley,

Just to confirm what all is being requested in this support ticket:

1. Update all active courses that have a historical course requisite to the active version of that course requisite, correct?
2. Update all active courses that have a historical program plan requisite to the active version of the program plan requisite, correct?
3. Update all active programs that have a historical articulation program to the active version of the articulation program, correct?
4. Remove any articulation program duplicates that are checked in the program tab “**Articulation Programs**”, so that the check articulation program only displays once as checked on this tab, correct?
5. Look into creating custom auto-update for the program tab “**Articulation Programs**” that are checked, to auto-update to the active version when the program is modified and is implemented, correct?


Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Shirley,

**Developer Comments:**

- Updated all active courses that have a historical course requisite to the active version of that course requisite.
- Updated all active courses that have a historical program plan requisite to the active version of the program plan requisite.
- Tested with the course **DEH 4852** (8343), confirmed I now see the course requisite active version, and also confirmed I now see the course program plan requisite active version.
- ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/9mmV1a1mMHttKbN2ReUvzyHLE/?name=image.png)
- On program tab “**Articulation Programs**”, in the checklist, updated the checked historical programs to their current active program version, also removed any checked program that was duplicated per that program.
- Created a custom auto-update for the program tab “**Articulation Programs**” for the checklist, so that when a checked program is modified, when it becomes implemented, to auto-update the program parent-id with the program child-id for that program family on this tab where that parent program was checked.
- Tested with the program “**(Drinking) Water Treatment Plant Operator Class A**” (1467), confirmed I now see the active program version checked, and I don’t see any duplicates of that same checked program. I then created a program modification for the checked program, and then implemented it, and confirmed that the active child program is now checked on this tab for this program.

Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Shirley,

We looked into this, and what's causing this problem is duplicated data. For example, for program TSSHS-AR, there are only 5 unique selected programs, but each one of those selected programs appears twice in the list, which is why it's showing 10 total selected programs. This most likely happened during an import error when migrating data from v2, but I'm not certain.

There were a total of 29 programs with this issue. But we'll go ahead and submit a script to delete the duplicated data, so you won't encounter this issue again once we deploy it to live. The "Clear All" button will work as well once we deploy this fix.

I'll also go ahead so only programs that are active or in the approval workflow show up in the list. Keep in mind that Historical programs will still show up if they were already selected in the list. If you unselect a historical program and save your changes, the historical program will not continue to appear.

Let me know if you have any questions.

Regards,

Daniel D.

THANKS Daniel for the quick and informative response. I appreciate explanations - helps me to understand the system better.
OK with your stated actions.
Please provide the 29 programs that have this issue so that we can go back in and select all the relevant programs.
~S

Here is a list of all the programs with the duplicated data, the Id of each program will be in the first column. Please wait until this ticket is deployed to your live site, otherwise the Clear All button will still not work correctly.

Let me know if you have any questions.

Daniel D.

subject: COURSES Requisites missing prefix/subject   CS192
description:
COURSES Requisites page  
When adding a course prerequisite, the subject and requisite course are entered with the data required.  However, when requisites are saved, the subject is missing, and the requisite course is its ID #.  The catalog view is missing the subject but the course number is the correct number (not its ID).
No consistency as to when or why this happens. When it does happen, it usually will take several entries to get it to save correctly.

EXAMPLE:  LAE 4940  (ID 9771)
Add prerequisite course LAE 4335 (ID 9769)
SEE ATTACHMENT FOR DETAILS AND SCREENSHOTS

(208) 522-1225

Hello Shirley,

This is a very odd corner case that came up actually a while ago but we have not fixed it through development yet.

The issue happens when

1. Add new item
2. Search for the subject by typing in the subject code
3. Click the subject code when it comes up
4. Click the correct Course in the next drop down
5. Then when you add a new item to the Ordered List the subject will all ready by selected
  1. Note it only looks selected but it actually is not
6. Then you end up clicking the course for that subject and saving the 2nd item
7. Now if you go back into that last item the subject drop down did not save and since it is chained to the course drop down the course only shows the id

Hello, I am the developer for this ticket.
What I was asking about was, when Group is added to an OL, if there are conditions or parenthesis dropdowns they need to be removed, because them and grouping are mutually exclusive, but we don't want to change the mean of the data of the OL while doing the transfer.
However, there are several Courses which have multiple possible meanings for example "AMH 2020 - History of the United States II" Courseid = 332, which has the following "Prerequisite ENC 0025 and
Prerequisite REA 0017 or
(Prerequisite EAP 1695 ) or
Prerequisite an appropriate score on the college placement test"
The problem with this course's requisites is that it is not clear whether it is "(Prerequisite ENC 0025 and Prerequisite REA 0017) or (Prerequisite EAP 1695 ) or Prerequisite an appropriate score on the college placement test" or if it is "Prerequisite ENC 0025 and (Prerequisite REA 0017 or (Prerequisite EAP 1695 ) or Prerequisite an appropriate score on the college placement test)"  So I was asking if you have some sort of order of operations to let you know which of these it is referring to.

Shirley,

Response from the developer:

Hello, I am the developer for this ticket.
What I was asking about was, when Group is added to an OL (ordered list), if there are conditions or parenthesis drop-downs they need to be removed, because them and grouping are mutually exclusive, but we don't want to change the mean of the data of the OL while doing the transfer.

However, there are several Courses which have multiple possible meanings for example "AMH 2020 - History of the United States II" Course id = 332, which has the following "Prerequisite ENC 0025 and
Prerequisite REA 0017 or (Prerequisite EAP 1695 ) or Prerequisite an appropriate score on the college placement test".

The problem with this course's requisites is that it is not clear whether it is "(Prerequisite ENC 0025 and Prerequisite REA 0017) or (Prerequisite EAP 1695 ) or Prerequisite an appropriate score on the college placement test" or if it is "Prerequisite ENC 0025 and (Prerequisite REA 0017 or (Prerequisite EAP 1695 ) or Prerequisite an appropriate score on the college placement test)", so I was asking if you have some sort of order of operations to let you know which of these it is referring to.

Trent J.

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Hi Trent…



We do use parentheses to separate keywords (a course or program) when using more than one operator. However, we do not have a rule to the order of operations that we use in Meta since Meta does not perform a function with them.  It just lists the keywords, operators, parentheses as marked in the requisite block.



I’m not sure how to explain how we use and decide on placement of the parentheses.  It just all depends on what keywords are grouped together with an AND or an OR.  As you mention in your response, order of operations and placement of the parentheses can make a difference.



As for the example course you used, AMH 2020, it was set up wrong to begin with.  It should be:



![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/3U6lyrI4rpWS1GV8SzYVT7xC8/?name=image004.jpg)



I can go in a fix it, however, I don’t know what other courses that have the Requisites set up wrong (or strange).



I imagine we have quite a few that were set up incorrectly. But many more should be right.  I’m not sure how else to answer your question. I can’t think of a way for on your end and the grouping on how to do it.  Do we need to talk this through?  How can I better answer your question?  I did talk with Natalie this morning about this some so she may have to additional insight for you.



~S

subject: PROPOSAL LANDING PAGE - Notes tab not working
description: The Notes tab on the proposal landing page is no longer working. Please see attached ticket and video for more information. We had 2 other tickets previously on this topic that are also included on the ticket.

comments.body: The Notes tab on the proposal landing page is no longer working. Please see attached ticket and video for more information. We had 2 other tickets previously on this topic that are also included on the ticket.

We did some further testing and found we can't add notes before approving in Technical Review (Level 2) but once approved and moved to Level 3 we can. At Level 2, the Notes doesn't allow us to select a Position Group, it is greyed out. However, at Level 3 it auto-selects Position Group: Admins. Can you please update so we are able to add notes to proposals at any level?

Andrijana,

"**Notes**" tab works off the position on that level with that position that was given rights.

So the reason it works at level 3 is that level 3 has position "**Curriculum Services**", and that position has "**Notes**" permissions. The reason level 2 doesn't work, because position "**Technical Review**" was not given permissions to "**Notes**".

To give level 2 with position "**Technical Review**" permissions to add "**Notes**", you will need to go to Setting Wheel > Administer Site > System Configuration > Position Groups > Edit record "Admins", here you only see the position "**Curriculum Services**". You can add "**Technical Review**", check "**Add Note**", then save.

 ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/X1LeBnbEBLkx08z1VMiqv9QCl/?name=image.png)

If you do the above, then now in the course at level 2 with that position, you should be able to add "**Notes**", for that level.

This is how the notes feature currently works, from what I can tell.

I can create a DST (development support ticket), requesting if we can create some sort of error or a warning that can pop up when trying to add a "**Notes**" and that position/level does not have rights to add a note, so that it will let the client know, the position needs to be added in that "**Position Groups**" to be able to add notes if you like.


Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Andrijana,

To add notes at any level, you would need to add the required position at each level to that **Position Groups** configuration currently to make that work. If the position is in more than one level, that is fine, you only have to add the position once to that **Position Groups** configuration I believe, and it should work on any level that has that position.

I can create a DST requesting that any admin user can add notes at any level, but it is not guaranteed that the company will decide to go down that path or not.

For now, you will have to use it the way it was designed to be able to add notes.

Are you wanting me to create a DST requesting that an admin user can add notes at any level?


Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Hi Mike, 

That is certainly helpful information to have! This wasn't mentioned in our previous tickets regarding the notes tab so we weren't aware there was such a set-up for it, or that we had access to it. I just added additional positions and tested it again - it's now working properly. No need for a DST ticket, now that we know how it works and where to go we can manage it. I'll share this with the rest of the team.

Thanks!

Andrijana,

I also was not aware when I work on your previous support tickets, I just found this out today myself, so I do apologize for that.

Sounds good, I won't create a new DST for the admin being able to add notes at any time, but I did create one requesting for a pop-up letting clients know that the position has not been given permission rights to add notes. I feel that this would be helpful to your college, and all other colleges, that might not know about how this works.

I will go ahead and close this support ticket out.

Happy holidays!


Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Shirley,

The reason is that all course block libraries must have a department/division in order to show in the course block libraries search screen.

The Active Course Block Libraries have a required "Associated Academic Organization" and "College/School/Department" drop-down that has been set to blank.  And therefore they do not show in the Modify Course Block Library's search.

These fields have to be filled in for them to display in the modify course block libraries search page when trying to modify an existing course block library.

"Associated Academic Organization" aka Division.
"College/School/Department" aka Department.

In the Create Proposal screen, you have to select an option for both of these to create new ones.

The division and department normally don't matter where the workflow, New Course Block Library only has one step/level. So when creating new course block libraries, it doesn't matter which option you select.

But for your Modify Course Block Library, you have it using the workflow Program Modification Workflow, which has four step/levels. So based on those positions, it will pull users for those position based off their position mapping to the department for approval.

If the position mapping to department does not matte for those step/levels, then it should be fine, but if it does, then you have to select the correct department for those positions, for that course block library.

If you would like to discuss over the phone, I can give you a call, or you can give our support line to call, and I can try to answer any questions you might have. Just let us know.


Regards,
Mike Gregston

Shirley,

The subject **FOS** was not in the drop-down because it was deactivated, so you created a brand new subject **FOS** that has a different ID than the one that is saved to course **FOS 2201**. Which is fine as far as I know of.

The reason you could only find it in course search page by course number is because that subject that was deactivated no longer displays in the subject filter drop-down, other than the new one you created.

I'm looking into why you can't reactivate course **FOS 2201**.


Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Hi Mike...thanks for info.  I don't necessarily need a reactivation since the course is so old and I don't need to keep the history.  If you can set it up so I can do a new credit course, that would probably be better.

~S

Shirley,

You will have to use the reactivate where you already have an existing course for it.

**Developer Comments:**

- Looks like the original subject code **FOS** was deactivated:
  - This is why the client could not filter by subject code in **Subject** drop-down filter on the course search page.
  - This is why the client could only find the course **FOS 2201** on the course search page by searching for just the course number.
- Client already created a new subject code **FOS**.
- Created a script to update all existing courses that are using the old **FOS** subject code, to now use the new **FOS** subject code, this updated the following courses:
  - **FOS 1999** (2852)
  - **FOS 2201** (2853)
  - **FOS 2999** (2854)
  - **FOS 3999** (2855)
- Now the client will be able to filter by the new subject **FOS** using the **Subject** filter on the course search page.
- Now the client will be able to reactivate the course **FOS 2201** (2853) with no issues.
- ![](https://jira.services.curriqunet.com/secure/thumbnail/110170/_thumb_110170.png)

subject: SYS REPORT Articulation Summary Report Total Units    CS177
description:
SEE ATTACHMENT FOR DETAILS AND SCREENSHOTS
The new system report for Articulation Summary Report (Addl Admin Reports) (see ZenDesk ticket #38153) has wrong total in the Total Credit Column.  It looks like the total is adding the min/max units for each row/course in the Course Blocks instead of using the Total Units in Course Blocks.  There are times when the total credits from the list is going to be overridden and the total credits will be different. And this number of credits should equal Total Units.
Many of the programs on the report look correct, however, those courses/blocks do not have an override to change the total credits.

EX:  CPLITHS-AR (ID 1356) – report shows 5 – 7 credits, when it should be 1 – 3 credits. (see screenshots) 
        PARASW-AR  (id1414) – report shows 50 credits, when it should be 46 credits.

description:
The State is replacing 2 math courses and I’m looking for a report that will give me a list of the active programs that have 1 or both courses in the course block and/or on the pathway.

We need to launch some transfer plans so I’m marking this ticket as urgent.

Hi Andrijana,

I have assigned this ticket to a developer's queue. The developer will be in contact with you if they have any questions.

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Hello,

My name is Mike G. I just wanted to let you know I am starting development on this request. If I have any questions, I will contact you.

Have a great day!

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Andrijana,

You deactivated the existing proposal types and workflows, so any proposal using those types would not have the updates. Where you created brand-new proposal types and workflows the workflow changes would only apply when you create a new program using proposal type **Transfer Plan New,** or if you modify an existing program using the new proposal type **Transfer Plan Modify**.

Looking at proposal type **Transfer Plan Deactivation**, it is not mapped to the new workflow **Transfer Plan Deactivation Workflow**, so I would assume you need to deactivate that proposal type, and then create a brand-new proposal type, and map it to the new workflow **Transfer Plan Deactivation Workflow**.

Any proposals in Draft status using the old proposal type will either need to be deleted and re-created, or we can look into writing up queries to move the Draft proposals over to the new proposal types.

Which way would you like to go?

If any proposals were In Review and you unlaunched them to relaunch them, this would not work where they are using the old proposal type, which means they are using the old workflows. To fix these, again, either delete and recreate, or unlaunch them, so they are back in Draft status, then we write quarries to move them over to the new proposal types and workflows.


Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Hi Mike, thank you for the explanation. Since we only have 3 transfer plans in draft we plan to delete and re-open them again so it will follow the update. 

With that said, I deleted TEST-TR and tried to re-open that New Transfer Plan; Shirley deleted PHARM-TR modification and tried to re-open it and we came across 2 issues. Please see the updated ticket, attached (pgs. 3-5)

Thank you!
Andrijana

Andrijana,

Looks like proposal type **Transfer Plan New** was created wrong. It was created as **Modify**, and it should have been created as **New**. I will write a query to try to fix it.



Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Hi Mike, editing using the pencil to create a new copy or creating a brand new proposal type - neither of these routes allowed me to select Action: New and display the Transfer Plan New & Modification Workflow.

However, if I try to edit the proposal type and select Action: Modify this is the only way the correct workflow shows.

Please see attached screenshots. 

Was there anything else I needed to do on my end when I was editing the workflows?

Andrijana,

- Setting Wheel > Administer Site > Builders > Proposal Types, updated proposal type "**Transfer Plan New**" to have the "**Action**" type "**New**".

- Confirmed, I can now see proposals for proposal type "**Transfer Plan Modify**", and created a "**Transfer Plan Modify**" proposal with no issues.
- ![](https://jira.services.curriqunet.com/secure/thumbnail/107146/_thumb_107146.png)
- Created a "**Transfer Plan New**" proposal with no issues.
- ![](https://jira.services.curriqunet.com/secure/thumbnail/107147/_thumb_107147.png)
- Confirmed, the **Draft** proposals for program transfer plans have been deleted and re-created, so there are no program transfer plan proposals using the old proposal type to move over to the new proposal types for program transfer plans.

Now moving this support ticket to our testing. If no errors, changes will then be applied to the production site.

We will let you know once the changes have been applied to the production site.


Regards,

subject: SPC - Pathway sequence validation
description:
I have run across a program (TRNDEV-ATC #1319) and for the Program Pathway, where the courses are entered and assigned a sequence number, all the courses are there, the total credits at the top is correct, however, when you look below that to see how the Pathway report will look and if there is a sequence # missing or duplicated, it should show in red.  However, it does not.



So, is the sequence validation done up top where the courses are entered and assigned?  If yes, then that could be a problem.  I would think that the validation should be on the information generated below that.



For this program, when you look at the validation info (and on the Pathway report), sequence # 3 is missing, but it is not indicated on here.  When you look at the above, the course was entered EME 4048 but 1 field is not filled out - ‘Course Options Available’.



I corrected this (in production) and added a “No” to this field and all is good now.  If you look in the Sandbox you can see my description above.



Where is the validation managed and what are our options to make sure this doesn’t happen again.

-- This notification was sent from JIRA MS-14328 to this ticket by Trent Jansen. --

I have looked into it and you are correct when "Course Options Available" is not filled out it will not appear in the Program Pathways report and if that item has a "Sequence #" that is missing it will not turn red.  In addition to this if it is set as a duplicate number the Program Pathways report will turn red as if there was a duplicate in the Program Pathways report even though it does not show.

There are a number of ways that this can changed to fix it, but I would like your input of what is the correct behavior before I change anything.  

One way would be for me to let the course show in the Program Pathway report with a blank in the Course Options row when the course does not have a course option assigned.

Another way would for me to add validation to the OL that require the "Course Options Available" textbox to be filled out if a Pathway is chosen.  I could also require the "Sequence #" dropdown to be filled out at the same time.   In this case then "Sequence #" and "Course Options Available" would not be able to launch blank.  They would be required fields.

Lastly, I could change the Program Pathways report to show red or not show red as if those courses that have a blank "Course Options Available" didn't exist.  In this case then Program Pathways report would continue to not show those items but would show the red correctly.

Any of these options would fix this problem, which would be the correct behavior?

-- This notification was sent from JIRA MS-14328 to this ticket by Trent Jansen. --

Mike, Natalie asked me to have you send the client the question before

Shirley,

Response from developer:

I have looked into it, and you are correct when "Course Options Available" is not filled out it will not appear in the Program Pathways report and if that item has a "Sequence #" that is missing it will not turn red. In addition to this, if it is set as a duplicate number, the Program Pathways report will turn red as if there was a duplicate in the Program Pathways report even though it does not show.

There are a number of ways that this can be changed to fix it, but I would like your input of what is the correct behavior before I change anything.

One way would be for me to let the course show in the Program Pathway report with a blank in the Course Options row when the course does not have a course option assigned.

Another way would for me to add validation to the OL (ordered list) that require the "Course Options Available" text box to be filled out if a Pathway is chosen. I could also require the "Sequence #" drop-down to be filled out at the same time. In this case, then "Sequence #" and "Course Options Available" would not be able to launch blank. They would be required fields.

Lastly, I could change the Program Pathways report to show red or not show red, as if those courses that have a blank "Course Options Available" didn't exist. In this case, then Program Pathways report would continue to not show those items, but would show the red correctly.

Any of these options would fix this problem, which would be the correct behavior?

Trent J.


Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Hi Trent...

Thanks for all the info...it's helpful!!  

I would like for both the Sequence # field and the Course Options field to be required.

Are there any other fields that are used for the validation?  If yes, would like those fields required also.

Would still like the validation return to be indicated in red for duplicates and/or missing sequence numbers.

Shirley

Would you check on this ticket for me please?  I don’t know what was done for this.

subject: CATALOG - Programs/Courses not updated
description:
CATALOG

URGENT – must be completed no later than end of day Monday, July 31st.  We publish the catalog on August 1st. 

Please update the following pages in the 23-24 Catalog.  I have already asked at least twice to make sure these pages were up to date.  

It seems that any PROGRAM proposals that have an effective term of Fall 2023 (625) are not being included in the catalog.  Most all these proposals were implemented and made active back in late March/early April time frame. Example:  AMH-ATC, HUMSVC-BS, BIOT-CT, CJPSS-AS, etc.

Also, in COURSES, Fall 2023 looks like it is being included, however, the historical version is also being included.  See for example EEC 1223, EEC 1308, (there are 8 EEC courses like this). Also see COP 1831, ATE 4000.  I’m sure there are probably more like this.

Pages in Catalog to update:
Academic Programs of Study
Programs by Degree Type
Course Descriptions

CATALOG

URGENT – must be completed no later than end of day Monday, July 31st.  We publish the catalog on August 1st. 

Please update the following pages in the 23-24 Catalog.  I have already asked at least twice to make sure these pages were up to date.  

It seems that any PROGRAM proposals that have an effective term of Fall 2023 (625) are not being included in the catalog.  Most all these proposals were implemented and made active back in late March/early April time frame. Example:  AMH-ATC, HUMSVC-BS, BIOT-CT, CJPSS-AS, etc.

Also, in COURSES, Fall 2023 looks like it is being included, however, the historical version is also being included.  See for example EEC 1223, EEC 1308, (there are 8 EEC courses like this). Also see COP 1831, ATE 4000.  I’m sure there are probably more like this.

Pages in Catalog to update:
Academic Programs of Study
Programs by Degree Type
Course Descriptions

Hi Shirley,

I have assigned this ticket to a developer's queue. The developer will be in contact with you if they have any questions.

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Shirley,

Note, programs do not automatically get pulled into the catalog, they never have, you have to manually update them as needed in your catalog.

Yes, courses are included based off their semester and the catalog start and end date. If their dates cross over each other, then both do display where they both were considered active during that catalog t time frame. Once this ticket comes up in developer's queue, they can give more details of the courses and why they are displaying in the catalog based off the dates that were used.

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Hello,

My name is Mike G. I just wanted to let you know I am starting development on this request. If I have any questions, I will contact you.

Have a great day!

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Shirley,

For course **EEC 1223** (9487) it has the semester **Fall 202**3, this semester term start date is **08/14/2023**.

The catalog start date is **08/01/2023**, because catalog start date is before the semester term start date, it pulls in the historical course, because it was active during this catalog start date for 13 days.

If you updated the catalog start date to **08/14/2023**, then it would remove the historical courses that were active for those 13 days.

Or, if you want, I can update the semester **Fall 2023** term start date to **08/01/2023**j, so it matches the catalog start date.

Which way would you like to go?


Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Shirley,

I also checked with courses EEC 1308, COP 1831, and ATE 4000, with the date update, it will remove the historical course for these courses from the active catalog as well.

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Shirley,

As for your programs, you have to manually go in and update programs as needed.

Do you need training with your project manager on how to do this?

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

We may change the term begin/end dates to match our dates in PeopleSoft. Or I may just adjust the catalog start/end dates. Not sure which is best way to go.

Thanks!

~S

Is that possible to do? This date pulls on both the Approved Course Outline and All Fields Report.

Andrijana

"_Correct me if I'm wrong but once the effective term is selected on the General Course Cover it auto-fills that field in the Codes and Dates, correct?_".

"_C&I Approval Date - we would prefer this date auto-populates once Curriculum Services moves the proposal out of Level 4 or 5 in the workflow, that way we still have control over it rather than the committee members. Is that possible to do?_".

subject: SPC - Course Blocks footnote/exception
description:
For the program course blocks, when (and why) did the course footnote change? I know there was some discussion a while back about the footnotes because of our API stuff but never heard anything about changing it permanently.  Is Exception Identifier and Exception Text supposed to be the footnote???  This is so different from before and from pathways. Why does it have to be called Exception and not footnote? How is it supposed to work? Allows for multiple identifiers, use one identifier and exception over multiple courses, each has to be unique, etc. etc.

As I was working on a new program’s course blocks, I noticed that the new program “exception” doesn’t seem to be working correctly on the Program of Study report.  See examples below

What am I missing in all of this????

For an existing program, CWPA-AS (1045), for course COP 1000, the footnote looks OK on the Program of Study (218)
 ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/LRNFLn0hrz8DyMr10RiCkEz9K/?name=inlineImage.jpeg)

 ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/Vu9Vfztpni0Rl7PtqVIMSRRbD/?name=inlineImage.jpeg)


However, for a test program, TEST-AS (1304), it is not looking correct on the Program of Study.  I refreshed the page (several times) and then run the POS report and the footnote identifier is missing.

 ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/w0qJAOHvvSqUvl4kRnmDRkmpY/?name=inlineImage.jpeg)     ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/umZAStFF1HHtkk1qbkaz8LbY7/?name=inlineImage.jpeg)

Hello,

My name is Mike G. I just wanted to let you know I am starting development on this request. If I have any questions, I will contact you.

Have a great day!

Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Shirley,

**Developer Comments:**

- "_For the program course blocks, when (and why) did the course footnote change?_":
  - Because in a previous support ticket, it was requested to develop the functionality to have multiple footnotes. Then it was requested to remove that new development back to what it was previously. When the code was updated back to what it used to be, a query was looking at a table that was no longer being used anymore, so it removed the exception identifiers by accident.
- "_Is Exception Identifier and Exception Text supposed to be the footnote?_":
  - Yes.
- "_Why does it have to be called Exception and not footnote?_":
  - It doesn't, it was probably missed when converting the code back to what it used to be and the developer probably didn't realize that the client had requested that the fields "Exception Identifier" be "**Footnote Identifier**" and "**Exception Text**" be "**Footnote**" on the page "**Course Blocks**".
- "_How is it supposed to work?_":
  - How is what supposed to work?

I will start on updating the report **Program of Study** to display the exception identifier like it is supposed too.

Did you want me to update the field display name from "**Exception Identifier**" to "**Footnote Identifier**"?

Did you want me to update the field display name from "**Exception Text**" to "**Footnote**"?

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Thanks for the info Mike!  I did get a little of this from Natalie this morning, but glad you provided more clarification.

For the question, "How is it supposed to work?" is a shorthand version of a conversation with Natalie.  But basically, will we be able to do multiple footnotes (with multiple identifiers) on a specific course...she said yes.  I was just wondering if any limitations or issues with multiples.

Definitely need the footnote identifier noted on the POS next to course.

Yes, please update the field display name (as you indicated in your comments) to use "Footnote" in place of "Exception". Wanted this to be same as how it is under the Program Pathways.  Also, can the Footnote Text box be an RTE box (like on the Pathways page)?

~S

Shirley,

No, you currently do not have multiple identifiers for each course. We had changed it to be that way back in December, but then we were told to change it back due to the state couldn't except it with multiple identifiers, I believe is what I was told.

So, are you saying your college wants to go back to having multiple identifiers again?


Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Mike...

It wasn't the state but our API pull that was having issues with multiple footnotes on a specific course. We have made some changes on our end for our API and the footnotes are no longer an issue. We didn't have many courses with multiples, but those few caused a problem.

We are OK now with allowing ONLY ONE (1) footnote per course in each course blocks. If a course should require more than one, we will work around it.

Is it possible to get a report that would provide program code, course subject & # and the related footnotes where the course had multiples?  Maybe run report from a point before all the footnote changes (maybe last Oct, Nov time frame)? I want to make sure sure of all the multiples from back then and check their programs as of today and make any edits to those courses so that we don't lose that info.

~S

Shirley,

To update the page **Course Blocks**, field **Exception Text**, to be an RTE:

- We would need to update the backing store in the database to a different backing store, where the current one does not allow that much text.
- We would need to move the existing data from the old backing store to the new one.
- We would need to update any report(s) that were using that old backing store to the new backing store, like your Program of Study report.
- Your college would have to update their API to use the new name for the new backing store, to be able to pull the data.


Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

OK...leave the Exception (Footnote) Text box as is...no RTE.  What is the character length of the field? I'll need to know how much room I have to work with...especially for the handful of multiples we have now and any in the future.

~S

Hello Shirley,

I spoke with our developers and they highly recommend not to change a footnote field to a RTE.  This could cause issues with formatted text in the footnote and the reports will not render correctly and if you copy and paste into a RTE, this could cause html tags to appear.

Natalie

Shirley,

I believe it allows up to 1,000 characters, that includes spaces.

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Shirley,

Here is the extract (see attached) of program courses that I could find where they have more than one footnote identifier.

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Thanks, Mike, for the character response and the report.

~S

Shirley,

**Developer Comments:**

- Updated the program report "**Program of Study**":
  - Added the exception identifier back to the display when exists.
  - Confirmed in program **TESTING** (1304), I can now see the exception identifier.
  - ![](https://jira.services.curriqunet.com/secure/thumbnail/103930/_thumb_103930.png)
- Updated the program forms, page "**Course Blocks**":
  - Updated the field display name "**Exception Identifier**" to "**Footnote Identifier**".
  - Updated the field display name "**Exception Text**" to "**Footnote**".
  - ![](https://jira.services.curriqunet.com/secure/thumbnail/103932/_thumb_103932.png)

Now moving this support ticket forward to our testing. If no errors, changes will then be applied to the Production site.

We will let you know once it has been applied to the Production site.


Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Mike Gregston

Andrijana,

Response from developer:

The developer hid the tab for everyone that is not an admin, and set it to read only for admins. We didn't want the tab or the data in the tab to be inaccessible to everyone as possibly of needing it. If this solution doesn't work please let us know, and we can hide it by other means.

If the user cannot see the tab, it also doesn't show up in the report, so to normal users it is completely gone.

Nathan W.

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Hi there, 
So if we as admins copy the Course Outline report link and send it to someone, although textbooks show for us, it won't show on the report for them? When I log out and search a course on the public view I'm still seeing course materials on the Course Outline report. 
Thank you!

Andrijana,

Response from developer:

If you are an admin and have logged onto Curriqunet on that browser (Chrome, Firefox, Edge) and then log out and run a public report then the Authorization token is still valid and so you will see them for a certain amount of time.

If someone hasn't logged in as an admin they won't see them, you could share the public URL and the receiver also won't see them.

A way to duplicate this for you is either clear the cache and rerun the report, or you can open an incognito tab and run the report, or use a browser that you have not used recently to access the public report and the information will no longer be there.  Hopefully this helps with the issue you are seeing

Nathan W.

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

I have assigned this ticket to a developer's queue. The developer will be in contact with you if they have any questions.

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Andrijana,

Response from developer:

In this ticket you ask for 3 of the date under the Approval Dates section to not copy over. There are some configuration details we need to ensure this meets your requirements.

1. Do you just want the 3 to not copy over, as they are all currently copying over as for this section?
2. If you just want those 3 not to copy over and the rest to copy over, we will have to create a little section below the rest of them to store these in, so they will be in a slightly different order, and it will meet that requirement and look similar to the current user interface.
3. If you have a list of the ones you want to copy over and ones not to copy, we can split them all out, so they can do that, just please send us a list of the ones to allow copy and ones to not allow copy.
Nathan W.

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Hello, thank you for the information. Please see my responses to each and let me know if you have any other questions. 

1. Yes those 3 in yellow should not copy over. Currently, all filled-in dates copy over.

2. That is fine to have a different section for the 3 not copying over, but can those be at the top of the Codes & Dates page, please? For now, these will be the most used date fields. Will these sections require headers?

3. 
DO NOT COPY OVER:
- BOT Approval Date
- Dean's Council Approval Date
- Approved by C&I Committee (please change the "and" to "&")

COPY OVER:
- Submitted to SACS
- Approved by SACS
- SACS notified of deletion (please change "deletion" to "closure")
- SACS acknowledged deletion (please change "deletion" to "closure")
- Submitted to FLDOE
- Approved by FLDOE

Thank you!

Hello, this is Martin from Curriqunet. The change being requested is feasible, but I have a question on


> Please only make this change on proposals going forward, proposals that have been completed do not need this new option.


Technically, that can be done, but I would discourage to do so because it increases maintenance in your forms. Let me explain. Currently, you have three forms (aka templates) which has all your fields (e.g. dropdowns, rtes), and those three forms are used by all your proposal types (e.g. Course Administrative Modification, Course Major Modification). The three forms are:

- New Course
- Modify Course
- Course Deactivate
These forms can be versioned. So, "New Course" can have version 1, version 2, etc. Currently, there is only one version for each form. In order to do what is being requested, I'll have to create a new version for all your forms. This mean that proposals moving forward will use the new/latest version, and existing proposals will use the past versions. However, because I versioned the forms, now there are a total of 6 versions, and this increase maintenance over the forms. Versioning the forms are encouraged when there are significant changes to the form. So, is it okay if I can make the change which will appear in proposal moving forward and existing proposals?

Andrijana,

Response from developer:

The three forms are:

- New Course
- Modify Course
- Course Deactivate
These forms can be versioned. So, "New Course" can have version 1, version 2, etc. Currently, there is only one version for each form. In order to do what is being requested, I'll have to create a new version for all your forms. This mean that proposals moving forward will use the new/latest version, and existing proposals will use the past versions. However, because I versioned the forms, now there are a total of 6 versions, and this increase maintenance over the forms. Versioning the forms are encouraged when there are significant changes to the form. So, is it okay if I can make the change which will appear in proposal moving forward and existing proposals?

Martin Q.

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Martin, thank you for the explanation - that certainly makes sense to me. Yes, please make the change to appear in proposals moving forward and existing proposals. I appreciate your assistance!

Kind Regards, 
Andrijana

description:
Can you find a few minutes today to give me a call please? I was going to wait until our call tomorrow, but Angela asked me to reach out today.

I’m not sure exactly what the problem is nor even how to explain it easily, especially in writing.  I’d rather talk/walk you through it first.  Then figure out next steps.

Starting with the report from # 37226 (Historical courses), there are 112 programs listed, and the majority are transfer plans.  When looking at a lot of those transfer plans they have many historical courses.  I don’t know why.

For example…FTLIT-TR (id 1145), in the course block under the AA Gen Ed block, you will see many historical versions of the courses.  And, when you open the Program of Study report and click on the course link, the course description is from the historical version of the course…not the most current/active version.  I followed POS 2041 and this historical version is 2 proposal ago
 ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/8kaVueV7wlrHk1f3lEv3VMi8y/?name=inlineImage.png)

I looked in V2 at the same program (id 1389) at the course blocks, but can’t tell there which version of the course it is using.
 ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/yxWTiVxvHKbmFlWpLGYwFCqpf/?name=inlineImage.png)

So I looked at the Look Up Data table for General Education Requirements for the Social Sciences

 ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/8BVgPMrGSA30w2paF6ch7TzOl/?name=inlineImage.png)
 ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/BHiXd2XaPdQY9M62hPmZBOQ2F/?name=inlineImage.png)

When selecting the courses button, it doesn’t see like I can edit (which is probably now correct since V2 is basically inactive).
When you look at the Program of Study, the courses no longer have links, so can’t check there either for the correct course version.

**So, the question is, how do we fix all the historical courses in the course blocks in Meta?  We have about 89 TRs and all will need to be reviewed.** Granted, the caveat is that some of the courses may truly be historical and there is not active version.  I haven’t had a chance to really dig into this, but also wanted to get with you before spending too much time before we know what is going on.

Check the Course Block library, that might be the issue. AA Gen ED requirements.

(208) 522-1225

Shirley,

Looking at the program forms, it looks like it just displays the correct **Implement Date** that is saved when the last action is taken in the workflow for field **Implementation/Approved Date** on page **Codes and Dates**.

Looking at the course forms, it looks like field **Implement Date** on page **Codes and Dates** is displaying a different backing store field then the normal course implement on date.

I would suggest we replace this field with the actual course implement on date, so that the date that is saved in the last action being taken in workflow will display here, just like how programs does it. There is no point saving the implement on date in two different backing store locations in database that I can think of.

Are you okay with this or did your college purposely request that the course field **Implement Date** be saved to a different backing store?


Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Shirley,

**Developer Comments:**

- Updated all course forms, page **Codes and Dates**, field **Implement Date**, updated field to pull from the correct implement date backing store from database.

Shirley,

As far as I know of we are not able to change any colors in the comparison report at this time.

The comparison works by comparing line by line, it's not able to compare an item in a proposal that has a different sort order in another version of a proposal. Each item as to line up in both versions of the proposals for the data to compare the same, if they do not have the same amount of records, or if data is in a different order, then they will compare differently.

description:

COURSES – Codes & Dates tab and Approved Course Outline Report (id 50) and Comparison COR Report (id 245) are missing the Related Programs.

comments.body:
Additional details and screenshots on attachment.

COURSES – Codes & Dates tab and Approved Course Outline Report (id 50) and Comparison COR Report (id 245) are missing the Related Programs.

DEP 2004 (CS Approved status – id 9495) – COR report missing related programs that course sits on; Codes & Dates tab > Related Programs field is empty. Active version (id 1971) is correct.

EME 4048 (CS Approved status new course id 9517) looks correct.

DEH 2804L (CS Approved status Maj Mod id 9196) is missing Related Programs

Need to review all courses in the Approval Workflows

Shirley,

The reason child courses that are still in the workflow do not have **Related Programs**, is because the course is not mapped to any programs yet.

Only the **Active** course are mapped to programs.

Once the child course becomes **Active**, then the auto update runs which updates all programs that are not **Historical**, where parent course id exist to the child course id that just became **Active**.

Then when you run the **Comparison - COR**, it will then display **Related Programs** in the child course that just became **Active**.


Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225
Please change this that the Related Programs shows no matter what the status of the course is at.
COR the Related Programs should show Active, Draft and In Review. Comparison would ahow all the Related Programs including Historical. Impact should also show everything.

Hi Shirley,

I have assigned this ticket to a developer's queue. The developer will be in contact with you if they have any questions.

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Additional information:

Comparison - COR report - Include all statuses
Approved Course Outline report - include all statuses except historical
Approved Course Outline report - include all programs except TRs and ARs
Course Impact report (419) - includes all statuses and all programs (include TRs & ARs)

Hi Mike, 
I noticed something off on the Admin Approvals side since putting this ticket in, is this at all related to the issue in testing for Erika's account? She is on the yellow group. If you could check on this soon, that would be ideal, as I am actively working on moving courses around. 

When I go to Approvals > All Approvals > Filter Position by C&I Group I'm not seeing all the courses that have been assigned to that group, like I had before. Some C&I color groups only show a few courses, this is not correct as these have NOT been moved out of Level 4 and at the status C&I Approved. 

When I was tracking reviews of these courses before, I would filter the same way and it would show me everything assigned only to that group, so this is a new issue I'm running into. 

EXAMPLES: (also see attached screenshots)
C&I Red Group shows 2 courses when there are 7 more courses that have not been moved from this level yet.

C&I Yellow Group shows all courses, it should only show 9. All courses that appear in this filter also show as if they were assigned to Yellow Team, which they were not. i.e., DEP 2004 is assigned to Red Team. 

Please be aware CJE 2940 was assigned to "All" so it should be on every group and has not been moved out of C&I Review. 

Thank you!
Andrijana

Andrijana,

You can always try moving the course back a level and then move if forward a level to see if it fixes the positions for that level.

Any time you update a user position, it will update any proposal that is on that group level, and when updating position, it does not follow the configuration for **Conditional Step Configuration** that was set up. That is how you're getting the extra users per proposal. The **Conditional Step Configuration** is only applied when proposal moves to that level in workflow and the position is already in place for that user.

Any time you update a user position, you will have to either move proposal back and forward if proposal is already on that group level, or you have to wait for us to run scripts like previous support ticket and this current support ticket. The current support ticket is in queue to be applied to Production site, but I believe it has not been ran yet.

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Andrijana,

This support ticket was just applied to the Production site.

Please confirm that approvals are looking correctly on the Production site.


Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Thank you for the explanation and script applied to this user account. We finally had some courses go back to C&I teams for a second review and I was able to verify Erika's account now properly represents what it supposed to be in her queue. This ticket can be marked as completed. Thanks again!!

-Andrijana

After reviewing the workflows more, we are still running into some issues.


1. Disapprove will not stop the workflow-. Use like a vote action and have it move to the next level.
  2. C&I Reviewer – If no one is checked – No one gets the proposals. They will assign this in on the 2nd step in the workflow –  Color group is NULL until it assigned. Currently appears and should have been resolved last week that all color grpups are assigned if nothing is selected.
 3. There could be times that they will  assign the groups at level 3. Can we do this without having to go back to level 2 and

This is what we are aiming for.

 Example with Color Group Blue...
If C&I Group is NULL
     Then all Color groups are NOT included AND NOT required.
Else If C&I Group is ALL
     Then ALL Color groups are included and ALL required.
Else If C&I Group is Blue
     Then ONLY Blue group is included AND ONLY Blue group required.
Else If C&I Group is Red, Green, Yellow
     Then ALL color groups are NOT included AND NOT required.

•	Color Group is NULL until assigned by Admin at Level 3 of New Credit Course and Course Major Modification approval workflows.
•	Email notification to the color group is sent at the time the color group (All, Blue, Red, Green, Yellow) is assigned.
•	Typically, only one color group is assigned, however, need capability to assign to either another group or to multiple groups anywhere in the workflow.
•	Admin should be able to move proposal at any time.
•	C&I Group members and Chair should be able to vote and make comments – approve/disapprove, however, no action is taken – does not move, does not stop flow.  If one member votes, should not stop process – all members are able to vote. Admin will take action to move at any time.

- Looking on the **Approval Workflows** page I see a workflow process called **Course New Workflow** (22), which is using process version (113).

subject: SPC - Approval Workflows
description:
Hi Natalie…
**HIGH IMPORTANCE**

We are having some issues with the Courses Approval  Workflows. We have a C&I Committee meeting next Friday to the members in their part of this process and we are running into snags. Please review the info below ASAP.  **Would like fixed no later then end of day Wednesday.** We will need Thursday to do color group assignments and make any other adjustments.

Angela is available for a phone call this afternoon until 5:00 (or Monday morning is your office is working on Monday) my time for any questions or need clarity on the info below.



Approval Workflows

11 Courses have been approved at level 2 Tech Review and are in Level 3 and waiting C&I Color group assignment. However, only 2 courses have been assigned a color group.
 ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/VpF25Ztm4Pjf6nVb0mGvABsdt/?name=inlineImage.jpeg)

ATE 2612 has been assigned the Blue group
 ![](https://governet.zendesk.com/attachments/token/z8wTdjOnYiMdUSeALx5m0ZYQj/?name=inlineImage.png)

PHI 2642 is assigned to the Green Group



Logged in as a C&I member of the Red Group
candi@spcollege.edu
changeme1

All the courses sitting at the level above is showing the Position of C&I Red Group.  Why is this?
Shouldn’t this be blank until a color group has actually been assigned?

The 2 courses that have a different color group (noted above) are showing as Red Group.  Why is this?

The C&I Committee member should only see courses assigned to their specific group (unless course set to all).  And they should not see them in Approvals until that color has been assigned to the course.

So for the user above, Candi should not be seeing any courses/approvals as none are assigned to her/red.








**Shirley Boyette**
_Research/Curriculum Analyst_
Curriculum Services | EPI Tech
727.341.3041 | boyette.shirley@spcollege.edu

subject: SPC - Building Pathways
description:
When we are building the Pathways and say the course we need is in DRAFT, will it automatically keep the course in the pathway as its status changes throughout the workflow and then turn ACTIVE?

I have to check into this more, but it seems like if we choose DRAFT, the sequence row looks good.  Then, if the status changes next to IN-REVIEW, the sequence row now is blank…still there but blank.

Andrijana is working on CPJSS-AS (id 1273) and in the Course Blocks yesterday, she chose CJJ 2358 (id 9453) DRAFT, and today in the CB it is now In-Review. So that seems OK.  However, on the Pathway, when adding the DRAFT course yesterday, the sequence looked ok, but today, since the course is now IN-REVIEW, the sequence row is blank and the course # is also blank.
Did the above make sense?  I still need to do some digging on my end for more examples.  However, thought this might give enough info to get someone to look into it.  We do not want to have to go through all the Pathways (or even Course Blocks) each time the status changes.  The course status that was originally selected (e.g. draft) should stay within the PW and CB as it goes through the workflow.

Side note:  I sent you an email similar to this on 12/8 @12:21pm re: Course Blocks and active/draft version of course.

Please let me know something ASAP!

subject: SPC - V2 New User Access Issue
description:
Our new C&I users, Erica Moulton and Paul Cutlip, are experiencing issues with login credentials in V2. I don’t have any information on submitting a Zendesk ticket so I figured I would email you directly. We are on a time crunch, as C&I is currently reviewing courses and the deadline is approaching. Would someone please be able to look into this by noon EST tomorrow?

Both new C&I members were added to the V2 system on 5/25/22, they were both able to login, access pages, and change their ‘temporary assigned’ passwords, as instructed. It seems something occurred after they changed their passwords, as both members were then unable to login with their updated password. I tried resetting the password, they cleared cache in multiple browsers, and nothing worked.

Erica first had the issue on 6/16 and even with a password reset she and Shirley could not login to her account, but I could. I deleted her from the system completely and then re-added her with the username now erica.moulton instead of the typical lastname.firstname naming convention. The work-around worked for her and she was able to login again. Now Paul is having the same problem and I prefer someone investigate what may be causing this and if there is another solution.

We also have a new Dean, I made him an account today and worry he will experience the same; Hernandez – Agosto, Emmanuel (Hernandez.Emmanuel)

Please let me know if you need any further information.

Three of us are showing the URL as http://www.curricunet.com/stpetersburg/index.cfm (http://www.curricunet.com/stpetersburg/index.cfm) ; whereas Angela has the secured URL of https://www.curricunet.com/stpetersburg/index.cfm. build/programs/p_builder/add_optional_courses.cfm

**Developer Comments:**

- Tested with program "**Health Sciences: General Health Sciences Focus**" (1277).
- Page "**Course Blocks**".
- Click on "**Courses**" for the 9th program course block "**SUPPORT COURSES: Lecture & Lab Option A Complete 12 credits**", got a error.
- This error is due to this block being set to have override calculations by having "**Credits (Specify Credit Range)**" selected and then user did not input a value into the max text box for the block.
- Added logic, for when you have override calculations selected, "**Credits (Specify Credit Range)**", if user leaves the min and/or max text box blank, the value would be pulled in as a "**0**" into the calculations to prevent the page from throwing a error when clicking on the "**Courses**" button that allows the user to manage courses in that block.
- Confirmed the "**Courses**" button page no longer throws a error on the block above.

No queries for LIVE.

comments.body: When creating a new proposal process I am unable to select a proposal type (drop down is empty) and receive an error upon saving because it is blank.

Hi Angela,

I have assigned this ticket to a developer's queue. The developer will be in contact with you if they have any questions.

Nothing appears to be broken. To add a new Process, the client must first add a new Proposal Type.  Natalie will discuss with the client if a new Proposal Type is needed or if this should be a modification of an existing Proposal Type.  The main concern here is that they will be moving to META in less than 3 months and they are planning to have no proposals In Review at that time.
Mike talked them through this.

When I go to add him as a user it gives me an error stating his username is already taken. I believe I ran into this issue last time as well. Could you please check on this? Andrijana,

It gives you a error because a user already has this as a user name in their account and that account was deactivated, so that is why you can not see it. They V2 system did not allow for duplication even though the account was deactivated.

The user Thomas Cooper had this user name cooper.scott along with the email Cooper.Scott@spcollege.edu.

Do you want me to reactivate this account?

Reactivating account will then let you see the account.

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225
If client does want user re-activated, run attached query on live site.

subject: SPC - Changes in V2
description:
Hi Natalie…new ticket…

**CS014-02 01 2021  :  Look Up Table – Primary Instruction Mode**

Primary Instruction Mode Look up table
Delete 5 instances of Distance…
Add 2 new options of F – Flex…and Z – SPC Live…
(Shirley did make edits to the table in the Sandbox, but at refresh on 2/1, no longer appears.  Changes to table will need to be made by Cunet on their side to be permanent.)
The table “Primary Instruction Mode” is found under Courses > General Course Cover tab > Primary Instruction Mode (drop down list).  Is it anywhere else?
Also, this same list is hardcoded in Programs > Program Information tab > How will program courses be offered.  This list needs to match the table.

We need to make these changes in v2 so when we pull the data for the migration, it comes over correct.

subject: ad hoc report
description: I have run an ad hoc report pulling all active courses and it is showing courses multiple times that should only have one record in the system. Sometimes the course shows multiple times with the same information - those instances are not as worrisome, but then there are some that show multiple records (different CurricUNET ID #s) although a search in the system only reflects one record (e.g. ETE 3003, ETE 4444). On the attached excel file I have highlighted a few of the records of concern.
