subject: FireFox users reporting issues opening dynamically generated PDF files
description: Some (not all) FireFox users are being prompted to download a .cfm file rather than having the PDF displayed when trying to view program descriptions. For example: http://curricunet.com/stpetersburg/reports/program_report.cfm?programs_id=480

comments.body: Some (not all) FireFox users are being prompted to download a .cfm file rather than having the PDF displayed when trying to view program descriptions. For example: http://curricunet.com/stpetersburg/reports/program_report.cfm?programs_id=480

Bill,

Can you tell me what Operating System they are using along with which version of Firefox they are using. I am using Windows 10 with Firefox version 55.0.3 and I am not having this issue.

Thanks,
Wesley L

It’s pretty random. Windows 7 – 10. The lowest version of FF from 53 up.

 

Bill

 

“The function of education is to teach one to think intensively and to think critically. Intelligence plus character – that is the goal of true education.” – Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr.

Bill,

This may be a setting in the browser. Have the users experiencing this issue check and see. The URL explaining how to check this is https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/change-firefox-behavior-when-open-file

Thanks,
Wesley L

It is not reasonable to expect random site visitors to modify their browser settings to view a PDF.

 

Bill

 

“The function of education is to teach one to think intensively and to think critically. Intelligence plus character – that is the goal of true education.” – Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr.

Bill,

Because this is a browser level setting the only way around the issue is to always make the file download. Unless this is done it will do whatever the setting is for displaying PDF documents on the browser.

Thanks,
Wesley L

The only reports we’ve receive are associated with dynamically generated pdf’s from your ColdFusion server. Please google “coldfusion dynamically generated pdf firefox” for more information.

 

Bill

 

“The function of education is to teach one to think intensively and to think critically. Intelligence plus character – that is the goal of true education.” – Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr.

I should also point out, we dynamically generate PDF’s on our systems (php and .Net) that are not causing problems for the same users that are reporting issues using your system. So, it’s not a PDF specific thing. It appears to be specific to your site, which is why I reported it to you.

 

Just so you know -- we are preparing to shut down due to Hurricane Irma. Please be patient if you should need additional follow up.

 

Bill

 

“The function of education is to teach one to think intensively and to think critically. Intelligence plus character – that is the goal of true education.” – Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr.

Bill,

Has this ticket been completed?

Regards,
Mike Gregston
(208) 522-1225

I have not received any further complaints, but I have not tested it recently either. Have you done any testing or made any adjustments?

Thanks,

Bill

http://test.curricunet.com/scripts/webservices/stpetersburg/spc_webservices.cfc?method=get_program_basics

Developer Comments:
- While testing I found a issue with the copy script, it is copying the same origination date for the program and course families every time it is copied.  I updated both program and course copy scripts to enter in the current date when they are copied.
- Looking at program "Computer Programming and Analysis" (programs_id=667) *Pending*, in the "Course Blocks" page, block 5, course "COP 2654", the reason it does not display selected when you click on the edit icon is because the course your seeing is the historical version.  The program is mapped to the course "COP 2654" (courses_id=6915) *Historical*.  The course your wanting mapped to this program is "COP 2654" (courses_id=8059) *Active*.
- Looking at the parent program "Computer Programming and Analysis" (programs_id=551) *Active*, it to was mapped to the "COP 2654" (courses_id=6915) *Historical* before you went in and saved "COP 2654" (courses_id=8059) *Active*.
- For testing on the DEV site, I updated "Computer Programming and Analysis" (programs_id=667) *Pending* to point to the active course "COP 2654" (courses_id=8059) *Active*.  I then copied the course "COP 2654" (courses_id=8059) *Active* and created course "COP 2654" (courses_id=8195).  I moved the course through the approval process and implemented it.  I checked the program "Computer Programming and Analysis" (programs_id=667) and the parent course Id did not auto update to the child course Id.
- I checked the logic that updates this and found out that the reason it did not update is because program "Computer Programming and Analysis" (programs_id=551) effective semester was less then the course "COP 2654" (courses_id=8059) *Active* effective semester.
- In ticket #21070 you requested that logic be added so that historical versions do not get updated.  It looks like you wanted, to have a program be modified every time a course that is attached to that program that is being modified.  This would keep the historical records intact if this procedure is followed.
- So the program effective semester has to be greater than or equal to the course effective semester for the auto update to ac-cure correctly.

Hi Michael,  So I think I followed your explanation, however, it bears more discussion.  So when we requested that historical versions not be updated, that is to say that if a program is in a historical status we would not want it to be updated with new course information (i.e. credit hour changes, title change, etc.).  We have many instances where a course will be updated and have a newer effective date than the program of study.  Very often the prerequisites or MLOs will change which do not impact the program of study but will result in a new effective term that is later than the program of study effective term.  Please feel free to call me to discuss further. I'm in the office tonight until 5:30 EST and tomorrow all day until 4:00 EST.  Thanks, Angela

This will only affect courses that are implemented after this change has been implemented to your LIVE site.  Any current courses that have already been implemented will need to be manually updated.

subject: Course Proposal Report to excel not working
description: The report (#3 on Reports Page) runs correctly for output to the screen, however, if I try to output to excel it does not produce any information.  Excel opens the file but it is blank.  (criteria:  Course Status = pending & submitted; Effective term: Spring 2017 (0525) and Summer (0530).

comments.body: The report (#3 on Reports Page) runs correctly for output to the screen, however, if I try to output to excel it does not produce any information.  Excel opens the file but it is blank.  (criteria:  Course Status = pending & submitted; Effective term: Spring 2017 (0525) and Summer (0530).

Angela,

The cause of this is from a recent Microsoft update to Excel that was originally released in July 2016 (KB3115322). Microsoft released a patch for this on August 9, 2016 that if it is downloaded and installed then the files will open correctly. The correct update that is need is dependent on which version of Microsoft Office you have. If you have Office 2016 then KB3115438, if Office 2013 then KB3115455, if Office 2010 then KB3115476.

If you cannot get the update downloaded then what you will need to do is after the file is downloaded go to your downloads folder (or where you have your browser download to) and right click on the file and select Properties (NOTE: if using firefox or IE then make sure to select Save or Save As instead of open with). Then on the General Tab at the bottom there is a section titled Security with a checkbox labeled Unblock (See attached image). Check this check box and click Apply and then OK. Then you can open the file.

Thanks,
Wesley L
My aplogies, I thought I had closed this.  Your direction was very helpful and worked perfectly.  Thanks so much Wesley.

subject: Adhoc Report output
description: The excel spreadsheet output for the adhoc reports does not seem to be working. It produces a blank excel window without any spreadsheet. We can look up the report results with the html output, but we need the excel output to be able to rearrange the data and email it to customers.

comments.body: The excel spreadsheet output for the adhoc reports does not seem to be working. It produces a blank excel window without any spreadsheet. We can look up the report results with the html output, but we need the excel output to be able to rearrange the data and email it to customers.

Will,

This is most likely caused by a recent security update to Excel. Try going into where you downloaded the file and then right clicking on the file. Then click on Properties. Once in the properties window on the General tab at the bottom there should be a check box that says something about unblock (see attached screen shot). Check that checkbox and click Apply and then OK then try to open the excel document. If it opens then, depending on your institutions settings there may be a way to get around having to do this for every individual file.

If the above works then you can try the fix. You will need to open Excel. Then click on File and then Options. Then click on Trust Center and then if available click on "Trust Center Settings". You will then need to find the option "Protected View" and click on it. Then you will need to uncheck the first box which reads "Enable Protected View for files originating from the Internet" and then click OK twice. You should then be able to open excel documents again. Test it by re-downloading an excel file from CurriCUNET and attempting to open it.

If you do not have access to the Trust Center area then you will need to talk to your IT department and see if they can change the setting for you but there is a possibility that they will not change it due to network security and/or compliance policies. Angela,

Sorry to take so long to get back to you, but I have been swamped with other projects.
As far as I can tell, the web service itself is operating correctly.

I see that you have made changes to the requisites on that STA 2023 course on your production site, but the development site still has an older version of your database with the data as it was in that document you sent.

In St. Petersburg's CurricUNET V2 web service, the only method that returns course requisite information is get_course_requisites.
Calling that method for STA 2023 returned XML that shows the values in the non_course_requirement field you were expecting.
The URL used is: https://securedev.curricunet.com/scripts/webservices/stpetersburg/spc_webservices.cfc?method=get_course_requisites&prefix=STA&course_number=2023
I have attached the current XML results of calling that URL as "spc webservices course req results STA 2023 (dev).xml"
The values there match what is in the UI on your dev site.

I have also attached the results of calling the same web service method on your live site, via the following URL:
https://secure.curricunet.com/scripts/webservices/stpetersburg/spc_webservices.cfc?method=get_course_requisites&prefix=STA&course_number=2023
This one shows changes to the requisites on that course and most of the non_course_requirement fields are empty now (as they are in the UI).
I have attached the current XML results of calling that URL as "spc webservices course req results STA 2023 (live).xml"

As far as I have been able to tell with the examples given, the web service is working properly. If you are still having issues with this, you might want to check into making sure the XML response is being consumed correctly on your end. You can also provide some more examples we can use to verify the correct behavior of the web services on our end, if you find that necessary. :)

I'm going to close this ticket for now, but you will be able to re-open it with a reply in the next few days.

Thanks,
-- Stephen

subject: Possibly erroneous data from web service function GET_PROGRAM_REPORT_DATA
description:
When using the get_program_report_data SOAP function with the arguments to pull the active version of program VETTC-BAS, we appear to be getting erroneous data.  

Below is a snippet of the information returned:

<COURSE_OPTIONS>
       <OPTION_HEADER/>
       <course_block_definition_title>AA GENERAL EDUCATION REQUIREMENTS</course_block_definition_title>
       <course_option_notes>A.A. Degree</course_option_notes>
       <COURSE_BLOCKS>
        <COURSE_BLOCK>
         <TITLE/>
         <COURSE_TITLE/>
         <MIN_CREDIT_HRS>41</MIN_CREDIT_HRS>
         <MAX_CREDIT_HRS>41</MAX_CREDIT_HRS>
         <exception_identifier/>
         <or_higher_exceptions/>
         <program_course_rule/>
         <condition_title>and</condition_title>
        </COURSE_BLOCK>
       </COURSE_BLOCKS>
      </COURSE_OPTIONS>
      <COURSE_OPTIONS>
       <OPTION_HEADER/>
       <course_block_definition_title>ADMISSIONS COURSES</course_block_definition_title>
       <course_option_notes>Lower Division Veterinary Technology (by transfer)</course_option_notes>
       <COURSE_BLOCKS>
        <COURSE_BLOCK>
         <TITLE/>
         <COURSE_TITLE/>
         <MIN_CREDIT_HRS>41</MIN_CREDIT_HRS>
         <MAX_CREDIT_HRS>41</MAX_CREDIT_HRS>
         <exception_identifier/>
         <or_higher_exceptions/>
         <program_course_rule/>
         <condition_title>and</condition_title>
        </COURSE_BLOCK>
       </COURSE_BLOCKS>
      </COURSE_OPTIONS>

According to your system, the values for "MIN_CREDIT_HRS" and "MAX_CREDIT_HRS" under the "AA GENERAL EDUCATION REQUIREMENTS" block should be 37 for each (see screen-shot, attached).  The value of 41 is correct for the same values in the  "ADMISSIONS COURSES" block.

We have not been able to determine anything on our end that might cause this, since the values you see returned in the snippet above are coming directly from the CurricUNet system.  However, if we are missing something, please let me know.

Thanks,
Wesley L

Bill,

Looking at your web service, it is not setup to return those block-level override fields.
The credits returned there are from a nameless non-course requirement added to that block.

The code on your Course Blocks page hides the "Courses" button when the value selected for the "Course Block Title" drop-down is 6 (AA GENERAL EDUCATION REQUIREMENTS) or 7 (AS GENERAL EDUCATION REQUIREMENTS). So it looks like someone set that "Course Block Title" to something else, added that course, then changed it back.

If you do that (change it to something like "Transfer Specific AA Plan"), then you can click on the Courses button and edit that course entry to change the number of credits. If you do so, you should see that unnamed entry is currently set to 41 credits. Once you have fixed the credits in that entry, you can then change the "Course Block Title" for that block back to what it was.

The Admissions Courses block has the same sort of thing, an unnamed non-course requirement with credits set to 41.

I hope this helps explain this behavior.

Thanks,

-- Stephen Tigner
Governet Developer

Yes, it does. Thank you.

 

Bill

Thanks,

-- Stephen

Pages Updated:
track/check_all_proposals.cfm

Developer Comments:
- Track > All Proposals, I have added the step title under the section "Comments" for courses and programs section.
- The course "HSC 3243" (courses_id=7948) has been submitted into the approval process.  The Originator just has not gone to the "My Approvals" page and taken action yet.  This is why the action and comments are not displaying for this proposal yet.  Proposals only display on the "All Proposals" page if they have been submitted into the approval process.  If your trying to make this course disappear then you need to click on "Remove Proposal", this will remove the proposal from the approval process and the originator will need to submit it back in again when they are ready, and after they submit it back in they need to remember they need to go to the "My Approvals" page like is says when they first submit the proposal and take action on level 1.
- Submit a proposal and taking action are two different things.  Submitting a proposal is when the originator submits the proposal into the approval process.  When the proposal displays in the "All Proposals" page, this means the proposal has been submitted.  When the originator takes action on the first level, that means the originator has taken action on the first level.  When the originator submits the course into the approval process, it saves a launch date.  The launch date does not come from when the originator takes action on the level 1.  So the submit date is not null, there for there is no need for a extra filter looking for null for launch date.  There is always a launch date.

Angela,

Your "Course Search Result" page pulls all courses, no matter if they are in the approval process or have completed the approval process or have not even been submitted into the approval process.

Your "All Proposals" page only pulls courses that have been submitted into the approval process and have not been completed in the approval process yet.

If you want to see proposal that have completed the approval process you would go to the "Completed Proposals" page.

Looking at your first course in your image "BSC 2085" (courses_id=7526) it has not been submitted into the approval process yet, there for will not display on the "All Proposals" page.  It should be displaying on the admin "Course Proposal Report".  I am looking into this, most likely I will have to modify one of the queries.

Regards,
Michael Gregston

Developer Comments:
- Course "ATE 1211" (courses_id=7752) does not display on the Track > My Approvals, page because this course has completed the approval process and will no longer display in the approval process page.  This course would now display in the Track > Completed Proposals, page.
- The reason this proposal status did not update was because the flag in the database that is set to false until the implement date comes to past was set to true, so when the implement date came to past it did not update.  Looks like the reason the flag was set to true was because this course was activated on "10/06/2015" and then on "12/03/2015", someone manually changed it to "Approved".  The reason it activated so early was because on the last step you took the action "Implement".  This action activates the proposal that day.  You should of took the action "Approved for Later Implementation", this changes the status to "Approved" and then does not go "Active" until the future implement date comes to past.
- I created a query to update the implement flag back to false on the course "ATE 1211" (courses_id=7752) and I ran this query on your LIVE site.  Tonight when the nightly script is ran it will auto implement the course and change the status to "Active and the parent course will go to "Historical".
- Course "DEH 2702C" (courses_id=7511) does not display on the Track > My Approvals, page because this course has completed the approval process and will no longer display in the approval process page.  This course would now display in the Track > Completed Proposals, page.
- The reason this proposal status did not update was because the flag in the database that is set to false until the implement date comes to past was set to true, so when the implement date came to past it did not update.  Looks like the reason the flag was set to true was because this course was activated on "02/09/2015" and then on "02/12/2015", someone manually changed it to "Pending" and then on "08/05/2015" someone manually changed it to "Submitted".
- I created a query to update the implement flag back to false on the course "DEH 2702C" (courses_id=7511) and I ran this query on your LIVE site. 1) if we manually change the status, will this continue to mess up the flag for moving the course to Active?  How do we make sure this doesn't happen again, as there are definitely situations where we need to move things manually.  2) on the Track>Completed Proposals there is a date appearing (i.e. DEH 2702C = 9/23/2014) where does this come from?  I wasn't able to see it on the proposal.  The submit date is 9/26/2014 - Is this the date the proposal was originally created? I'm thinking this should reflect the implementation date given the filter is for completed proposals.  3) Is it correct to have the status set to "Approve" when the Action is "Approved for Later Implementation"?

Thanks so much, Michael.

Angela,

- Manually changing the status should not mess up the flag in the database.  What it will mess up is where the users think the proposal is at or not at in the approval process.  For this to not happen again, the super admin user that manually changes the status needs to keep a close eye on the proposal to make sure the proposal get set back to the status it is supposed to be.
- Looks like the date on the "Completed Proposals" page is the launch date when the originator launched the proposal into the approval process.  It looks like you have to different launch dates for course proposals.  You have the launch date when the originator first finishes all the pages and launches, and the the originator goes into the "My Approvals" page and takes action on level 1.  The date you see on the "Completed Proposals" page is the first submit.  The user for "DEH 2702C" originally took action on 9/23/2014, but then did not go into the "My Approvals" page and take action on level 1 until "9/26/2014".  Some colleges have it set up where level 1 action is taken when the originator first submits the proposal, but someone at your college did not want this, they wanted to be able to submit the proposal into the approval process but not actually take action on level 1 until the originator goes into the "My Approvals" page and take action on level 1.
- Yes, the status "Approved" is correct for when the action "Approved for Later Implementation", because you do not want the course to have the status of Active before the Implementation date and you do not want the parent proposal to go Historical until it's child course goes Active.

Regards,
Michael Gregston
(208) 522-1225
Thank you.

description: Please see attached file for details. Basically, we are getting conflicting reports with the "Proposal Impact Report" function for the same course whether it is an active course or a proposal.

Will,

Yes, your "Course Impact Report" is based off of the status, if the current course is Active and does not have a child then the CIR report will display the courses the current course is a requisite in.

If the current course does have a child course with the status of Pending then the courses the parent course was in will display on the child course because when the child course goes active all the courses the parent course was a requisite in will update to the new child course that goes active.

If you want we can break the section "Courses" up into two different sections, one to display the parent (courses the parent is a requisite to), and one to display the child (requisites the child will be in once it goes active).

If you do want this?  What titles do you want to use for the two different sections?

Regards,
Michael Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Hello Michael,

I understand how it works now. As long as we can see the course impact report on courses that have gone active we should be fine.

Thanks,

Will

subject: Update Screen in Admin Tasks
description: We've documented the details below, but essentially we are looking for a function under Admin Tasks to assign "approval dates" to a batch of course outlines in one sweep without having to edit nearly a hundred of them individually.

comments.body: We've documented the details below, but essentially we are looking for a function under Admin Tasks to assign "approval dates" to a batch of course outlines in one sweep without having to edit nearly a hundred of them individually.

Hi Will,

I have assigned this ticket to a developer's queue. The developer will be in contact with you once they begin working on this ticket and if they have any questions.

Thanks,
Wesley L

Hello,

My name is Michael Gregston. I just wanted to let you know I am starting development on this request. If I have any questions, I will contact you.

Have a great day!

Regards,
Michael Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Will,

- We can create a new page under the "Look-Up Data".
- In this new page it will have filters where you will select a subject (drop down that lets you select one subject code), date type (drop down that lets you select one date field form the "Codes & Dates" page), effective term (drop down that lest you select one option that displays in the field "First Term" from the "General Course Cover" page, and then a multi select window witch will display all active and historical courses that match the subject you selected in the first field, they will display in a list and each course will have a check box.  You will select your values for the filters above and then check all the courses you want to update and it will do a massive update to all the courses that are selected.
- When you select to update all courses we can generate a report in excel showing all the changes that you just did, but we will not be able to save it, so if you do not save the report when it is first generated then there will be no way to get that report again if you accidentally close the report with out saving first.
- Also we do not show changes of fields unless you make a modify to a proposal.  There is no way we can save the old date and the new date on the same course, so we are not able to add the old date and new date to the "Course Outline" report in the section "History of Changes", unless it showing the date from the parent and the date from the child proposals.

Regards,
Michael Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Angela,

As we discussed on phone I will:

- Call new look up page "Manage Key Dates".
- Pull only courses that have the status of "Active".
- We will have a drop down to filter by "First Term".
- We will have a drop down for Date type, where the user will select which date they want to update for all selected courses below.  Will have a calendar icon for user to be able to select date.  The three options will be "C & I Approve, BOT Approval, Peoplesoft Effective Date".
- Add a check all options for the multi select.
- Also include a output in Excel report of the changes just made (one time extract).
- Check course outline that it is display the dates correctly in section "History of Changes".

Regards,
Michael Gregston
(208) 522-1225

1 to many fields in ad hoc I warned them about this.

Angela,

The are not duplicating. If you add field "CURRICUNET_ID" you will see all three courses "ETE 3473C" are different courses.  They have different ID's,  when you pull this much data into a report your going to have course that look like others but there not, they have something different in one of the fields that you are not displaying.  If you look in your Build > Courses > Course Prefix /Subject Area drop down you will see that you have three different "ETE" and each one has the same named course.

Does this resolve your issue?

Regards,
Michael Gregston

Hi Michael,  thanks for that response and it actually brings up another issue.  There should only be one instance of a prefix - in this case ETE should only appear one time in our prefix table.  I did come across this on another prefix -  CET - and removed the incorrect one.  In this case, since there are course records tied to two other prefixes, I believe that I need to update the prefix on the course to the correct prefix and then inactivate the incorrect prefix through Look Up Data .  Correct?  

Also, could you please add the field "Discipline Reference Field" to the Course Data table?  I need to make all unused prefixes inactive and this field indicates whether they are active. Thanks!

"    Hi Michael, thanks for that response and it actually brings up another issue. There should only be one instance of a prefix - in this case ETE should only appear one time in our prefix table. description:
A 510 proposal for WEBSDM-AS was initially duplicating. I pushed one of the proposals through to an "approved" status, which you can navigate to via the following path: Track > Completed Proposals > WEBSDM-AS (2/21/2015)

I then tried to push the other duplicate proposal through as well, which caused the "approved" proposal to disappear from search results when you  do a program search for WEBSDM-AS.  Also, this incomplete proposal is the only proposal that shows up when navigating this path: Track > All Proposals > WEBSDM-AS. 

Could you please

Thanks so much for your assistance. If you require additional information, please feel free to contact me by email (shaw.karen@spcollege.edu) or phone (727) 302-6482.

Karen,

You should never push duplicated proposals through the approval process.  Only one should go through and the other one should be removed if it is a duplicate.  Duplicate is two proposals that both point to the same course or program.

To remove the unwanted proposal go to Track > All Proposals > Remove Proposal (in the URL you should see "proposals_id=948" after you select the button "Remove Proposal") > Remove Proposal Continue, now the duplicated proposal has been removed.

To fix the status of the program "WEBSDM-AS: Web Development" (programs_id = 500), go to Admin > Look-up Data > Activate Course/Subject > Change Program Status > Find program "Web Development WEBSDM-AS 510 *Pending", select it, change the status to "Approved" > Change Status, now the program "WEBSDM-AS: Web Development" (programs_id = 500), displays as "Approved" on the page "My Programs".

On my LOCAL site, I deleted the program "WEBSDM-AS: Web Development" (programs_id = 500).  I then copied the Active version, program "WEBSDM-AS: Web Development" (programs_id = 459).  I then submitted the program "WEBSDM-AS: Web Development" (programs_id = 516) into the approval process.  It only submitted once.  After the user submits the proposal it then displays some text and a link to "My Approvals" page.  To the left the "Submit" button still displays.  I click the "Submit" button again and it gives me the error "Web Development Mike's Test Proposal already exists.".  This means this error check for double submit is working correctly.  If one of your users is doing something different, I will need to know the exact details of the way the user is doing it to narrow down a the possible issue that I can not duplicate at this time.

Does this help solve this ticket?

Regards,
Michael Gregston
(208) 522-1225

Hello Michael,

Yes, that resolved the issue. Thank you!

Additionally, do I need to submit a ticket when I just have a question? Just in case not, here’s a quick question that came up yesterday:

We have a few proposals that are either in a submitted or pending status for Spring (510). We just discovered a problem with a couple of the programs and so actually now need to do Fall 2015 (505) proposals for these programs. Given that the 510 proposals already exist, will creating 505 proposals negatively impact the system in any way (a colleague was thinking parent-child relationships might be messed up). Could you please advise?

Thanks for any insight you can provide. I appreciate your assistance.

Best regards,

Karen

Karen L. Shaw

Research Specialist

Curriculum Services Office

Shaw.Karen@spcollege.edu

(727) 302-6482

Helping you make efficient and effective curriculum changes that best serve our students!

Karen,

You can submit a ticket for a question, but you would probably get a quicker answer if you called.

As long as the proposal has not been approved or completed, you should be OK to change the field "First Term" on the page "General Course Cover".  If the proposal has been submitted and the status is not pending, I believe only super admin users will be able to unlock the page and change the field.

I believe this completes this ticket.

subject: New report:  Curriculum Course Updates by Term
description:
Hi Travis/Michael, I know it has been some time since we discussed this new report but please see the attached documentation for identification of the fields needed for this report. Michael had provided me with a sample report (from Hawkeye Community College?) which appeared to be a good solution for what we are in search of; a report that combines the information and formatting of the "outline comparison report" (deletions in red/ additions in green) and the All Fields Report.  In the sample, I kept the static length fields at the top and the three variable length fields (rationale, description and prerequisites) at the bottom to minimize the alignment issues we had with previous attempts.  We will be uploading curriculum updates starting next week so the sooner the better for completion (hence the "High" priority) for us.

Ideally I would like to be able to add this report to Home>Admin>Reports and run it with a selection criteria by term.  i.e. each course that changed effective in term "X" would be 1 page; the report would sort by prefix/number and would be produced in batch.  I don't want to have to run a report for each course but rather have one large output by term.  I would also add more fields if there is room for them; this was just an initial pass through with the minimum needed.   Please call me to discuss further if needed.  Thank you.

subject: Existing Proposal status
description: Hi Travis, this might be more of a question than something to fix.  I had to update some PRO courses to reflect a change in the College/School/Department (changed from College of Health Sciences to Health Science Department).  On course PRO 4850 it has "Existing proposal" noted on left column although it is not a current proposal.  I verified that it is in the "Completed Proposals" list; it is also in an active status and has an implementation date/step completed.  I don't believe this notation should be there; it is confusing.  I also logged in as Dr. Nicotera and did NOT see the "Existing Proposal" message (which is good). The update I made for the college/School/Department should have also changed the Originator from Ludwig to Nicotera . . . or is this not done until a new proposal is created?  (Dr. Nicotera does have a copy button available to him for the course.)  Thanks!

comments.body: Hi Travis, this might be more of a question than something to fix.  I had to update some PRO courses to reflect a change in the College/School/Department (changed from College of Health Sciences to Health Science Department).  On course PRO 4850 it has "Existing proposal" noted on left column although it is not a current proposal.  I verified that it is in the "Completed Proposals" list; it is also in an active status and has an implementation date/step completed.  I don't believe this notation should be there; it is confusing.  I also logged in as Dr. Nicotera and did NOT see the "Existing Proposal" message (which is good). The update I made for the college/School/Department should have also changed the Originator from Ludwig to Nicotera . . . or is this not done until a new proposal is created?  (Dr. Nicotera does have a copy button available to him for the course.)  Thanks!

The "Existing Proposal" text is from the course checker, which determines if the course can be launched into the approval process. "Existing Proposal" means it's already been launched, which is why you can find it on the completed proposals page.

Thank You,

Travis Hillyard
Governet Customer Support Manager

Please feel free to contact me if you have any questions or concerns
208.534.7901  -   travish@governet.net
What about the college/school/department change?  Shouldn't the "owner" name change as soon as that update is made?

The left menu is a different page so unless its told to reload it will retain the same information. Usually this can be done by changing pages or leaving the course and going back in.

Thank You,

Travis Hillyard
Governet Customer Support Manager

Please feel free to contact me if you have any questions or concerns
208.534.7901  -   travish@governet.net

subject: Impacted programs on Course outline
description: Hi Michael, I know that you ran the update query to resolve the missting Impacted programs from course outlines but I noticed this issue today. CET 1610 had a title change in 0505 and one of the programs it sits on is ITSC-AS (PID#460 - Active, 0505).  The program of study still reflected the old title and did not have the ITSC-AS listed in the Impacted programs (shouldn't this have been corrected with the query?)  So I went into the program of study (PID #460) and reselected the course (new title) and the program then appeared at the bottom of the course outline.  What I noticed and what concerns me is that we have lost all of the historical information that should also be appearing on the Course outline (was on previous version of CET 1610).  See attached.

comments.body: Hi Michael, I know that you ran the update query to resolve the missting Impacted programs from course outlines but I noticed this issue today. CET 1610 had a title change in 0505 and one of the programs it sits on is ITSC-AS (PID#460 - Active, 0505).  The program of study still reflected the old title and did not have the ITSC-AS listed in the Impacted programs (shouldn't this have been corrected with the query?)  So I went into the program of study (PID #460) and reselected the course (new title) and the program then appeared at the bottom of the course outline.  What I noticed and what concerns me is that we have lost all of the historical information that should also be appearing on the Course outline (was on previous version of CET 1610).  See attached.

Hi Angela,

I have assigned this ticket to a developer's queue. The developer will be in contact with you once they begin working on this ticket and if they have any questions.

Thanks,
Wesley L

Hello,

My name is Michael Gregston. I just wanted to let you know I am starting development on this request. If I have any questions, I will contact you.

Have a great day!

Regards,
Michael Gregston

Angela,

The query I ran updated the data that I showed you.  There could be other programs with different situations that we do not know about and maybe that is why this program was not included in the query I ran.  The query I ran got the majority of them.  If there is some stragglers out there, the only way to find them is to stumble upon them or to go though each program manually.  You did the right thing by selecting the course in the program that the course is supposed to be in.  I can see the program is displaying on the course outline report now.

The reason the historical programs are not displaying on the new course is because the historical programs are mapped to the historical courses.  If you look at the historical courses you will see historical programs.  This is to preserve your historical data.  Each historical program does not keep connecting to each new course that you create.  If it did that then you would have a cluster of data that would not be able to read.  Your historical data is still there, you just have to look at your historical courses.

Does this help complete this ticket?

Regards,
Michael Gregston

comments.body: The formatting of the program outline comparison report is all askew and it is very difficult to follow.  I've used both IE and Firefox and they show the same result. Program ID #402, #337 for example.  Not sure if it is affecting all or just some.

I believe the report is taking to long to load due to all the courses that are attached to the program_id = 337. Trying to clean it up and figure out how to move the function into the same page instead of calling for cfc file.

Angela,

I have attached are new format for doing comparison reports.  This may help it line up more but not necessarily, the more content you have on either comparison report it will be askew because one program will have more then the other program when changes are made. Would you like to try this new format or keep keep the one you have now for comparison?

Regards,
Michael Gregston

Angela,

I have altered the code and tried to make program id = 337 to line up the best I can.  Dew to how much content is in this program makes it very difficult to line up because there is some much difference.  This will be push to your DEV site tonight, please take a look at it and let me know if it is good to go or let me know if you want to try the new comparison report that attached to the comment above.

Regards,
Michael Gregston
Reports/program_report_html.cfm

I'm sorry to say that i don't care for the new report.  The old report was working fine until just recently - in fact if you look at #395 BUS-BS it looks perfect.  I want to keep the old format but do need it to be able to be read easily.  Thank you.

Angela,

The report is working correctly. On your Live site I look at program id = 395, and yes it does look nice, so does a lot of your other programs that where copied and they do not have very many changes.  The reason program id = 337 looks so off is because of how many changes there are.  The more changes you have, the harder it is for the compare report to be able to compare each individual properly.

I do not see a way to fix this report for this program due to the load of changes that have occurred.  How often do you have this many revisions for a program?

Regards,
Michael Gregston

We definitely have situations where there is a significant number of changes being made so I won't say this was an exception, however, I had not noticed the issue before now so perhaps it was a combination of the type and number of changes?  Like I said earlier, I would prefer to keep the old report and will just have to monitor if the "readability" becomes a pervasive problem.  I will close this ticket now.  Thank you Michael.

subject: Related programs feature is not functioning correctly
description: Courses are on a program of study but are not appearing under the listing of "Related programs" on the course outline document.  Please see attached.

comments.body: Courses are on a program of study but are not appearing under the listing of "Related programs" on the course outline document.  Please see attached.

Angela,

I just pulled up the Program Report for the program you have highlighted in your document "Technology Management" Effective Spring 2014
Unlinke your document I do not see ISM 4915 listed, has this been changed.

Also you will note that in your document and also in the Course Outlines for the Pending version of ISM 4915 it lists two programs both with a status of Historical. Which the program in your document has a status of Active.

These reports are showing which version of the programs is linked to which version of the course. So if ISM 4915 Pending needs to be included in an Active program this can be altered through the Course Blocks pages.

Thank You,

Travis Hillyard
Governet Customer Support Manager

Please feel free to contact me if you have any questions or concerns
208.534.7901  -   travish@governet.net

I'm not sure that I understand this.  If an active course has a proposal in a pending status (no change to prefix or number) or the course version is newer (later) than the active version of the program, it won't show up under the Related Programs?  I'm specifically referencing COP 2806 which has a 0490 active version, appears on the CWPA-AS program (0480 active version) but is not listed under Related Programs on the outline.  I thought that regardless of term, if a course appears on a program of study (Active, Historical or pending) it would be listed under the Related Programs.

The Related Program section of the Course Outline works this way
If the course is not active AND it has a parent course 
The report will show the programs that the previous(or parent) version of the course is connected to
If the course is active OR the course doesn't have a parent course
The report will show the programs that this version of the course is connected to.

COP 2806 (Active) is connected to Computer Programming Specialist

Computer Programming and Analysis Associate in Science CWPA-AS is connected to the historical version of COP 2806



Thank You,

Travis Hillyard
Governet Customer Support Manager

Please feel free to contact me if you have any questions or concerns
208.534.7901  -   travish@governet.net
Thank you for that explanation.  Very helpful.

Is there anything that needs to be addressed regarding this?

Thank You,

Travis Hillyard
Governet Customer Support Manager

Please feel free to contact me if you have any questions or concerns
208.534.7901  -   travish@governet.net
Sorry, should have marked it resolved with your explanation.

subject: Radial button for financial aid
description:
VETTC-AS: On the program information page under "Is this program eligible for financial aid?"  currently the "No" button is selected (I believe this is the default for a proposal) but I am unable to move it to "Yes". Can this be edited by an administrator ONLY after the proposal has been submitted? I'm not sure what rules are applying to these fields but this is what it should be:
1) When a new proposal is created for an AS, BAS or BS degree the selection should be "Yes" and remain "Yes".
2) When a new proposal (new CT, major mod or minor mod) is created for a Certificate (CT) the default should be "No"
3) An Administrator should be able to edit this field once the program has been submitted.

I hope this makes sense.  Thanks.

I have created a new service called get_basic_course_data_combo
It accepts 3 parameters 
combo: this accepts a list of prefix/number combonations e.g ("ACG-2001,ACG-2021")
status: this accepts a list of spelled out course status e.g. ("Active,Pending"), note if no status is supplied the service will return only Active
curricunet_id: this accepts a list of CurricUNET identifiers e.g.("260,262")

Thank You,

Travis Hillyard
Governet Customer Support Manager

Please feel free to contact me if you have any questions or concerns
208.534.7901  -   travish@governet.net

subject: Codes and Dates fields
description: Hi Travis - when Tim was here we discussed the need to be able to update the dates in the Codes and Dates fields for all proposals that fall under a particular term.  i.e. all First Term 0490, BOT submission date = 2/15/2014.  Ideally I will request new admin screens for these updates but until that can be done it seemed we should request an update script be run.  I'd like to request this  but would like to be able to see what the current fields show.  The Table "Course Dates" does not contain all of the fields from the Codes & Dates page - would it be possible for me to view those fields either in that table or in Course Data table?  Thanks.

comments.body: Hi Travis - when Tim was here we discussed the need to be able to update the dates in the Codes and Dates fields for all proposals that fall under a particular term.  i.e. all First Term 0490, BOT submission date = 2/15/2014.  Ideally I will request new admin screens for these updates but until that can be done it seemed we should request an update script be run.  I'd like to request this  but would like to be able to see what the current fields show.  The Table "Course Dates" does not contain all of the fields from the Codes & Dates page - would it be possible for me to view those fields either in that table or in Course Data table?  Thanks.

All of the dates in the Entry of Special dates should be in the Course Dates table.
All other dates should be in the Courses table
If any are missing please let me know.

Thank You,

Travis Hillyard
Governet Customer Support Manager

Please feel free to contact me if you have any questions or concerns
208.534.7901  -   travish@governet.net
The only date I can see is the "Course Date" which appears to be a course origination date.

I made a test course to verify this see attached images

All 9 "Entry of Special Dates" showed up on the ad-hoc view for course dates.

Likewise the Origination date shows on the Course Data ad-hoc view


Thank You,

Travis Hillyard
Governet Customer Support Manager

Please feel free to contact me if you have any questions or concerns
208.534.7901  -   travish@governet.net
Ah, I see and I'm sorry I didn't understand the set-up.  I was expecting to see each special date as an individual field.  I will send a request for that update script shortly.  Thanks.

I will mark this as pending until then

Thank You,

Travis Hillyard
Governet Customer Support Manager

Please feel free to contact me if you have any questions or concerns
208.534.7901  -   travish@governet.net

I've attached a brief spreadsheet which outlines the date field updates that we need.  For the field "Submitted to C&I" I would like to use the same date as is reflected for the "launch/submit" date.  I will submit an additional ticket for the removal of the date fields we no longer need.  We were also thinking that these dates need to be appended to the bottom of the Notes and/or History field but I see a new(?) field "Previous Version" that might suffice.  Although I'm not sure why that is a drop down/selectable field?  Thanks.

"I will submit an additional ticket for the removal of the date fields we no longer need"
If these dates are in the Special Entry of Dates section you can remove them through the Admin>Look-Up Data>Date Types page

"We were also thinking that these dates need to be appended to the bottom of the Notes and/or History field but I see a new"
Is this a request or a "heads up"?

Thank You,

Travis Hillyard
Governet Customer Support Manager

Please feel free to contact me if you have any questions or concerns
208.534.7901  -   travish@governet.net

Thanks for the information on modifying the dates fields - that was exactly what I had wanted to do.  I was able to modify and remove fields.  If I added a field would it automatically appear on the screen or would I have to request it be added to the page?

The Previous Version field looks like it will satisfy our need to see the term of the last version of a course but I don't understand why that is an editable field - it shouldn't be.  Thanks.

"If I added a field would it automatically appear on the screen or would I have to request it be added to the page?"
Yes that Look-Up page controls what shows in the Entry of special dates section on the codes page, if you change, update, add or remove it will be reflected on that section of the codes page.

"The Previous Version field looks like it will satisfy our need to see the term of the last version of a course but I don't understand why that is an editable field - it shouldn't be. Thanks."
I do not see a Previous Version field on the codes page, are you referring to a different field or possibly to a different page?


This still remains unanswered

"We were also thinking that these dates need to be appended to the bottom of the Notes and/or History field but I see a new" 
Is this a request or a "heads up"?

Thank You,

Travis Hillyard
Governet Customer Support Manager

Please feel free to contact me if you have any questions or concerns
208.534.7901  -   travish@governet.net

Love the functionality of the codes/dates page! Is it possible to add a field under the dates type that is not formatted as a date?  i.e. a 5 digit text field,  

I've attached a snip of the page showing that Previous Version field.
  
Re: appending dates:  We were discussing the course outline report  this morning and (let me apologize up front) we had requested to have that removed from the bottom of the course outline but we need to have it back.  We would also like to include a couple of other fields so I've attached a sample of what we need. Would you prefer I add this request to #22242 as that ticket was regarding course outline formatting or submit a new ticket?

The previous version is editable, on this page because its an admin only page. This is the field that connects the course to the previous version and is needed. For example a faculty creates a new version of a course that should have been a revision of an existing course. And instead of deleting the version you can change the proposal type to Course Modification and the previous version to the older version of the appropriate course.

"Love the functionality of the codes/dates page! Is it possible to add a field under the dates type that is not formatted as a date? i.e. a 5 digit text field,"
No this section is only for dates if you need a different field on the page it will need to be added through code.

Regarding the dates could you please create a new ticket as #22242 seems to be almost complete

Thank You,

Travis Hillyard
Governet Customer Support Manager

Please feel free to contact me if you have any questions or concerns
208.534.7901  -   travish@governet.net

Thanks for the explanation on the previous version field - now I understand. :)  We will talk through the additional field and decide if it is a need or want.  I will submit a new ticket for the other requested items.  Thanks.

alright marking this ticket as solved for now

Thank You,

Travis Hillyard
Governet Customer Support Manager

Please feel free to contact me if you have any questions or concerns
208.534.7901  -   travish@governet.net

subject: Related programs
description: Several course outlines (e.g. NUR 4636, NUR 4835) are not reflecting the program of study that they reside on. These are not the only courses that have been noted as missing this information.  Almost all upper division courses should have a program of study attached to them.  See attached.  Thanks. Angela

comments.body: Several course outlines (e.g. NUR 4636, NUR 4835) are not reflecting the program of study that they reside on. These are not the only courses that have been noted as missing this information.  Almost all upper division courses should have a program of study attached to them.  See attached.  Thanks. Angela

Hi Angela,

I have assigned this ticket to a developer's queue. The developer will be in contact with you once they begin working on this ticket and if they have any questions.

Thanks,
Wesley L

Hello,

My name is Michael Gregston. I just wanted to let you know I am starting development on this request. If I have any questions, I will contact you.

Have a great day!

Regards,
Michael Gregston

Angela,

I went to Build > Programs > Edit Program "Nursing" (programs_id = 270) > Course Blocks > Courses (MAJOR CORE COURSES) > Edit "NUR 4636", when the page loads for some reason the field "Course Number & Name" is not displaying the course, I went and reselected the course in the drop down and click "Update", now the program displays on the Course Outline for the course.  It looks like the parent courses_id did not get updated to the child courses_id when the child course went Active.  The code looks like it is updating correctly, this might of been a past issue and it got fixed but the affected courses never got updated and you may just be running on to them now.  The quick way to update these are when you see them to go into the course block and re-save the field to the new course proposal.

Does this help solve this ticket?

Regards,
Michael Gregston

good morning Michael,  I'm not sure I understand - is this what you mean? If the impacted program of study is not reflected on the course outline I should go to the program of study, edit the block and reselect the course into the block and save? How will I know what courses are on what programs of study (or if they are missing) without reviewing every program of study and checking each course?

Angela,

Yes, you would have to know which programs need updated and the only way to do that is to go through each one.  This would be the best way to do it because it would be done manually.

I have been looking into writing a query that will update these for you, so it would look for all active programs that have courses that are attached to the program where the course status is historical and that course has a child course and the child course has a status of active and it would update all of them to the correct course.  I think I can do this but if there is any courses that are mapped to the wrong parent then it will end up having bad data, but as long as they are all mapped correctly then it would be a faster fix for you.  Do you want me to write this query to do a massive update for you?

Regards,
Michael Gregston

COMMENTS:
- After I run this query, I go to: Build > Courses > Course Outline "NUR 4835L" (courses_id = 7486), section "Related Programs:", the program now displays on the report.
- Build > Programs > Edit Program "Nursing" (programs_id = 270) > Course Blocks > Courses (MAJOR CORE COURSES) > Edit "NUR 4835L", this course now displays in the drop down as selected.

QUERIES FOR LIVE:
update stpetersburg.program_courses pc_outer
set pc_outer.courses_id = (
	select c2.courses_id
	from stpetersburg.programs p
		inner join stpetersburg.course_options co on p.programs_id = co.programs_id
		inner join stpetersburg.program_courses pc on co.course_options_id = pc.course_options_id
		inner join stpetersburg.courses c
			inner join stpetersburg.subjects s on c.subjects_id = s.subjects_id
			inner join stpetersburg.course_status cs on c.course_status_id = cs.course_status_id
		on pc.courses_id = c.courses_id
		left join stpetersburg.course_proposal cp
			inner join stpetersburg.courses c2 on cp.courses_id = c2.courses_id
			inner join stpetersburg.subjects s2 on c2.subjects_id = s2.subjects_id
			inner join stpetersburg.course_status cs2 on c2.course_status_id = cs2.course_status_id
		on pc.courses_id = cp.prev_courses_id
	where c.course_status_id = 2
	and pc_outer.program_courses_id = pc.program_courses_id
	and c2.course_status_id = 1
	and p.program_status_id = 1
)
where exists (
	select 1
	from stpetersburg.programs p
		inner join stpetersburg.course_options co on p.programs_id = co.programs_id
		inner join stpetersburg.program_courses pc on co.course_options_id = pc.course_options_id
		inner join stpetersburg.courses c
			inner join stpetersburg.subjects s on c.subjects_id = s.subjects_id
			inner join stpetersburg.course_status cs on c.course_status_id = cs.course_status_id
		on pc.courses_id = c.courses_id
		left join stpetersburg.course_proposal cp
			inner join stpetersburg.courses c2 on cp.courses_id = c2.courses_id
			inner join stpetersburg.subjects s2 on c2.subjects_id = s2.subjects_id
			inner join stpetersburg.course_status cs2 on c2.course_status_id = cs2.course_status_id
		on pc.courses_id = cp.prev_courses_id
	where c.course_status_id = 2
	and pc_outer.program_courses_id = pc.program_courses_id
	and c2.course_status_id = 1
	and p.program_status_id = 1
)
;

Angela,

I have made the following changes:

- After I run this query, I go to: Build > Courses > Course Outline "NUR 4835L" (courses_id = 7486), section "Related Programs:", the program now displays on the report.
- Build > Programs > Edit Program "Nursing" (programs_id = 270) > Course Blocks > Courses (MAJOR CORE COURSES) > Edit "NUR 4835L", this course now displays in the drop down as selected.

My changes have been pushed to your DEV site.  Test my changes on your DEV site and let me know if this ticket is complete and if you want me to run this query on your LIVE site.

To log in to your development site, navigate to dev.curricunet.com/stpetersburg. The username and password are the same as your live site.

Regards,
Michael Gregston

I'll be honest - I'm a little nervous to have a "massive" update done on the system.  Is there any way to identify the time frame in which this error was impacting the system?  In the instance of NUR 4835, the only update done since the initial load was the most recent cycle of 0505.  Is there any automated way for you to check the courses with 0505 active versions to see if they are missing impacted programs of study?

Angela,

If I run the query, it will update 168 courses that are attached to programs.

I do not have a way to tell how long it was impacting the system, where there would be 168 updates, I would think it has been a while.

Is the "cycle 0505" saved any where in the course so I can tell what courses are in this "cycle 0505"?  I am not seeing anything that will help me identify the courses your looking for with out more details.  I would need specific data that is in the course to narrow the search and this specific data would need to be the same for each courses your wanting to see.

Regards,
Michael Gregston

Hi Michael, 0505 refers to the effective term (field = first term on course data table).  I looked at some of the courses that were updated in 0505 (PRO 3505, RTE 2782, MAE 3823) and they are all missing the Related programs information even though it was on the previous versions.  I checked a couple that were updated the previous term (0495) and they do not appear to have the issue.  We processed approximately 92 course modifications in 0505 so that leads me to think that it may be isolated to the most recent copies made.  Report #4  - course updates by term - would generate this list, too.  I wonder where the extra 76 are coming from then (168 - 92 = 76)?  With this additional information can you compare the courses?  We would also like to add the program code and term  (i.e. BUS-BS (0495), MGTORG-BAS (0505)  to the Related programs listing on the COR but I can submit a ticket for this if that would be better.  Just let me know.  Thanks! Angela

Angela,

Out of my list of 168 courses that need updated, only 67 of them have the option "(505)" selected.  This means a lot of them are older programs that where updated before your last updates and I believe that by running this query that I have created will correct all your current data and your older data to the way it should be.  I checked courses "PRO 3505, RTE 2782, MAE 3823" and with running this query these courses are displaying there "Related Programs".  This brings us to two options, I can run the query and update all existing courses that need updated in the course blocks or you can manually go in and check every one and re-save the courses to the course blocks in the programs and check them that way.  Which way would you like to proceed?

Regards,
Michael Gregston

Michael,  Interesting information.  Could you send me the list of 168 courses that you have identified?  We will most likely have you run the update query but I would feel better if I could review them first.  Thanks, Angela

Angela,

I have attached a excel file of my data.  This is a list of programs that have a Active status.  It then displays the courses attached to the programs that have the status of Historical.  After that it displays the child courses to the Historical courses that have the status of Active.  Some of the "H" and the "L" will not display on the child courses do to the change of how the letters are captured, but the "courses_id" is still the same and correct.  The "programs_id" and "courses_id" are the unique id's that you can use to look up the program and courses.  The child courses I believe are what should be attached to the programs.  Please review the list and let me know what you want to do.

Regards,
Michael Gregston

Good morning Michael,  Thank you for sending that information.  I've looked through it and am comfortable with your using a query to update the records. If you could let me know when it is completed I will take another look through to verify, too.  Thanks!  Angela

Angela,

The query has been ran on your LIVE site.  This completes this ticket.

Regards,
Michael Gregston

subject: Non-credit course deactivations
description: Hi Travis  - With the integration of CurricUNET data with our online course catalog we need to remove the Non-credit courses from displaying with the credit courses.  I created a new status -  "Inactive" - for these courses and need to have them updated.  There were courses that were Active as well as Historical within this criteria (Academic Group = Non-Credit, Status = Active or Historical).  I've attached a  spreadsheet with CurricUNET IDs.

subject: Program modification checklist
description: When creating a program modification proposal the checklist isn't appearing correctly and consequently the "submit" button is available even though all of the information has not been completed.

comments.body: When creating a program modification proposal the checklist isn't appearing correctly and consequently the "submit" button is available even though all of the information has not been completed.

We will need to review the course checker for this proposal type

Thank You,

Travis Hillyard
Governet Customer Support Manager

Please feel free to contact me if you have any questions or concerns
208.534.7901  -   travish@governet.net

Angela,

Are you still using the pre-launch for modify program proposal types? I ask this because there is an issue with it that is causing a part of this problem. Also, which checklist items would you like to have "Finished" to cause the submit button to appear?

admin/look-up/subjects.cfm
build/courses/c_builder/e_cover.cfm
build/courses/create_course.cfm

ALTER TABLE STPETERSBURG.SUBJECTS 
ADD (REFERENCE_NUMBER NUMBER(6, 2) );

UPDATE stpetersburg.subjects s
SET s.reference_number = (SELECT ls.ref_nbr
                          FROM stpetersburg.load_subjects ls
                          WHERE s.subject_code = ls.prefix);

UPDATE stpetersburg.courses c
SET c.disc_ref_num = (SELECT s.reference_number
                      FROM stpetersburg.subjects s
                      WHERE c.subjects_id = s.subjects_id);

Angela,

As we discussed on the phone I have completed:
- I have uploaded from your attached excel file on tab "Prefix by Discipline" the column "Ref Nbr" to match your column "Prefix" to are column "subject_code" and inserted "Ref Nbr" into our Subjects table.  You can see this by going to Admin>Look-up Data>Prefixes, not all of your "Prefix" was in are Subjects table so some of them are blank.  If you want to update the ones that are blank you should be able to go in and edit any of the subjects and add the "Ref Nbr" to the field "Reference Number" on the Prefixes page.  Also your "Prefix" if there where multiple ones that had the multiple "Ref Nbr" the first "Ref Nbr" got inserted and the other ones did not because they look like duplicates and did not get added to the Subjects table.
- I have made it to when you create a new course depending on what subject is selected it will auto populate the field "Discipline Reference Number" on the "General Course Cover" page with your "Ref Nbr".  Now if you do not have your "Ref Nbr" saved to a subject then it will not auto populate because it will not have a number to auto populate it with.
- I have made it to where the field "Discipline Reference Number" on the "General Course Cover" page is only editable by Super Admins.
- I have updated all existing courses with there field "Discipline Reference Number" on the "General Course Cover" page.  This means if the subject has "Ref Nbr" then it updated the course and the "Ref Nbr" is displaying correctly on all courses except for the courses that the subject does not have a "Ref Nbr".  You can manually enter them in or give me a updated list with all of the "Ref Nbr" that matches all your subjects and I can run a update query again and update all courses with a subject.

*This will be pushed to your DEV site some time tonight, so you will be able to test this out starting tomorrow.  Please test it out and let me know if it is good to go or not.

*To log in to your development site, navigate to dev.curricunet.com/stpetersburg. The username and password are the same as your live site.

Regards,
Michael Gregston

delete
from stpetersburg.course_requisites cr
where exists (select 1
				from (select course_requisites_id, courses_id, programs_id, row_number() over (partition by courses_id, programs_id order by courses_id, programs_id) as result_num
						from stpetersburg.course_requisites cr2
						where programs_id is not null
					) dup
			where result_num > 1
			and cr.course_requisites_id = dup.course_requisites_id
			)
;

subject: Web Service changes: get_program_report_data
description:
Hello Travis.  Two more things for the get_program_report_data function:
1) Return program_code database field instead of program_code function parameter – we need the actual data, especially when requesting multiple programs.

2) Structure XML with "Records" container and each record as a "recordSet" for consistency with other functions -- (as opposed to "recordset" top-level with "record" objects).

Thanks

Bill Bliss

comments.body:
Hello Travis.  Two more things for the get_program_report_data function:
1) Return program_code database field instead of program_code function parameter – we need the actual data, especially when requesting multiple programs.

2) Structure XML with "Records" container and each record as a "recordSet" for consistency with other functions -- (as opposed to "recordset" top-level with "record" objects).

Thanks

Bill Bliss

subject: SPC web services
description:
Hi Travis,



We’re finally starting to resume our progress after our long holiday break.
  We’ve found 2 things that need addressing.



The newest function you created for us, GET_PROGRAM_BASICS, is returning a
string rather than XML.

subject: Approval Process step is not changing status
description: During the New Course approval process when we send a proposal back to the originator for editing the status is changing back to "Pending" for editing purposes.  When the proposal has been marked "Reviewed and made changes" it should move to the next level with a "Submitted" status.  This is not happening.  I have double checked the process steps and tested it in dev but the status remains in pending.

comments.body: During the New Course approval process when we send a proposal back to the originator for editing the status is changing back to "Pending" for editing purposes.  When the proposal has been marked "Reviewed and made changes" it should move to the next level with a "Submitted" status.  This is not happening.  I have double checked the process steps and tested it in dev but the status remains in pending.

Hi Angela,

I have assigned this ticket to a developer's queue. The developer will be in contact with you once they begin working on this ticket and if they have any questions.

Thanks,
Wesley L

Hello,

My name is Michael Gregston. I just wanted to let you know I am starting development on this request. If I have any questions, I will contact you.

Have a great day!

Regards,
Michael Gregston

Angela,

I removed the proposal "EAP 1686" (courses_id = 7560) from the approval process and resubmitted it back in.  I took action on step 1 and it changes the status to "Submitted", then I took action on level 4 and sent it to 4.50 and the status changes to "Pending", then I sent it to level 5 and the status changes back to "Submitted".  The status is working correctly.  I did notice when I first click on "Check Status" on the proposal I saw the attached red text.  See attached image below.  This tells me that you are making changes to the approval process while you still have proposals that are already in the approval process using the same approval process that your making changes too.  You can no do this with out it causing errors.  Best practice is to create a new version of the process and then make your changes, but these new changes will not affect the proposals that are currently in the approval process.  If you want the current proposals to have the new changes, you will need to remove the proposal from the approval process and then resubmit it after you have made your new changes in your new version of the approval process.  If you need more details on how the approval process works, please give me a call and I can explain them to you. My work number is (208) 522 - 1225.

Does this help solve this ticket?

Regards,
Michael Gregston

I thought I could make changes and that they just wouldn't be effective for the proposals in the pipeline.  I didn't think it would create errors.  I'll have to wait until this cycle clears through and then try again.  Thanks for the explanation.

I think there is some confusion on this request.  We had already created a new proposal type called "Curriculum Services Administrative Modification" that we are using for program wide updates.  We created it to just have two steps and it is working as we need it to.  i think this request was actually about dealing with the fact that when we make any change to a process it requires a new version and is this necessary.

Yes any change to the process requires a new version of the process. This is a core part of the functionality of the approval process and removing it would require us to rework the approval process module as a whole, which would be very time consuming and could possibly lead to significant issues.

Thank You,

Angela,

Ideally we want it to be in an excel style format where field changes would be highlighted by red font or something like that.  The attached example is not ideal by any stretch but what I had put together for the last cycle.

Most likely the report Tim showed you was a board/committee report which displays a certain number of fields and compares them. If you would prefer we can create a report that does that. I have added a new web service get_program_basics
which will accept the following arguments
		program_code type="string" required="false" hint="allows for a comma-separated list of program codes."
		status type="string" required="false" hint="allows for a comma-separated list of program statuses." 
		degree_type type="string" required="false" hint="allows for a comma-separated list of Degree Types."
		curricunet_id type="string" required="false" hint="allows for a comma-separated list of program ids."


It will return the following values

AWARD_TYPE_TITLE 	example Certificate with Financial Aid Eligibility
DIVISION_TITLE 	example   College of Business
PROGRAMS_ID 	example   319
PROGRAM_CODE 	example	ACTAP-CT
PROGRAM_TITLE 	example	ACCOUNTING APPLICATIONS

Thank You,

Travis Hillyard
Governet Customer Support Manager

Please feel free to contact me if you have any questions or concerns

delete from stpetersburg.course_grade_options a

subject: Program Information Page; Financial Aid eligibility
description:
On the Program Information page, the fourth question asks "Is this program eligible for financial aid?"  This is a required field and on NEW programs it should default to "Pending Approval, this program has been added to the PPA"  (please update text to "this program has been added to the PPA document; upon approval by SACS this will be updated by Curriculum Services".  This field should be editable by an admiistrator.  

If a major or minor modification is created the existing selection (i.e. Yes, No) should carry over to the new proposal and can be modified by the originator/co-contributor.  Currently users can only select Pending on major/minor modifications.
