error on programs query


Error starting at line 157 in command:
CREATE OR REPLACE FORCE VIEW "STPETERSBURG"."VW_PROGRAM_DATA" ("CURRICUNET_ID", "TERM" "PROGRAM_CODE", "PROGRAM_NAME", "STATUS", "AUTHOR", "PROPOSAL_TYPE", "ACTIVE_STATUS", "DEGREE_TYPE", "COLLEGE_SCHOOL_DEPARTMENT", "ACADEMIC_ORG", "FIRST_TERM", "FEASIBILITY_DATE", "SUBMISSION_REASON", "JUSTIFICATION", "NON_CREDIT", "UPPER_DIVISION", "POST_BACCALAUREATE_CERT", "WHERE_ADMINISTERED", "COURSES_OFFERED", "STUDENTS_ADMITTED", "LIMITED_ACCESS", "SPC_APPLICATION", "OWN_WEBPAGE", "GRADUATE", "FL_FRAMEWORK", "FL_CIP_CODE", "FEDERAL_CIP_CODE", "STATE_CREDITS", "SPC_CREDITS", "FLORIDA_PREREQUISITES", "SOC_CODE", "ASSOCIATED_OCCUPATIONS", "SOC_WEB_LINK", "PROGRAM_APPROVED", "PROGRAM_NEED", "PROGRAM_DESIGN", "PROGRAM_WAGE_ANALYSIS", "PROGRAM_TEACHOUT", "PROGRAM_SUMMARY", "JOB_RELATED_OPPORTUNITIES", "RELATED_WEBSITES", "ARTICULATE", "ADMISSION_REQUIREMENTS", "ADMISSION_RULES", "ADMISSION_NOTES", "GRADUATION_REQUIREMENTS", "GRADUATION_RULES", "GRADUATION_NOTES", "PRINT_PROGRAM_ON_DIPLOMA", "DIPLOMA_DESCRIPTION", "PRINT_PROGRAM_ON_TRANSCRIPT", "TRANSCRIPT_DESCRIPTION", "PEOPLESOFT_PLAN_DESCRIPTION", "PEOPLESOFT_SHORT_DESCRIPTION", "ACADEMIC_CAREER", "PENDING_SACS_APPROVAL", "LAST_GRADUATE_TERM", "DATES_HISTORY_NOTES", "ON_TIME_GRADUATION_RATE", "ON_TIME_GRADUATION_STUDENTS", "ON_TIME_GRADUATION_FISCAL_YEAR", "ON_TIME_GRADUATION_SOURCE", "ONTIME_GRADUATION_WEBSITES", "IN_STATE_TUITION", "OUT_OF_STATE_TUITION", "TUITION_SOURCE", "TYPICAL_COSTS", "JOB_PLACEMENT_RATE", "JOB_PLACEMENT_STUDENTS", "TUITION_FISCAL_YEAR", "JOB_SOURCE", "MEDIAN_DEBT", "MEDIAN_LOAN_NOTE") AS 
  SELECT  p.programs_id as curricunet_id,
  sem.semester_title||' '||sem.semester_year||' ('||sem.semester_code||')' as term,
        p.program_code, 
        p.program_title as program_name,
		ps.program_status_title as status,
        (u.fm_name||' '||u.l_name) as author,
        pt.prop_type_title as proposal_type,
        acs.active_status_desc as active_status,
        at.award_type_title as degree_type,
        di.division_title as college_school_department,
        d.department_title as academic_org,
        CASE
          WHEN pp.semesters_id IS NOT NULL THEN (sem.semester_title||' '||sem.semester_year||' ('||sem.semester_code||')') 
        END as first_term,
        pp.feasibility_date,
        sub.submission_desc as submission_reason,
		stpetersburg.concat_prgneed(p.programs_id) as justification,
        CASE pi.non_credit WHEN 1 THEN 'Yes' WHEN 0 THEN 'No' END as non_credit,
        CASE pi.upper_division WHEN 1 THEN 'Yes' WHEN 0 THEN 'No' END as upper_division,
        CASE pi.post_bacc WHEN 1 THEN 'Yes' WHEN 0 THEN 'No' END as post_baccalaureate_cert,
        ploc.prog_locs as where_administered,
        pdm.offerings as courses_offered,
        pos.offered as students_admitted,
        CASE pi.limited_access WHEN 1 THEN 'Yes' WHEN 0 THEN 'No' END as limited_access,
        CASE pi.spc_application WHEN '1' THEN 'Yes' WHEN '0' THEN 'No' END as spc_application,
        CASE pi.webpage WHEN 1 THEN 'Yes' WHEN 0 THEN 'No' END as own_webpage,
        CASE pi.graduate WHEN 1 THEN 'Yes' WHEN 0 THEN 'No' END as graduate,
        CASE p.fl_framework WHEN 1 THEN 'Yes' WHEN 0 THEN 'No' END as fl_framework,
        CASE WHEN p.fl_cip_id IS NOT NULL THEN (fcc.fl_cip_code||': '||fcc.fl_cip_desc||' ('||fcc.fl_cip_award||')') END as fl_cip_code,
        CASE WHEN p.cip_id IS NOT NULL THEN (cc.cip_code||': '||cc.cip_desc) END as federal_cip_code,
        p.state_credits,
        p.spc_credits,
        CASE p.florida_prereq WHEN 1 THEN 'Yes' WHEN 0 THEN 'No' END as florida_prerequisites,
        p.soc_code,
        p.soc_list as associated_occupations,
        p.soc_link as soc_web_link,
        p.program_approved,
        p.program_need,
        p.program_design,
        p.program_wage as program_wage_analysis,
        p.program_teachout,
		stpetersburg.concat_prgdesc(p.programs_id) as program_summary,
        p.career_options as job_related_opportunities,
        p.related_websites,
        CASE p.articulated WHEN 1 THEN 'Yes' WHEN 0 THEN 'No' END as articulate,
        CASE p.admission WHEN 1 THEN 'Yes' WHEN 0 THEN 'No' END as admission_requirements,
        p.admission_rules,
        p.admission_notes,
        CASE p.graduation WHEN 1 THEN 'Yes' WHEN 0 THEN 'No' END as graduation_requirements,
        p.graduation_rules,
        p.graduation_notes,
        CASE p.print_program WHEN 1 THEN 'Yes' WHEN 0 THEN 'No' END as print_program_on_diploma,
        p.dipl_desc as diploma_description,
        CASE p.print_trans WHEN 1 THEN 'Yes' WHEN 0 THEN 'No' END as print_program_on_transcript,
        p.trans_desc as transcript_description,
        p.peoplesoft as peoplesoft_plan_description,
        p.peoplesoft_short_description,
        CASE p.academic_career WHEN 1 THEN 'Undergraduate' WHEN 0 THEN 'Non-Credit' END as academic_career,
        CASE p.sacs_approval WHEN 1 THEN 'Yes' WHEN 0 THEN 'No' END as pending_sacs_approval,
        CASE
          WHEN pp.last_Graduate IS NOT NULL THEN (sem.semester_title||' '||sem.semester_year||' ('||sem.semester_code||')') 
        END as last_graduate_term,
        p.is_comments as dates_history_notes,
        pe.grad_rate as on_time_graduation_rate,
        pe.grad_students as on_time_graduation_students,
        fy.fiscal_year_desc as on_time_graduation_fiscal_year,
        pe.graduation_source as on_time_graduation_source,
        pe.graduation_web_sites as ontime_graduation_websites,
        pe.in_state as in_state_tuition,
        pe.out_state as out_of_state_tuition,
        pe.tuition_source,
        pe.books_supplies as typical_costs,
        pe.job_rate as job_placement_rate,
        pe.job_students as job_placement_students,
        fy2.fiscal_year_desc as tuition_fiscal_year,
        pe.job_source,
        pe.median_debt,
        pe.loan_note as median_loan_note
FROM   stpetersburg.programs p
	   INNER JOIN stpetersburg.program_status ps ON p.program_Status_id = ps.program_status_id
       INNER JOIN stpetersburg.users u ON p.users_id = u.users_id
       INNER JOIN stpetersburg.prop_types pt ON p.prop_types_id = pt.prop_types_id
       LEFT OUTER JOIN stpetersburg.active_status acs ON p.active_status_id = acs.active_status_id
       LEFT OUTER JOIN stpetersburg.award_types at ON p.award_Types_id = at.award_types_id
       LEFT OUTER JOIN stpetersburg.divisions di ON p.divisions_id = di.divisions_id
       LEFT OUTER JOIN stpetersburg.departments d ON p.departments_id = d.departments_id
       INNER JOIN stpetersburg.program_proposal pp
                  LEFT OUTER JOIN stpetersburg.semesters sem ON pp.semesters_id = sem.semesters_id
                  LEFT OUTER JOIN stpetersburg.submissions sub ON pp.submissions_id = sub.submissions_id
                  LEFT OUTER JOIN stpetersburg.semesters sem2 ON pp.last_graduate = sem2.semesters_id
          ON p.programs_id = pp.programs_id
       LEFT OUTER JOIN stpetersburg.program_info pi
                       LEFT OUTER JOIN stpetersburg.fin_aid_status fas ON pi.fin_aid_status_id = fas.fin_aid_status_id
            ON p.programs_id = pi.programs_id
       LEFT OUTER JOIN (SELECT pl.programs_id, listAgg(loc.location_desc,', ') WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY loc.order_num) as prog_locs
                        FROM  stpetersburg.program_locations pl
                              INNER JOIN stpetersburg.locations loc ON pl.locations_id = loc.locations_id
                        GROUP BY pl.programs_id) ploc
            ON p.programs_id = ploc.programs_id
      LEFT OUTER JOIN (SELECT pdm.programs_id, LISTAGG(dm.delivery_method_desc,', ') WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY dm.order_num) as offerings
                      FROM   stpetersburg.program_delivery_methods pdm
                             INNER JOIN stpetersburg.delivery_methods dm ON pdm.delivery_methods_id = dm.delivery_methods_id
                      GROUP BY pdm.programs_id) pdm
          ON p.programs_id = pdm.programs_id
      LEFT OUTER JOIN (SELECT pos.programs_id, listagg(os.ordered_semester_title,', ') WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY os.ordered_semester_title) as offered
                      FROM   stpetersburg.program_ordered_semesters pos
                             INNER JOIN stpetersburg.ordered_semesters os ON pos.ordered_semesters_id = os.ordered_semesters_id
                      GROUP BY pos.programs_id) pos
          ON p.programs_id = pos.programs_id
      LEFT OUTER JOIN stpetersburg.fl_cip_codes fcc ON p.fl_cip_id = fcc.fl_cip_id
      LEFT OUTER JOIN stpetersburg.cip_codes cc ON p.cip_id = cc.cip_id
      LEFT OUTER JOIN stpetersburg.program_employment pe 
                      LEFT OUTER JOIN stpetersburg.fiscal_years fy ON pe.graduation_fiscal_year = fy.fiscal_years_id
                      LEFT OUTER JOIN stpetersburg.fiscal_years fy2 ON pe.job_fiscal_year = fy2.fiscal_years_id
          ON p.programs_id = pe.programs_id

subject: Cannot delete a pending proposal
description: A proposal for one of our courses (HSC 1149) was created, however, because of some complications with the original document we needed to create a second copy (under the administrator role).  I have tried to delete the incorrect copy (Author = Phil Nicotera, Status = Pending) however when I try to, it gives me a message (see attached) and does not remove the copy.  Please help.

comments.body: A proposal for one of our courses (HSC 1149) was created, however, because of some complications with the original document we needed to create a second copy (under the administrator role).  I have tried to delete the incorrect copy (Author = Phil Nicotera, Status = Pending) however when I try to, it gives me a message (see attached) and does not remove the copy.  Please help.

This course is a requisite and is being referenced in a program
You can click on the links showing the course and program and either remove this course as a requisite for HIM 2810 or change it to the previous version of the course
Also the link for the program MEDICAL CODER will be on the Course blocks page in one of the blocks under Courses link. Again this course needs to be removed from the block or it needs to be replaced with the previous version of the course.



The underlying issue is that you are deleting the course from the database and the database doesn't like any loose ends. If you just delete the course that uses this course as requisite will be looking at a non existent course

Thank You,

Travis Hillyard
Governet Customer Support Manager

Please feel free to contact me if you have any questions or concerns
208.534.7901  -   travish@governet.net

Given the message I thought it was something like that, however, what you referenced wasn't actually true because all I was doing was trying to delete a proposal, not the actual course.  The issue, which I realized based on what you said, thought, was that when the co-contributors selected the pre-reqs for HIM 2810 and the course for MEDICAL CODER, rather than selecting the Active version they selected the pending version.  Makes total sense. Well, to me anyways. ;)  Thanks for the help!

subject: Course Identifier field
description: The course identifier field text is reversed and incorrect.  Transferrable status should read:  Transferrable (P), Non-Transferrable (O).  Also, how is this field being populated?  Is it based on the ICS Type?  I don't see where the field is an editable one for the user?

comments.body: The course identifier field text is reversed and incorrect.  Transferrable status should read:  Transferrable (P), Non-Transferrable (O).  Also, how is this field being populated?  Is it based on the ICS Type?  I don't see where the field is an editable one for the user?

This field is "hard coded" meaning that the values are written statically on the cover page currently to change these we would have to change them in the code on the page.

Is it based on the ICS Type? 
No it is selected on the cover page there are no ties with any other field

I don't see where the field is an editable one for the user?
It appears on the general course cover page for user

Thank You,

Travis Hillyard
Governet Customer Support Manager

Please feel free to contact me if you have any questions or concerns
208.534.7901  -   travish@governet.net

OK - sorry for my confusion.  Course Identifier is a field with the radial buttons, i.e. Advanced and Professional (P), Post Secondary Vocational (O), etc. but it is then stored with the "Transferrable (O) or Non-Tranferrable (P)" text, correct? This stored text is what is incorrect.  

In looking at these fields we are finding alot of error or inconsistency with the user inputs.  If we sent you a table clearly defining the groupings, would it be possible for you to limit the selection options (cascading logic?).  i.e. If ICS type Advanced and Professional (1000) was selected, then under Course ICS Code only the selections starting with 1.1 would be available; selecting Postsecondary Vocational (2001) would only allow 1.2 ICS codes, etc.

Thanks!

Yes we could set it up that way if you provide us with the full explanation of what values for Course Identifier should influence/restrict other values we will start working on it

Thank You,

Travis Hillyard
Governet Customer Support Manager

Please feel free to contact me if you have any questions or concerns

subject: Workload Hours field saving data incorrectly
description: On the Course Credits/Hours page the field Workload Hours (ECH) is rounding up the data entry.  i.e. if 3.25 is entered and saved and finished it appears as 3.25.  When the page is unlocked, the field will reflect 3.0.  We have looked at the historical data and it appears as if it was transferred correctly so the issue is when the field is modified and saved.  Also the other fields on that page appear to be correct.  This is a HUGE issue.

comments.body: On the Course Credits/Hours page the field Workload Hours (ECH) is rounding up the data entry.  i.e. if 3.25 is entered and saved and finished it appears as 3.25.  When the page is unlocked, the field will reflect 3.0.  We have looked at the historical data and it appears as if it was transferred correctly so the issue is when the field is modified and saved.  Also the other fields on that page appear to be correct.  This is a HUGE issue.

Bill,

after spending the majority of last week on this issue I have found two solutions, each will not be optimal but will help you get the data you need.

Option 1:
I can receive the all of the data in the larger functions when I send it as a string instead of XML. All the xml appears to be correct and passes my xml validater however I cant use the XML programmatically in my code after it is sent this way. So the file would have to be saved and parsed on your end.


Option 2:
I was working with another school last week that also has very large web services. They however are capped to only get 100 records at a time. They use the CurricUNET ID that is supplied from the service to then start the next call from the last id that is sent. This ensures that their requests will not time out. This can be done to your services with only a slight alteration.
As I have thought more about this I seem to favor this approach because it is they way that I consume web services from other sources It requires a little work on my end but I have set up my functions to loop over these to get the newest versions.

What are your thoughts on this?






Thank You,

Travis Hillyard
Governet Customer Support Manager

Please feel free to contact me if you have any questions or concerns
208.534.7901  -   travish@governet.net

subject: Web Service Filters
description:
The way all of these functions have been constructed seems rather 
restrictive, especially on how we can pull filtered information. For 
example, let us suppose there are several degree programs offered by the 
“College of Business” (CoB). The program information contains the 
“College of Business” in the COLLEGE_SCHOOL_DEPARTMENT field – however, 
that field is not one I can use to filter requests. If I wanted to 
generate a list of those programs, I would have to get a list of all 
programs, then manually parse out the ones associated with the CoB. It 
would certainly be easier for me if I could define my parameters in an 
array to be passed in the SOAP call. I realize this would require 
completely rewriting what you’ve done – but I hope you will consider it for 
future modifications.

comments.body:
The way all of these functions have been constructed seems rather 
restrictive, especially on how we can pull filtered information. For 
example, let us suppose there are several degree programs offered by the 
“College of Business” (CoB). The program information contains the 
“College of Business” in the COLLEGE_SCHOOL_DEPARTMENT field – however, 
that field is not one I can use to filter requests. If I wanted to 
generate a list of those programs, I would have to get a list of all 
programs, then manually parse out the ones associated with the CoB. It 
would certainly be easier for me if I could define my parameters in an 
array to be passed in the SOAP call. I realize this would require 
completely rewriting what you’ve done – but I hope you will consider it for 
future modifications.

Thank You,

Governet Customer Support Manager

Please feel free to contact me if you have any questions or concerns
208.534.7901  -   travish@governet.net

Travis,

If you were to break down that snippet of my message into a request, it would be to give the ability to define the filter parameters as an array to be passed to the SOAP call. Using the department as a filter was just a single example.

It will take me some time to go through the functions listed in Jared’s document, but once I have, I’ll send you a list of the functions that would benefit from adding that filter.

Bill Bliss

Manager, Web Programming & Support

Web & Instructional Tech.

St. Petersburg College

Phone: 727.394.6226

All parameters will accept multiple values as in an array as long as they are separeated by commas. 
For example in the courses functions I was just working on I was testing on two CurricUNET_ids 3,7407
my test calls looked like this
get_course_data("","","","7047,3")

Thank You,

Travis Hillyard
Governet Customer Support Manager

Please feel free to contact me if you have any questions or concerns
208.534.7901  -   travish@governet.net

I don’t think I’m explaining myself well. Sorry.

Currently you have defined an exclusive set of parameters that we can use when calling these functions. I’m suggesting you make it so that your functions accept an associative array as the filter parameter. That way the calling program could pass any valid FIELD identifier and its associated value(s) to your function to be used as the filter.

So, rather than calling

get_course_data("","","","7047,3")

I would call for your test (in php)

$parameters = array(‘curricunet_id’ => ‘7047, 3’);

get_course_data($parameters);

or, using the same function, I could call something like:

$parameters = array(‘college_school_department’ => ‘College of Business’, ‘status’ => ‘Active’, ‘offered_semesters’ =>’Fall, Spring’);

get_course_data($parameters);

That way, the function is more flexible in that I can filter the data any way I need to, on the fly.

We do this in PHP, so I can only assume it’s doable in ColdFusion.

If I’m still not making any sense, please feel free to give me a call.

As I said in the initial message, I understand this would require a complete rewrite of the functions you’ve already defined – but I hope you will keep it in mind for future enhancements.

Bill Bliss

Manager, Web Programming & Support

Web & Instructional Tech.

St. Petersburg College

Phone: 727.394.6226

subject: Web Service New Course call
description:
A service that gives us access to just the basic course info would be 
helpful – something that returns the course title, number of credits, and 
description when given the course prefix and (optionally) course_number 
would be ideal.

Here are some examples of how we use the course data from our existing 
system:

· To generate a list of all courses where the prefix begins with a 
specific letter: http://www.spcollege.edu/courses/prefix/A

· To generate a list of all courses with a specific prefix: 
http://www.spcollege.edu/courses/prefix/ACG

· To list a specific course: 
http://www.spcollege.edu/popcourse/acg-2021

As I’ve mentioned, my goal is to use the data from CurricuNet to display on 
our public web site with similar functionality as our current system. 
Another example of how we would use this data, this one a program 
description output, is http://www.spcollege.edu/courses/program/BUS-BS. It 
was recently pointed out to me that your system can generate something 
similar. If you could provide us with a simple service whereby we can get 
that output – a basic program description similar to what we already have – 
that would be excellent. It looks like (once the program functions are 
working) we could build that kind of an output, but it would certainly be 
simpler if we could just request the description for “BUS-BS” and then just 
worry about handling the display. Since your product already generates 
that kind of output, I hope it won’t be too difficult to incorporate into 
these functions.

*From:* Bill Bliss [mailto:Bliss.Bill@spcollege.edu]
*Sent:* Friday, May 24, 2013 1:39 PM
*To:* Jared Pratt; Timothy McDowell
*Cc:* Margaret Bowman; Angela Ashe
*Subject:* RE: St. Petersburg CurricUNET move to Maintenance/Support



Hello Jared,



I’ve started looking at the web service functions provided.  I'm sorry to
say they are not working as expected.  I have not gone through all of them,
but the first 2 I tried were not working, so I stopped.



The ones I tried are the "GET_PROGRAM_COURSE_BLOCK" function, which seems
to be returning mostly the "GET_PROGRAM_DATA" information (but not all of
it), and the "GET_PROGRAM_DATA" function, which returns an error:* Function
("GET_PROGRAM_DATA") is not a valid method for this service*.  Looking at
the WSDL confirms that.



The way all of these functions have been constructed seems rather
restrictive, especially on how we can pull filtered information.  For
example, let us suppose there are several degree programs offered by the
“College of Business” (CoB).   The program information contains the
“College of Business” in the COLLEGE_SCHOOL_DEPARTMENT field – however,
that field is not one I can use to filter requests.  If I wanted to
generate a list of those programs, I would have to get a list of all
programs, then manually parse out the ones associated with the  CoB.  It
would certainly be easier for me if I could define my parameters in an
array to be passed in the SOAP call.  I realize this would require
completely rewriting what you’ve done – but I hope you will consider it for
future modifications.  Until then…



For the Course functions, please re-order the arguments to:



Prefix

Course_number

Status

Curricunet_id



and if the Status argument could default to whatever value represents
Active, that would be great. Then we only need to specify the prefix and
course number – which is our natural way of searching for courses.



Since “curricunet_id” is an identifier that is internal to your product,
I’m not sure how we would ever use it in our applications.  It would be
fine with me if you could just eliminate it – but moving it to the end
effectively makes it ignorable, so that would work, too.  This applies to
the program functions, too.



A service that gives us access to just the basic course info would be
helpful – something that returns the course title, number of credits, and
description when given the course prefix and (optionally) course_number
would be ideal.



Here are some examples of how we use the course data from our existing
system:

·         To generate a list of all courses where the prefix begins with a
specific letter: http://www.spcollege.edu/courses/prefix/A

·         To generate a list of all courses with a specific prefix:
http://www.spcollege.edu/courses/prefix/ACG

·         To list a specific course:
http://www.spcollege.edu/popcourse/acg-2021





As I’ve mentioned, my goal is to use the data from CurricuNet to display on
our public web site with similar functionality as our current system.
Another example of how we would use this data, this one a program
description output, is http://www.spcollege.edu/courses/program/BUS-BS.  It
was recently pointed out to me that your system can generate something
similar.  If you could provide us with a simple service whereby we can get
that output – a basic program description similar to what we already have –
that would be excellent.  It looks like (once the program functions are
working) we could build that kind of an output, but it would certainly be
simpler if we could just request the description for “BUS-BS” and then just
worry about handling the display.  Since your product already generates
that kind of output, I hope it won’t be too difficult to incorporate into
these functions.



Thanks for your time and effort.



Bill Bliss

Manager, Web Programming & Support

Web & Instructional Tech.

St. Petersburg College

Phone: 727.394.6226

[mailto:tmcdowell@governet.net<tmcdowell@governet.net>]

Ashe

to Jared.



Thank you,

Timothy McDowell

*From:* Bill Bliss [mailto:Bliss.Bill@spcollege.edu]
*Sent:* Wednesday, April 03, 2013 5:38 AM
*To:* Jared Pratt; Leigh Goldberg Hopf; Margaret Bowman; Angela Ashe
*Cc:* Travis Hillyard; Todd Andersen; Timothy McDowell
*Subject:* Re: St. Petersburg CurricUNET move to Maintenance/Support



Jared,



In early January, I was exchanging emails with you regarding web services
(see below).  Since 1/16, I have not received any further information – let
alone complete documentation on accessing the CurricuNet web services.  I
believe Leigh also followed up on this in March.



Is that phase still in development, or should I now work with your support
team to implement access to the data?



Thanks,



Bill Bliss

Manager, Web Programming & Support

Web & Instructional Tech.

St. Petersburg College

Phone: 727.394.6226







*From:* Bill Bliss
*Sent:* Wednesday, January 16, 2013 10:16 AM
*To:* 'Jared Pratt'
*Cc:* Leigh Goldberg Hopf
*Subject:* RE: Follow-up to conference call about web services



Hello Jared,



This is much more helpful than the WSDL file – thanks.  The concise
descriptions of the parameters and their expected values/formats is just
what we need.



When you send the next iteration, rather than inserting a screen-shot of
the XML returned (which is very difficult for me to read), a description of
the recordset would be more helpful.  To use the “getCourses” example, it
would be helpful to have something along the lines of:



The output for getCourses contains:

Count_Retake_Credit (“Yes” or “No”)

Count_Retake_Times (integer)

Courses_ID (Curricunet internal ID: integer)

Course_Number (Institutional course number, without prefix, e.g. 1101 or
1101H)

Course_Status (“Active” “Pending” or “Historical”)

And so forth…



Finally – a brief description of non-obvious expected behavior for each
method would be helpful.  For example, in the getCourses method, all the
parameters are marked as optional.  The obvious expected behavior is that,
if I enter only a single “Courses_ID” value, I will get that one record.
Likewise if I enter only a “STATUS” value, I will get all records with that
status.  But what happens when I set no parameters?  That is what I mean by
non-obvious.  Do I get every single record, or do I get none?



This part is, I admit, a little picky – but it’s one of my personal
peeves:  It would be nice if the method and parameter names were
consistent.  For example, make the naming convention for methods either
camel case (“getCourses”) or underscored (“get_requisites”) – not both.
Likewise, decide whether parameters should be all caps (as with getCourses)
or all lower-case (as with all the others).



I look forward to seeing the next round of documentation.



Bill Bliss

Manager, Web Programming & Support

Web & Instructional Tech.

St. Petersburg College

Phone: 727.394.6226



*From:* Jared Pratt [mailto:jpratt@governet.net <jpratt@governet.net>]

*From:* Bill Bliss [mailto:Bliss.Bill@spcollege.edu]
*Sent:* Thursday, January 10, 2013 6:14 AM
*To:* Jared Pratt (jpratt@governet.net)
*Cc:* Leigh Goldberg Hopf
*Subject:* RE: Follow-up to conference call about web services



Hello Jared,



Thank you for sending the WSDL file for your web services.  Unfortunately,
you didn’t include any documentation detailing the functionality and
utilization.  While it is helpful to know, for example, that the parameters
COURSES_ID, STATUS, DISCIPLINE, and COURSE_NUMBER are needed to access the
operation “getCourses” – we have no idea what those parameters should
contain (other than a string), or in what format.  To me, a valid
COURSES_ID is ‘ENC-1101’ – but I don’t have any way of knowing from the
WSDL file what your service is expecting.



Likewise, we have no idea what to expect as returns for the various
operations.  We could certainly do a test on each one, but it would be much
less time consuming if you could provide that information.



Also, I’m sure there is some kind of authentication required to access this
data, but I don’t see that in your email, and I’m not seeing any indication
of it in the WSDL file.



Thank you,



Bill Bliss

Manager, Web Programming & Support

Web & Instructional Tech.

St. Petersburg College

Give me a call, or email, and we will find a time to chat.

Leigh

*From:* Bill Bliss [mailto:Bliss.Bill@spcollege.edu]
*Sent:* Friday, May 24, 2013 1:39 PM
*To:* Jared Pratt; Timothy McDowell
*Cc:* Margaret Bowman; Angela Ashe
*Subject:* RE: St. Petersburg CurricUNET move to Maintenance/Support



Hello Jared,



I’ve started looking at the web service functions provided.  I'm sorry to
say they are not working as expected.  I have not gone through all of them,
but the first 2 I tried were not working, so I stopped.



The ones I tried are the "GET_PROGRAM_COURSE_BLOCK" function, which seems
to be returning mostly the "GET_PROGRAM_DATA" information (but not all of
it), and the "GET_PROGRAM_DATA" function, which returns an error:* Function
("GET_PROGRAM_DATA") is not a valid method for this service*.  Looking at
the WSDL confirms that.



The way all of these functions have been constructed seems rather
restrictive, especially on how we can pull filtered information.  For
example, let us suppose there are several degree programs offered by the
“College of Business” (CoB).   The program information contains the
“College of Business” in the COLLEGE_SCHOOL_DEPARTMENT field – however,
that field is not one I can use to filter requests.  If I wanted to
generate a list of those programs, I would have to get a list of all
programs, then manually parse out the ones associated with the  CoB.  It
would certainly be easier for me if I could define my parameters in an
array to be passed in the SOAP call.  I realize this would require
completely rewriting what you’ve done – but I hope you will consider it for
future modifications.  Until then…



For the Course functions, please re-order the arguments to:



Prefix

Course_number

Status

Curricunet_id



and if the Status argument could default to whatever value represents
Active, that would be great. Then we only need to specify the prefix and
course number – which is our natural way of searching for courses.



Since “curricunet_id” is an identifier that is internal to your product,
I’m not sure how we would ever use it in our applications.  It would be
fine with me if you could just eliminate it – but moving it to the end
effectively makes it ignorable, so that would work, too.  This applies to
the program functions, too.



A service that gives us access to just the basic course info would be
helpful – something that returns the course title, number of credits, and
description when given the course prefix and (optionally) course_number
would be ideal.



Here are some examples of how we use the course data from our existing
system:

·         To generate a list of all courses where the prefix begins with a
specific letter: http://www.spcollege.edu/courses/prefix/A

·         To generate a list of all courses with a specific prefix:
http://www.spcollege.edu/courses/prefix/ACG

·         To list a specific course:
http://www.spcollege.edu/popcourse/acg-2021





As I’ve mentioned, my goal is to use the data from CurricuNet to display on
our public web site with similar functionality as our current system.
Another example of how we would use this data, this one a program
description output, is http://www.spcollege.edu/courses/program/BUS-BS.  It
was recently pointed out to me that your system can generate something
similar.  If you could provide us with a simple service whereby we can get
that output – a basic program description similar to what we already have –
that would be excellent.  It looks like (once the program functions are
working) we could build that kind of an output, but it would certainly be
simpler if we could just request the description for “BUS-BS” and then just
worry about handling the display.  Since your product already generates
that kind of output, I hope it won’t be too difficult to incorporate into
these functions.



Thanks for your time and effort.



Bill Bliss

Manager, Web Programming & Support

Web & Instructional Tech.

St. Petersburg College

Phone: 727.394.6226

*Sent:* Wednesday, January 16, 2013 10:16 AM
*To:* 'Jared Pratt'
*Cc:* Leigh Goldberg Hopf
*Subject:* RE: Follow-up to conference call about web services



Hello Jared,



This is much more helpful than the WSDL file – thanks.  The concise
descriptions of the parameters and their expected values/formats is just
what we need.



When you send the next iteration, rather than inserting a screen-shot of
the XML returned (which is very difficult for me to read), a description of
the recordset would be more helpful.  To use the “getCourses” example, it
would be helpful to have something along the lines of:



The output for getCourses contains:

Count_Retake_Credit (“Yes” or “No”)

Count_Retake_Times (integer)

Courses_ID (Curricunet internal ID: integer)

Course_Number (Institutional course number, without prefix, e.g. 1101 or
1101H)

Course_Status (“Active” “Pending” or “Historical”)

And so forth…



Finally – a brief description of non-obvious expected behavior for each
method would be helpful.  For example, in the getCourses method, all the
parameters are marked as optional.  The obvious expected behavior is that,
if I enter only a single “Courses_ID” value, I will get that one record.
Likewise if I enter only a “STATUS” value, I will get all records with that
status.  But what happens when I set no parameters?  That is what I mean by
non-obvious.  Do I get every single record, or do I get none?



This part is, I admit, a little picky – but it’s one of my personal
peeves:  It would be nice if the method and parameter names were
consistent.  For example, make the naming convention for methods either
camel case (“getCourses”) or underscored (“get_requisites”) – not both.
Likewise, decide whether parameters should be all caps (as with getCourses)
or all lower-case (as with all the others).



I look forward to seeing the next round of documentation.



Bill Bliss

Web & Instructional Tech.

subject: Web Service Issues
description:
See the following email about St. Petersburg webservices:

I oversee the web development team here at SPC, and have been the one reporting the issues to Margaret and her team, as well as occasionally working with first Jared, then Travis and others when I’ve submitted tickets.  I’m happy to discuss the details of the issues we’ve had.
 
We are using the web service interfaces to pull course and program information for display in several of our public-facing web-based systems.  When we first started working with your folks several years ago, I sent links to our existing systems to show what we were doing, what we were trying to replicate, and therefore what was needed from the web service interfaces.  I never got any response to those emails, and based on what we eventually received, I would have to guess those links were never reviewed.  It took many, many emails back and forth between myself and Travis to get the interfaces to a useable state for us.
 
From the beginning trying to work with the web services has been challenging.  The functions were rife with inconsistencies in output, restrictive in required inputs, and (as already mentioned) very slow.  Over a year ago I sent an email to Travis (which he converted to a ticket) with several suggestions for how to better structure the web services.  You closed it last week.  After the initial conversation, I never received any comment nor was there any action on it that I was made aware of. So, we’ve worked with what you gave us, and have tried to mitigate the issues as best we can. 
 
The ongoing problem with the web services is that they have been, from our perspective, unreliable, unstable, and often slow to the point of being unusable. If these are our experiences in a low-use testing environment, I am very concerned about the public reaction when we start heavily utilizing the services in a production environment. 
 
The most recent issues with stability and reliability are detailed in the message below – but these are by no means the only examples.  Because we try to insure that the problem is not something on our end, we don’t necessarily open a trouble ticket immediately.  Often, as with the disappearing groups of programs described below, by the time we have insured the problem is not on our end, the behavior has “fixed itself.”
 
I would be happy to discuss further.

If these are our experiences in a low-use testing environment, I am very concerned about the public reaction when we start heavily utilizing the services in a production environment. 
 
The most recent issues with stability and reliability are detailed in the message below – but these are by no means the only examples.  Because we try to insure that the problem is not something on our end, we don’t necessarily open a trouble ticket immediately.  Often, as with the disappearing groups of programs described below, by the time we have insured the problem is not on our end, the behavior has “fixed itself.”
 
I would be happy to discuss further.

Anna Nelson

I know we do not have the links and things he mentions from years ago. We need to make their web services as stable and fast as possible so we can make them happy. We need to have an update for him by the end of the day today. This ticket has to be pushed by the end of the day.

Hello Bill,

My name is Stephen Tigner, and I'm working on improving the speed and stability of your web services. As such, I have a question for you.
Within the RECORDSET tags (or the equivalent in functions that don't use the RECORDS and RECORDSET tags), do the child tags need to stay in the same order that they are now?

Thanks!

-- Stephen Tigner
Governet Support Developer

Re-writing the web services to use the XML generation functions of the Oracle database itself, running *much* faster now.
This does require enabling the CLOB ("Enable long text retrieval (CLOB)") option in the Advanced settings for the datasource on the CF server, just like it did for the UAEU re-write.

Stephen,

Thanks for looking into this for us.

I’ve copied Michael Crumley on this response – he’s the developer here with the most familiarity with how we are using your services. You can direct technical questions to him.

The short answer is that we do depend on ordering in some of the calls (e.g. get_program_report_data), but not all of them.

Bill Bliss

Manager, Web Programming & Support

Instructional Design and Development

St. Petersburg College

Phone: 727.394.6226

“We’ve bought into the idea that education is about training and ‘success’, defined monetarily, rather than learning to think critically and to challenge. We should not forget that the true purpose of education is to make minds, not careers. “ -- Chris Hedges
There's a web services ticket for St. Petersburg that was recently closed (#19095), he may be referring to that one in his comment.
Correction/clarification: He may be referring to ticket #19095 in the email from the first comment on this ticket.

Bill,

Thanks for your response. I'm afraid it appears that Zendesk ate Michael's email address, so could you put it in the body of a reply to this message?

Thanks again,

-- Stephen Tigner
Governet Support Developer

description: When sending a proposal back for revisions it is unable to be edited.  I can make it work by deleting the proposal, however, I do not believe that is aligned with the workflow process. Please see detailed documentation.

comments.body: When sending a proposal back for revisions it is unable to be edited.  I can make it work by deleting the proposal, however, I do not believe that is aligned with the workflow process. Please see detailed documentation.

The reason they can not edit the course has a status of Launched. Only courses with a status of Pending can be edited by non CurricUNET Administrators.
To change this you can do so through the Admin>Look-Up Data>Change Status>Change Course Status page.

I have repaired the Technical Review step to change the status of the course to Pending when Changes Needed action is taken on the Technical Review step. So this issue will not occur from now on.

subject: FW: new report
description:
Here’s an email from St. Petersburg about a report they want.



Have fun.







*From:* Angela Ashe [mailto:ashe.angela@spcollege.edu]
*Sent:* Wednesday, May 01, 2013 8:52 AM
*To:* Timothy McDowell (tmcdowell@governet.net) (tmcdowell@governet.net)
*Cc:* Margaret Bowman
*Subject:* new report



Hi Tim,

As we continue to move through our C&I process and work with committee
members, we have discovered that they are not able to easily see other
reviewers’ comments and this is a critical piece in their own review.
 While comments can be seen under “All Proposals”, moving between screens
is awkward.  Also, with 15+ reviewers commenting it will become a
cumbersome way to view the information.



It seems to us that the ideal solution for this would be to create another
report option that is visible to all (submitters, co-contributors, C&I
members, chair, etc.) as follows:



               Track>My Approvals (and All Proposals)>Click on Reports icon
to open report options>

                              Add a standard report labeled "Reviewer
Comments/Actions" that would capture:

                                             Reviewer's role (e.g., C&I
Committee member, Curriculum Services)

                                             Reviewer's name (2nd sort,
last name)

                                             Reviewers' recommended action
(1st sort, Z-A so the “Not Approved” appears first)

                                             Reviewer's comments

                                             Date of review



Since we have already had the first of our two meetings we feel that this
is a very high priority.  Please let me know how/what we need to do to make
this a viable solution as soon as possible.  I am in the office all day if
you need to discuss further.  Thanks!

[image: cid:image001.png@01CE4646.4FA1EC70]





Angela Ashe

Updated:
webservices/stpetersburg/spc_webservices.cfc

The degree_type argument to get_program_basics was supposed to support a comma-separated list of award type titles, but I forgot the "list" attribute on the cfqueryparam tag for that argument. Added that, and now the function properly accepts a comma-separated list of award type titles.

The other program functions had the same issue, so I fixed that there as well.

Testing:
Make sure multiple award type titles can be passed in as the degree_type parameter to the get_program_basics function.

For example:
http://test.curricunet.com/scripts/webservices/stpetersburg/spc_webservices.cfc?method=get_program_basics&degree_type=Associate%20in%20Science,Bachelor%20of%20Science

That should return both Associate in Science and Bachelor of Science programs. Previously it was returning nothing, because it was treating the entire comma-separated list as a single award type title.

Example URLs for the other functions that were fixed (get_program_data and get_program_report_data):

http://test.curricunet.com/scripts/webservices/stpetersburg/spc_webservices.cfc?method=get_program_data&degree_type=Associate%20in%20Science,Bachelor%20of%20Science

http://test.curricunet.com/scripts/webservices/stpetersburg/spc_webservices.cfc?method=get_program_report_data&degree_type=Associate%20in%20Science,Bachelor%20of%20Science
***** REMINDER: This is on /scripts/, NOT on /stpetersburg/. When this is pushed live, push /scripts/ *****

Bill,

The function has been fixed to allow a comma-separated list of award/degree type titles. The same functionality should have been there for the other two program functions (get_program_data) and (get_program_report_data), so that has been fixed there as well.

This change has been sent off to our internal testing.

-- Stephen

Thanks Stephen! Please keep us updated as testing progresses.

Bill

subject: Originator Change
description: We are having a problem with a course that has Jamie Wise (? Governet Employee?) as an originator.  Full documentation is attached with explanation (email).  Thank you!

comments.body: We are having a problem with a course that has Jamie Wise (? Governet Employee?) as an originator.  Full documentation is attached with explanation (email).  Thank you!

•	I assigned an academic org to it which now enables Greg to at least pull up the course (Search/Course)
Pat has origination rights to the RMI course prefix which means he can create new courses and create modifications of existing versions. It does not give him right to edit any RMI course.

•	He (Greg) cannot do anything to the course, however, and the only reason I can figure out is because Jaime Wise’s name is attached to it, but I don’t see why that would matter.
Jamie (our Data Entry lead) is the originator of the course, as she entered it into the system. Only originators and co-contributors can edit a course

•	The course appears to be in Pending status as a new course, however, it does not pull up when I try to access it using the Build/Courses function as Greg.
The Build>Courses page is labeled "My Courses" as it only shows courses that the user is the originator for or co-contributor for, essentially the courses that they have the ability to edit


•	I can pre-launch the course and send it through the approval workflows when I am logged in as myself, but we need this to come from Greg, not me. 
Margaret is an administrator, which means she can do almost anything she needs to in the system. He can not launch because he is not the originator of the course


1.	Why is Greg not able to edit the course? 
He is not an originator or co-contributor

It is assigned to his academic org, and he has course ownership for it (I entered that in the system, and it saved).
Origination rights give them the ability to create new and create modifications in the course prefix not carte blance to edit courses they are not originators for. 
"and he has course ownership for it" I am not sure what that means, the only ownership in CurricUNET is through being the originator for the course

2.	Why is Greg not able to view the course when he looks for it under Build/Courses?  
The Build>Courses page is labeled "My Courses" as it only shows courses that the user is the originator for or co-contributor for, essentially the courses that they have the ability to edit

I think this has something to do with the “Author”.  I notice that the only courses that are listed under “My Courses” have his name listed as the author.  This is why I’m thinking it has something to do with Jamie Wise’s name.  Perhaps she is being considered the “Originator”?  
Yes that is exactly what is happening

When I log into the system as her and go to Track/My Proposals, two courses (COP 2001, C++ Programming with DirectX; and RMI 2117) and one program (Microsoft Certified Server Administrator) are listed.  Could this be the problem?
Not sure what the problem is My Proposals show what courses/programs have been launched. This list does not include courses/programs that have not been launched or have completed the approval process.

3.	Why am I not able to delete the course from the system?  Possibly related to #2?
Actually as administrator you can delete courses regardless or who is the originator.
The issue here is that this course (RMI 2117) is included as part of a program namely Insurance Services. When trying to delete a course and this message comes up it will link you to the program so if needed you can adjust this link so the course is removed from the program and/or a new course or version of the course is added to the program to replace it. This is done through the Course Blocks page. This particular course is in the block names Year 2 Term I.

Thank You,

Travis Hillyard
Governet Customer Support Manager

Please feel free to contact me if you have any questions or concerns
208.534.7901  -   travish@governet.net

Hi Travis,
Thanks for your response.  Our issue was that there was a CurricUNET employee even on the courses to begin with since that doesn't serve us well even if the "rules" of the system are being followed.  I figured out this morning that we can change the Originator and that was ultimately what needed to be done.  Thank you for the explanation on the RMI 2117 course - that was very helpful.

While I have tried to be discriminating, I realize that some of our tickets are more about how the system works and how to do something than fixing functionality.  If ths is the case I will try to utilize Tim a bit more. Thanks for your help.

Angela

subject: Course Credit changes are not appearing correctly
description: When a modification is made to the course credit hours it is not reflected correctly in the course outline or the comparison report.  See attached documentation.

comments.body: When a modification is made to the course credit hours it is not reflected correctly in the course outline or the comparison report.  See attached documentation.

The issue is that the course still has a value for the max course credits even though it is marked as No for Variable Hour Course.
see image
We can fix the report to not show the max value if ithe course is not variable however in the meantime you can fix these course by course by doing the following:
Click yes on the Variable Hour Course
Save with values min 0 and max 0
click no on variable hour course
then add back the appropriate value for min (3 in this case)
then saving


Thank You,

Travis Hillyard
Governet Customer Support Manager

subject: Changing College/School/Dept on program
description: The FUNSE-AS program is currently assigned to the College of Health Sciences and should be under the Health Science Department.  As an administrator, can I make this change without creating a whole new proposal?  I have tried to make the correction and select finish but an error message comes up noting that all of the fields have not been completed.

comments.body: The FUNSE-AS program is currently assigned to the College of Health Sciences and should be under the Health Science Department.  As an administrator, can I make this change without creating a whole new proposal?  I have tried to make the correction and select finish but an error message comes up noting that all of the fields have not been completed.

The errors you are seeing are for the other fields if you add values to these fields the page will save correctly.

Thank You,

Travis Hillyard
Governet Customer Support Manager

Please feel free to contact me if you have any questions or concerns
208.534.7901  -   travish@governet.net
Thank you.

Hi Travis,  I don't think I was totally clear on my question here.  I understand that there are other fields required for a program change, however, the update that I need to make is because the initial entry of the program into CurricUNET never assigned the College/School/Department.  There are 9 programs like this and they are listed as "unknown".  

If I select edit and go to the Program Cover to make that change then I am also required to respond to  the four required fields (fig. 1, term that changes will take effect, feasibility study date, etc.).  It is not my intention to create a submission.  Can these changes be made from under an administrative function without creating a submission? I noticed that the program cover for FUNSE-AS has the correct College/School/Department assignment but those other three required fields do not appear to have been completed. fig. 2 (last saved by you).

There isn't an admin function that bypasses these business rules. nor is there another page or area of the site were you can edit these. We would have to add functionality to accomplish either of those.

However putting data into those fields will allow you to save it. For example for Justification for you can put in a period. As these will not be a submission as they are already completed and wont go through the approval process. 

Thank You,

Travis Hillyard
Governet Customer Support Manager

Please feel free to contact me if you have any questions or concerns
208.534.7901  -   travish@governet.net

Sounds like entering some simple data is a work-around that will accomplish what I need to do.  Thank you.  Just an FYI . . . we are knee deep in our process of submissions so you may be hearing from me frequently over the next couple of days.  So far it seems to be going pretty smoothly so hopefully that will continue.

Hi Travis,
I have what is a very elementary question but wanted to verify . . . when is a course submission is considered a "Proposal"? Once it has been launched into the workflow?  In regards to Dr. Ludwig's courses, she has two courses (RET 4285 and PRO 4850) which have been launched but are not coming up under "My Proposals". However, they are under "All Proposals".  We also noticed that when "My Proposals" is selected the banner at the top still says "All Proposals"- this is confusing especially with the breadcrumbs not indicating the page level.  Please feel free to contact me by phone (727) 341-3041 if necessary.

Yes a course is considered a proposal when it is launched into an approval process.

I did fix the My Proposals page yesterday but I failed up update your live site with this change it shows correctly now. Also I changed the table head to read "My Proposals" instead of "All Proposals"
